title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: R package automated classification soil profiles WRB 2022 (4th ed.) SiBCS 5th ed., multimodal extraction, spatial priors (SoilGrids) attribute prediction via spectroscopy (OSSL). Migrated pedokey_architecture_1.md (original draft) 2026-04-25 package-name decision. living architecture document — update decisions change throughout implementation.

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: Name: soilKey. Decided 2026-04-25, evaluating alternatives (pedokey, pedorules, autopedo). License: MIT (see LICENSE / LICENSE.md). Choice reconciled DESCRIPTION 2026-04-30. MIT (rather GPL-3, considered early drafts) maximises interoperability academic government pipelines — without copyleft virality limit embedded use internal tools Embrapa, FAO IUSS-WRB. Target audience: pedologists, agronomists, environmental consultants, research institutions. ML expertise required.

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: Strict separation concerns. Extraction (VLM), attribute inference (spectra, prior), classification (key) independent stages. key never delegated LLM. LLM serves transform unstructured data schema-validated structured data. Full traceability. assigned class carries complete trace: diagnostics tested, attributes used, origin attribute (measured / predicted / extracted / assumed), chain decisions led result. Declarative possible. key rules live versioned YAML, scattered code. enables auditing pedologists program, turns updates (e.g., future WRB 5th ed.) matter editing YAML + adjusting diagnostic functions. Tests scientific documentation. diagnostic fixtures based published canonical profiles (didactic examples WRB, RADAMBRASIL, Soil Atlas Europe, WoSIS). Interoperability existing ecosystem. PedonRecord converts /aqp::SoilProfileCollection; spectra follow conventions prospectr/resemble/ossl; spatial follows terra/sf. Explicit grades evidence. final result includes evidence_grade (/B/C/D) based provenance critical attributes. important opaque numeric “confidence”.

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: central structure carries everything profile — site, horizons, spectra, images, documents, provenance attribute.
code: PedonRecord <- R6::R6Class("PedonRecord",   public = list(      # ---- data ----     site = NULL,        # list:                         #   lat, lon, crs (default 4326), date, country,                         #   elevation_m, slope_pct, aspect_deg,                         #   landform, parent_material,                         #   land_use, vegetation, drainage_class      horizons = NULL,    # data.table with columns (fixed schema):                         #   top_cm, bottom_cm, designation,                         #   boundary_distinctness, boundary_topography,                         #   munsell_hue_moist, munsell_value_moist, munsell_chroma_moist,                         #   munsell_hue_dry, munsell_value_dry, munsell_chroma_dry,                         #   structure_grade, structure_size, structure_type,                         #   consistence_moist, consistence_wet, clay_films,                         #   coarse_fragments_pct,                         #   clay_pct, silt_pct, sand_pct,                         #   ph_h2o, ph_kcl, ph_cacl2,                         #   oc_pct, n_total_pct,                         #   cec_cmol, ecec_cmol, bs_pct, al_sat_pct,                         #   ca_cmol, mg_cmol, k_cmol, na_cmol, al_cmol,                         #   caco3_pct, caso4_pct,                         #   fe_dcb_pct, fe_ox_pct, al_ox_pct, si_ox_pct,                         #   bulk_density_g_cm3, water_content_33kpa, water_content_1500kpa      spectra = NULL,     # list:                         #   vnir = matrix (rows = horizons, cols = 350:2500 nm),                         #   mir  = matrix (rows = horizons, cols = 4000:600 cm^-1),                         #   metadata = list(instrument, date, preprocess_applied)      images = NULL,      # list of:                         #   list(type="profile_wall", path=..., reference_card=TRUE, annotations=...)      documents = NULL,   # list of:                         #   list(type="field_sheet"|"lab_report"|"survey_report", path=..., text=...)      # ---- provenance ----     provenance = NULL,  # data.table: horizon_idx, attribute, source, confidence, notes                         # source ∈ {measured, extracted_vlm, predicted_spectra,                         #           inferred_prior, user_assumed}      # ---- methods ----     initialize = function(site = NULL, horizons = NULL,                           spectra = NULL, images = NULL, documents = NULL) { ... },     validate   = function() { ... },   # check sanity: top<bottom, %sand+silt+clay≈100,                                         # pH in plausible range, CEC >= sum of bases, etc.     to_aqp     = function() { ... },   # coerce to SoilProfileCollection     from_aqp   = function(spc) { ... },     add_measurement = function(horizon_idx, attribute, value, source, confidence = 1.0) { ... },     summary    = function() { ... },     print      = function() { ... }   ) )

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: Returned every diagnostic function. Never just TRUE/FALSE — always carries evidence.
code: DiagnosticResult <- R6::R6Class("DiagnosticResult",   public = list(     name      = NULL,   # "argic"     passed    = NULL,   # TRUE/FALSE/NA (NA = missing attributes prevented test)     layers    = NULL,   # which horizons (indices) satisfy     evidence  = NULL,   # list with sub-tests and their results     missing   = NULL,   # which attributes would be needed and are absent     reference = NULL,   # literature citation     notes     = NULL   ) )

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: diagnostic function takes PedonRecord returns DiagnosticResult. Example: Sub-tests (test_clay_increase_argic etc.) live R/utils-diagnostic-tests.R also exported — pedologists can call isolation auditing.
code: #' Argic horizon (WRB 2022) #' #' Tests whether any horizon or combination meets the argic horizon criteria #' per Chapter 3 of WRB 2022 (IUSS Working Group WRB, 2022). #' #' @param pedon A PedonRecord. #' @param min_thickness Minimum thickness in cm (default 7.5). #' @return A DiagnosticResult. #' @references IUSS Working Group WRB (2022), Chapter 3, Argic horizon. #' @export argic <- function(pedon, min_thickness = 7.5) {    h <- pedon$horizons   tests <- list()    # Test 1: qualified textural increment (one of three conditions)   tests$clay_increase <- test_clay_increase_argic(h)    # Test 2: minimum thickness of the candidate argic horizon   tests$thickness <- test_minimum_thickness(h, min_thickness,                                              candidate_layers = tests$clay_increase$layers)    # Test 3: minimum texture (cannot be sand)   tests$texture <- test_texture_argic(h, candidate_layers = tests$clay_increase$layers)    # Test 4: exclusions (albeluvic glossic -> Retisol instead of Luvisol/Acrisol)   tests$not_albeluvic <- test_not_albeluvic(h)    # Required attributes that are missing   missing <- attributes_missing_for_tests(tests, h)    all_ok <- all(vapply(tests, function(t) isTRUE(t$passed), logical(1)))    DiagnosticResult$new(     name      = "argic",     passed    = if (length(missing) > 0) NA else all_ok,     layers    = if (isTRUE(all_ok)) tests$clay_increase$layers else integer(0),     evidence  = tests,     missing   = missing,     reference = "IUSS Working Group WRB (2022), Chapter 3, Argic horizon"   ) }

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: testing order 32 RSGs explicit inst/rules/wrb2022/key.yaml:
code: version: "WRB 2022 (4th edition)" source: "IUSS Working Group WRB (2022). World Reference Base for Soil Resources.         International soil classification system for naming soils and creating         legends for soil maps. 4th edition. IUSS, Vienna." checksum: "sha256:..."  # verifiable against the edition  # Canonical key order; excludes_previous is implicit (if not satisfied here, # falls through to the next RSG).  rsgs:   - code: HS     name: Histosols     tests:       any_of:         - organic_material: {thickness_gte: 10, from_surface: true}         - organic_material: {thickness_gte: 40, within_top_cm: 80, cumulative: true}    - code: AT     name: Anthrosols     tests:       any_of:         - hortic_horizon: {}         - irragric_horizon: {}         - plaggic_horizon: {}         - pretic_horizon: {}         - terric_horizon: {}    - code: TC     name: Technosols     tests:       any_of:         - artefacts: {volume_pct_gte: 20, within_top_cm: 100}         - geomembrane: {within_top_cm: 100}         - technic_hard_material: {depth_lte: 5, coverage_pct_gte: 95}    - code: CR     name: Cryosols     tests:       all_of:         - cryic_conditions: {within_top_cm: 100}    - code: LP     name: Leptosols     tests:       any_of:         - continuous_rock: {within_cm: 25}         - coarse_fragments: {pct_gte: 90, within_top_cm: 75}    # ... (continues through the 32 RSGs in order: SN, VR, SC, GL, AN, PZ, PT, NT, FR,   #      PL, ST, CH, KS, PH, UM, DU, GY, CL, RT, AC, LX, AL, LV, CM, AR, FL, RG)    - code: RG     name: Regosols     tests:       default: true   # catch-all — all mineral soils that did not key out earlier

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: R/rule-engine.R reads YAML, resolves references diagnostic functions, executes:
code: run_wrb_key <- function(pedon, rules = NULL) {   rules <- rules %||% load_rules("wrb2022")   trace <- list()    for (rsg in rules$rsgs) {     result <- evaluate_rsg_tests(pedon, rsg$tests)     trace[[rsg$code]] <- list(       code = rsg$code, name = rsg$name,       passed = result$passed,       evidence = result$evidence,       missing = result$missing     )     if (isTRUE(result$passed)) {       return(list(assigned = rsg, trace = trace))     }     # NA (insufficient attributes) — annotate but continue; the final result     # flags the ambiguity.   }   # default: Regosols (catch-all)   list(assigned = rules$rsgs[[length(rules$rsgs)]], trace = trace) }  evaluate_rsg_tests <- function(pedon, tests) {   if (!is.null(tests$all_of)) {     results <- lapply(tests$all_of, run_single_test, pedon = pedon)     passed <- all(vapply(results, `[[`, logical(1), "passed"))   } else if (!is.null(tests$any_of)) {     results <- lapply(tests$any_of, run_single_test, pedon = pedon)     passed <- any(vapply(results, `[[`, logical(1), "passed"))   } else if (isTRUE(tests$default)) {     return(list(passed = TRUE, evidence = list(), missing = character(0)))   }   missing <- unique(unlist(lapply(results, `[[`, "missing")))   list(passed = passed, evidence = results, missing = missing) }

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: RSG assigned, qualifiers-wrb2022.R runs ~202 qualifiers, filtering available RSG (Chapter 4 WRB 2022). qualifier also pure function pattern. Principal qualifiers ordered specified; supplementary qualifiers listed alphabetically. Final name output: "Rhodic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric)".

what: 6.4.1 Canonical inventory for v0.9 (WRB 2022 Ch 5, pp 127-156)
title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: full code table Ch 6 (pp 152-153) contains 4 groups: Implementation strategy v0.9: qualifiers reduce 4 structural patterns: “X horizon depth ≤ Y cm” (>= 60% qualifiers): Calcic, Petrocalcic, Argic (already indirect via RSG), Spodic, Histic, Plinthic, Andic, etc. Implementation: thin wrapper qualifier_has_horizon(horizon_diagnostic_fn, max_top_cm). “layer Z properties depth ≤ Y cm” (~25%): Stagnic, Gleyic, Vitric, Vertic, Sodic, Reducing, etc. Analogous wrapper property_diagnostic_fn. “Material gating” (~10%): Calcaric, Dolomitic, Tephric, Fluvic, Sulfidic etc. Wrapper material_diagnostic_fn. “Specific chemical/physical composition” (~5%): Dystric, Eutric, Hyperdystric, Hypereutric (BS thresholds), Magnesic, Hyperalic, Geric, Pellic, Rubic, Xanthic (Munsell), Vermic (worm features), Skeletic (coarse fragments), etc. Implemented case case. Sub-qualifiers (Hyper-, Hypo-, Proto-, Neo-) parametric variants base qualifier — fit naturally suffixes YAML file, modified thresholds. Specifiers apply compositionally qualifiers (e.g. Epileptic = leptic 0-50 cm band; Endoleptic = leptic 50-100 cm band) — implemented via decorator. Proposed YAML schema inst/rules/wrb2022/qualifiers.yaml: format makes expanding 200+ qualifiers matter generating YAML programmatically canonical text (already indexed). Risks v0.9: - Several qualifiers require Ch 3 diagnostics yet implemented (chernic, hortic, irragric, plaggic, pretic, terric, anthraquic, hydragric, hortic, panpaic, etc.). v0.9 must include ~12 missing diagnostics resolving qualifiers. - order principal qualifiers soil name matters: WRB lists principal qualifiers “according list top bottom” Ch 4 — .e., order listed per RSG canonical must preserved YAML.
code: qualifiers:   - code: ap     name: Abruptic     type: principal     applicable_rsgs: [PL, ST, LV, AC, LX, AL, RT]  # from Ch 4     test:       diagnostic: abrupt_textural_difference       max_top_cm: 100     references: "Ch 5, p 127"     sub_qualifiers:       - code: go         name: Geoabruptic         condition: "abrupt_textural_difference NOT associated with argic/natric/spodic upper limit"

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: Structurally identical implementation, system-specific diagnostics. Example — Latossolo: SiBCS key (inst/rules/sibcs5/key.yaml) tests order: Latossolos, Argissolos, Neossolos, Plintossolos, Chernossolos, Vertissolos, Gleissolos, Nitossolos, Cambissolos, Espodossolos, Luvissolos, Organossolos, Planossolos (adjustments per 5th edition). vignette 07-cross-system-correlation.Rmd classifies profile systems documents known correspondence patterns (Latossolo ↔︎ Ferralsol, Argissolo ↔︎ Acrisol/Lixisol/Alisol/Luvisol depending CEC BS%, Nitossolo Vermelho eutrófico ↔︎ Rhodic Nitisol, etc.).
code: #' Horizonte B latossólico (SiBCS) #' #' @references SiBCS 5ª ed. (Embrapa, 2018), Capítulo ... #' @export B_latossolico <- function(pedon) {   h <- pedon$horizons   tests <- list()   tests$minimum_thickness <- test_thickness(h, min_cm = 50)   tests$ki_lte_22 <- test_ki(h, max = 2.2)   tests$kr_lte_17 <- test_kr(h, max = 1.7)   tests$cec_by_clay <- test_cec_by_clay(h, max_cmol_per_kg_clay = 17)   tests$no_textural_gradient <- test_no_textural_gradient_b_latossolico(h)   tests$no_clay_films <- test_no_clay_films(h)   # ...   DiagnosticResult$new(     name = "B latossólico",     passed = all(vapply(tests, `[[`, logical(1), "passed")),     layers = tests$minimum_thickness$layers,     evidence = tests,     missing = attributes_missing_for_tests(tests, h),     reference = "SiBCS 5ª ed. (Embrapa)"   ) }

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: VLM never classifies. three things: Transforms textual descriptions (survey PDFs, field sheets, reports) structured data validated JSON schema. Extracts Munsell colour photos reference card present. Approximates horizon boundaries profile photos (explicitly low-flagged confidence). Every extracted value receives () provenance tag extracted_vlm, (b) confidence score provided model, (c) textual citation source applicable.

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: gives important option: run everything locally via Ollama (Gemma 3, Qwen2-VL, LLaVA) preserve institutional independence protect sensitive data. Particularly relevant given Rosa Deserto documentation context — extraction can run infrastructure control.
code: vlm_provider <- function(name = c("anthropic", "openai", "google", "ollama"),                          model = NULL, ...) {   name <- match.arg(name)   model <- model %||% default_model(name)   switch(name,     anthropic = ellmer::chat_anthropic(model = model, ...),     openai    = ellmer::chat_openai(model = model, ...),     google    = ellmer::chat_gemini(model = model, ...),     ollama    = ellmer::chat_ollama(model = model, ...)   ) }

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: function: 1. Reads document/image. 2. Loads prompt template (inst/prompts/) JSON schema (inst/schemas/). 3. Calls provider type = "json" enforced. 4. Validates return jsonvalidate. 5. validation fails, re-runs appending error prompt (3 attempts). 6. Reconciles existing data PedonRecord: never overwrites measured values; VLM fills NA fields lower-authority provenance, unless overwrite = TRUE.
code: extract_horizons_from_pdf(pedon, pdf_path,  provider) extract_munsell_from_photo(pedon, image,    provider) extract_structure_from_photo(pedon, image,  provider) extract_site_from_fieldsheet(pedon, image,  provider)

For each field, also provide: - a confidence score (0.0 to 1.0), - a short textual citation (<= 20 words) from the document supporting the   extraction.  If a field is absent, return null. DO NOT INFER OR GUESS.  Return valid JSON conforming to this schema: {schema_json}  Source document: --- {document_text} ---

title: soilKey — Package architecture
soilKey — Package architecture
text: SoilNet paper (Feldmann et al., 2025) shows generic VLMs perform horizon classification well — requires specialised model sequential segmentation hierarchical embeddings. Conservative position soilKey: Munsell photo reference card: testable, accepted moderate confidence. Horizon boundaries photo: extracted proposal, always confidence <= 0.4, flagged human review. Quantitative attributes (clay %, CEC, etc.) photo: never. come laboratory Module 4.

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: ISRIC publishes SoilGrids Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFFs accessible via WCS/WMS. WRB, SoilGrids directly provides P(RSG | pixel) principal RSGs.
code: spatial_prior_soilgrids <- function(pedon,                                      system  = c("wrb2022", "usda"),                                      buffer_m = 250,                                      source_url = soilgrids_wcs_url()) {    coord <- sf::st_sfc(sf::st_point(c(pedon$site$lon, pedon$site$lat)), crs = 4326)   coord_utm <- sf::st_transform(coord, utm_crs_for(pedon$site))   buf <- sf::st_buffer(coord_utm, dist = buffer_m)    rst <- terra::rast(source_url)   vals <- terra::extract(rst, terra::vect(buf), fun = mean, na.rm = TRUE)    # Normalise to a probability over the RSGs   probs <- normalize_rsg_probs(vals, system = system)   data.table::data.table(rsg_code = names(probs), probability = probs) }

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: prior enter deterministic key (remains authority). used three situations: Tie-breaking ambiguity: two RSGs nearly pass missing attributes, prior orders candidates. Sanity check: final classification P_prior < 0.01 region, warning emitted ClassificationResult. Example: classifying Cryosol Gainesville triggers immediate alert. Optional Bayesian posterior: separate posterior_classify() function user explicitly wants probabilistic rather deterministic result.

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: Mapa de Solos Brasil 1:5,000,000 (Embrapa 2021, style available QGIS) can reprojected raster SiBCS classes. natively provide probabilities, approximation via neighbourhood window (15×15 cells) gives approximate P based local frequencies.

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: Open Soil Spectral Library (OSSL), maintained Soil Spectroscopy Global Good consortium (Woodwell Climate Research Center, OpenGeoHub, ISRIC), provides >100k spectra paired laboratory data Vis-NIR (350–2500 nm) MIR (4000–600 cm⁻¹) ranges, open licence. R infrastructure documented https://soilspectroscopy.github.io/ossl-manual/ workflows PLSR, Cubist memory-based learning. terrain direct comparative advantage — Module 4 essentially pedagogical formalisation already soilVAE Vis-NIR/SWIR pipelines.

title: soilKey — Package architecture
code: fill_from_spectra <- function(pedon,                               library    = "ossl",                               region     = c("global", "south_america",                                              "north_america", "europe", "africa"),                               properties = c("clay_pct", "sand_pct", "silt_pct",                                              "cec_cmol", "bs_pct", "ph_h2o",                                              "oc_pct", "caco3_pct", "fe_dcb_pct"),                               method     = c("pretrained", "mbl", "plsr_local"),                               preprocess = "snv+sg1",                               k_neighbors = 100,                               overwrite  = FALSE) {    region <- match.arg(region); method <- match.arg(method)    # 1. Pre-processing of the user's spectrum   X <- preprocess_spectra(pedon$spectra$vnir, method = preprocess)    # 2. Prediction   preds <- switch(method,     pretrained  = predict_ossl_pretrained(X, properties = properties, region = region),     mbl         = predict_ossl_mbl(X, properties = properties, region = region,                                    k = k_neighbors),     plsr_local  = predict_ossl_plsr_local(X, properties = properties, region = region,                                            k = k_neighbors)   )   # preds: data.table with columns property, value, pi95_low, pi95_high, n_neighbors    # 3. Sanity-checked merge   for (i in seq_len(nrow(preds))) {     r <- preds[i]     horizon_idx <- r$horizon_idx     if (r$property %in% names(pedon$horizons) &&         (is.na(pedon$horizons[[r$property]][horizon_idx]) || overwrite)) {       pedon$horizons[[r$property]][horizon_idx] <- r$value       pedon$add_measurement(horizon_idx = horizon_idx,                             attribute = r$property,                             value = r$value,                             source = "predicted_spectra",                             confidence = pi_to_confidence(r$pi95_low, r$pi95_high))     }   }   pedon }

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: MBL via resemble::mbl tends outperform global PLSR heterogeneous libraries OSSL, integrates naturally regional filtering (South America subset Brazilian profiles). Suggested defaults: k = 100 neighbours, PLS-scores distance, 5 local components, internal LOO validation.

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: WRB/SiBCS key critical quantitative attributes: % clay (textural gradient argic / B textural), CEC per 1 kg clay (Ferralsol/Latossolo, Nitisol), base saturation (Acrisol vs. Lixisol vs. Luvisol), % Fe DCB (plinthic, ferralic). attributes come spectral prediction narrow PI95%, evidence_grade drops B. PI95% wide enough cross critical diagnostic threshold (e.g., predicted CEC = 15 ± 6 cmol/kg threshold 16), system reports ambiguity ClassificationResult$ambiguities suggests specific lab measurement missing_data.

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: Predictions outside plausible ranges biome (cross-checking SoilGrids climate data + WoSIS data summarised biome) raise warning. Example: CaCO₃ prediction = 12% humid Atlantic Forest profile almost certainly error spectral model problem acquisition.

title: soilKey — Package architecture
code: library(soilKey)  # 1. Build record pedon <- PedonRecord$new(   site = list(lat = -22.5, lon = -43.7, date = "2024-03-10",               country = "BR", parent_material = "gneiss",               elevation_m = 180, slope_pct = 8),   documents = "perfil_042_descricao.pdf",   spectra   = list(vnir = vnir_matrix),   # matrix 6 horizons × 2151 bands   images    = "perfil_042_parede.jpg" )  # 2. Extract from the PDF and the photo pedon <- pedon |>   extract_horizons_from_pdf(     provider = vlm_provider("ollama", model = "gemma3:27b")   ) |>   extract_munsell_from_photo(     provider = vlm_provider("ollama", model = "gemma3:27b")   )  pedon$validate()  # 3. Fill gaps with spectra via OSSL pedon <- fill_from_spectra(   pedon,   method     = "mbl",   region     = "south_america",   properties = c("clay_pct", "silt_pct", "sand_pct",                  "cec_cmol", "bs_pct", "ph_h2o", "oc_pct", "fe_dcb_pct") )  # 4. Spatial prior prior <- spatial_prior(pedon, source = "soilgrids", system = "wrb2022")  # 5. Classify in both systems res_wrb   <- classify_wrb2022(pedon, prior = prior, on_missing = "warn") res_sibcs <- classify_sibcs(pedon)  # 6. Inspect print(res_wrb) # ClassificationResult (WRB 2022) #   Name: Rhodic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric) #   Evidence grade: B (clay %, CEC predicted by spectra; pi95 narrow) #   Prior check: consistent (P_prior = 0.41) #   Ambiguities: none #   Missing data to refine: none # # Key trace (abbreviated): #   HS (Histosols)     — failed (no organic material >= 10 cm) #   AT (Anthrosols)    — failed (no anthric horizons) #   ... #   FR (Ferralsols)    — PASSED (ferralic horizon, 25–180 cm; CEC 12 cmol/kg clay)  # 7. Report report(list(res_wrb, res_sibcs), file = "perfil_042_report.html")

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: inst/examples/profiles_wrb/ contains one profile per RSG (32 total) extracted : - Didactic examples WRB 2022 (Annex Schad 2023, Table 1). - Soil Atlas Europe (JRC, 2005). - ISRIC’s soil monolith collection (re-classified WRB 2022). inst/examples/profiles_sibcs/ contains one profile per order (13) extracted : - RADAMBRASIL (volumes available digitally). - Embrapa Solos technical bulletins. - SiBCS 5th ed. reference profiles.

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: diagnostic dedicated test file:
code: # tests/testthat/test-diagnostics-wrb-argic.R test_that("argic passes on canonical Luvisol profile", {   pedon <- readRDS(test_path("fixtures", "luvisol_canonical.rds"))   res <- argic(pedon)   expect_true(res$passed)   expect_gte(length(res$layers), 1) })  test_that("argic fails on Ferralsol", {   pedon <- readRDS(test_path("fixtures", "ferralsol_canonical.rds"))   res <- argic(pedon)   expect_false(isTRUE(res$passed)) })  test_that("argic returns NA when clay data missing", {   pedon <- readRDS(test_path("fixtures", "no_clay_data.rds"))   res <- argic(pedon)   expect_true(is.na(res$passed))   expect_true(length(res$missing) > 0) })  test_that("argic discriminates from natric (ESP)", {   pedon <- readRDS(test_path("fixtures", "solonetz_with_clay_increase.rds"))   res_argic <- argic(pedon)   res_natric <- natric(pedon)   expect_false(isTRUE(res_argic$passed))  # excluded by ESP   expect_true(res_natric$passed) })

what: Factual state on 2026-04-27 (after 929b4dd – v0.7 SiBCS shipped)
title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: 20 commits main. 4 modules original scope implemented (Module 1 WRB key, Module 2 VLM, Module 3 spatial prior, Module 4 OSSL). v0.3.3 closed WRB coverage Ch 3.1/3.2/3.3, v0.3.4 brought strict RSG-level gates, v0.3.5 closed 32 WRB horizons. v0.7 delivers full Module 6 (SiBCS 5ª ed.): 17 diagnostic attributes + 24 horizons + 13 RSG-level orders wired canonical key Ch 4. 368 test_that blocks / 830 expectations passing. 1 deliberate skip (test requires ellmer installed). WRB 2022 + SiBCS 5ª ed. first-level key complete: 32/32 WRB RSGs + 13/13 SiBCS orders, canonical order system. 31 WRB fixtures + 13 SiBCS fixtures, classifying intended class. WRB 2022 key 32/32 RSGs canonical order strict Tier-2 gates VR//GL/PL/FR/CH/KS. 31 canonical fixtures, classifying intended RSG. Coverage Ch 3.1 (diagnostic horizons): 32 32 horizons implemented (full coverage). Includes petric variants (petrocalcic, petroduric, petrogypsic, petroplinthic), anthropogenic family (anthraquic, hydragric, hortic, irragric, plaggic, pretic, terric), vertic_horizon (strict), thionic, fragic, sombric, chernic, four historic niches v0.3.5: tsitelic (red Caucasian), panpaic (buried), limonic (meadow redox), protovertic (vertic-spectrum lower bound). Coverage Ch 3.2 (properties): 17 17 properties implemented (full coverage): abrupt_textural_difference, albeluvic_glossae, andic_properties, anthric_properties (via anthric_horizons), continuous_rock, gleyic_properties, lithic_discontinuity, protocalcic_properties, protogypsic_properties, reducing_conditions, retic_properties, shrink_swell_cracks, sideralic_properties, stagnic_properties, takyric_properties, vitric_properties, yermic_properties. Plus cryic_conditions, planic_features, leptic_features (belong Ch 3.2 operational properties). Coverage Ch 3.3 (materials): 19 19 materials implemented (full coverage): aeolic, artefacts, calcaric, claric, dolomitic, fluvic, gypsiric, hypersulfidic, hyposulfidic, limnic, mineral, mulmic, organic, organotechnic, ornithogenic, soil_organic_carbon, solimovic, technic_hard, tephric. Histic_horizon (Ch 3.1) also serves gate organic_material. Expanded horizon schema (24 new columns v0.3.3): cementation_class, p_mehlich3_mg_kg, worm_holes_pct, water_dispersible_clay_pct, sulfidic_s_pct, volcanic_glass_pct, phosphate_retention_pct, artefacts_industrial_pct, artefacts_urbic_pct, rock_origin, permafrost_temp_C, cracks_width_cm, cracks_depth_cm, polygonal_cracks_spacing_cm, desert_pavement_pct, varnish_pct, ventifact_pct, vesicular_pores, rupture_resistance, plasticity, al_kcl_cmol, layer_origin.

Oxisols wired v0.2 via oxic_usda() (delegating WRB ferralic). - R/key-usda.R — classify_usda(), run_usda_key(). - R/diagnostics-horizons-usda.R — oxic_usda() argillic_usda() delegations WRB ferralic / argic v0.2 (central criteria coincide; edge differences scheduled v0.8). v0.8 implement: mollic_usda, umbric_usda, ochric, kandic, spodic_usda, cambic_usda, calcic_usda, gypsic_usda, duripan, fragipan, placic, albic, petrocalcic, petrogypsic, gelic conditions, USDA organic materials, USDA andic properties, USDA vertic features, aridic moisture regime, argillic_low_bs, argillic_high_bs. Positioning: USDA Soil Taxonomy public maintained implementation key; SoilTaxonomy package CRAN covers lookup tables. soilKey + Module 5 first public implementation USDA key.

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: v0.2 delivers structural scaffold: - inst/rules/sibcs5/key.yaml — 13 orders provisional key order (O, V, E, F, G, M, P, T, L, N, C, S, R). Latossolos wired via B_latossolico() (delegating ferralic). Argissolos wired via B_textural() (delegating argic). - R/key-sibcs.R — classify_sibcs(), run_sibcs_key(). - R/diagnostics-horizons-sibcs.R — B_latossolico() B_textural() delegations v0.2. v0.7 implement: B nítico, B espódico, B incipiente, B plânico, B textural high activity, chernozêmico, húmico, proeminente, vertic character, glei within top 50, plinthic horizon, organic hydromorphic horizon, hortic horizon, alitic character, aluminic character, acric character. Direct validation Embrapa Solos (partnership via UFRRJ). Positioning: public R/Python package implements SiBCS today. soilKey + Module 6 first public implementation.

title: soilKey — Package architecture
text: package fills three identifiable gaps literature: public, maintained implementation complete WRB 2022 key. preface 4th edition acknowledges automated classification algorithms developed internally within IUSS WRB WG drafting, public release exists. SoilTaxonomy CRAN contains lookup tables. package SiBCS. Embrapa makes book QGIS styles available, inference library. tool integrates multimodal extraction + spectral prediction + taxonomic key single traceable workflow. methodological paper can position soilKey open infrastructure digital pedology, complementary SoilGrids mapping work (classifies covariates pixel scale) — soilKey classifies profile data pedon scale, using canonical key. distinct complementary inferences.

title: Contributing to soilKey
text: Thank considering contributing soilKey. document explains file issue, develop change, submit pull request.

title: Contributing to soilKey
text: soilKey enforces three architectural invariants. Contributions must violate : taxonomic key never delegated language model. LLMs restricted schema-validated extraction (Module 2). Every classification deterministic walk versioned YAML rules full decision trace. Every value carries provenance tag. contribution adds new attribute workflow, must populate pedon$provenance attribute, source, confidence, notes. Side modules never overrule key. Spatial priors flag inconsistencies silently change assigned RSG; spectral predictions fill missing attributes explicit confidence; multimodal extraction pulls structured data without writing class names. change requires reviewing invariants (e.g. strong reason delegate part key LLM), please open discussion issue first talk design.

title: Contributing to soilKey
text: Please use issue templates .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/: 🐛 Bug report – reproducible problem classification output, error messages, unexpected behaviour. 💡 Feature request – new diagnostic, qualifier, loader, workflow. 🌱 Soil profile classification help – classified real profile disagree result; ’ll trace . general “use soilKey X” questions, please start GitHub Discussion rather opening issue.

Install soilKey from the local checkout devtools::install("path/to/soilKey", dependencies = TRUE)  # 4. Run the test suite to confirm everything works testthat::test_local() # Expected: ~3,100+ PASS / 0 FAIL / ~10 SKIP

title: Contributing to soilKey
text: Branch main (master) every feature / fix. Branch names: feature/<short-description> fix/<short>. Keep branches focused: one logical change per PR.

title: Contributing to soilKey
text: Follow existing style: 2-space indent, snake_case, data.table large tables, R6 stateful objects. Roxygen2 docstrings exported functions. Cite canonical book + page number YAML rule comments (e.g. # WRB 2022 Ch 3.1.20, Salic horizon, p 49). Tests live tests/testthat/ use testthat::test_that().

title: Contributing to soilKey
text: Define function R/diagnostics-<scope>.R full roxygen documentation including page-precise references. Add unit tests tests/testthat/test-<scope>-<diagnostic>.R covering: positive case, negative case, NA handling, edge cases. Wire function relevant inst/rules/*.yaml file. Run roxygen2::roxygenise() regenerate man/*.Rd NAMESPACE. Run full test suite + R CMD check. Update NEWS.md one-line summary change.

Add tests using tiny synthetic example head = N slice. Document loader’s input schema, license, download instructions roxygen. Add benchmark report inst/benchmarks/reports/<dataset>_<DATE>.md /B numbers vs previous release.

title: Getting started with soilKey
text: Pick one 31 canonical profiles dropdown (upload horizons CSV), click Classify, read WRB / SiBCS / USDA names plus deterministic key trace evidence grade.
code: library(soilKey) run_demo()  # opens a one-screen Shiny app in your browser

what: B. One R call, one fixture
title: Getting started with soilKey
text: whole package: PedonRecord , classification . remaining sections walk build pedon side modules (VLM, spatial, spectral) fit together.
code: library(soilKey) #>  #> Attaching package: 'soilKey' #> The following object is masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     %||%  pedon <- make_ferralsol_canonical()      # canonical Latossolo Vermelho classify_wrb2022(pedon, on_missing = "silent")$name #> [1] "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)" classify_sibcs(pedon)$name #> [1] "Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos" classify_usda(pedon, on_missing = "silent")$name #> [1] "Rhodic Hapludox"

title: Getting started with soilKey
text: PedonRecord central data carrier. bundles site metadata, horizons table (fixed canonical schema – see horizon_column_spec() full list columns), optional spectra, images, documents, per-attribute provenance log. validator catches inverted depths, texture sums far 100, implausible pH, sum bases CEC, Munsell --range, handful soil-physical sanity checks.
code: my_pedon <- PedonRecord$new(   site = list(     id              = "example-001",     lat             = -22.5,     lon             = -43.7,     country         = "BR",     parent_material = "gneiss"   ),   horizons = data.frame(     top_cm      = c(0,  15, 65, 130),     bottom_cm   = c(15, 65, 130, 200),     designation = c("A", "Bw1", "Bw2", "C"),     clay_pct    = c(50, 60,  65,  60),     silt_pct    = c(15, 10,  8,   8),     sand_pct    = c(35, 30,  27,  32),     cec_cmol    = c(8,  5,   4.5, 4),     bs_pct      = c(20, 12,  10,  11),     ph_h2o      = c(4.8, 4.9, 5.0, 5.1),     oc_pct      = c(2.0, 0.4, 0.2, 0.1)   ) )  my_pedon$validate() #> ✔ PedonRecord validates: 4 horizons OK

title: Getting started with soilKey
text: soilKey ships sixteen canonical fixtures designed exactly one eleven v0.2 diagnostics passes . profile also classifies cleanly wired WRB key.
code: fixtures <- list(   Ferralsol  = make_ferralsol_canonical(),   Luvisol    = make_luvisol_canonical(),   Acrisol    = make_acrisol_canonical(),   Lixisol    = make_lixisol_canonical(),   Alisol     = make_alisol_canonical(),   Chernozem  = make_chernozem_canonical(),   Kastanozem = make_kastanozem_canonical(),   Phaeozem   = make_phaeozem_canonical(),   Calcisol   = make_calcisol_canonical(),   Gypsisol   = make_gypsisol_canonical(),   Solonchak  = make_solonchak_canonical(),   Cambisol   = make_cambisol_canonical(),   Plinthosol = make_plinthosol_canonical(),   Podzol     = make_podzol_canonical(),   Gleysol    = make_gleysol_canonical(),   Vertisol   = make_vertisol_canonical() )  ferralsol <- fixtures$Ferralsol ferralsol #>  #> ── PedonRecord ── #>  #> Site: id=FR-canonical-01 | (-22.5000, -43.7000) | BR | 2024-03-10 | on gneiss #> Horizons (5): #> 1) A 0-15 cm clay=50.0 silt=15.0 sand=35.0 CEC=8.0 pH=4.8 OC=2.0 #> 2) AB 15-35 cm clay=52.0 silt=14.0 sand=34.0 CEC=6.5 pH=4.7 OC=1.2 #> 3) BA 35-65 cm clay=55.0 silt=10.0 sand=35.0 CEC=5.5 pH=4.7 OC=0.6 #> 4) Bw1 65-130 cm clay=60.0 silt=8.0 sand=32.0 CEC=5.0 pH=4.8 OC=0.3 #> 5) Bw2 130-200 cm clay=60.0 silt=8.0 sand=32.0 CEC=4.8 pH=4.9 OC=0.2

title: Getting started with soilKey
text: Every diagnostic returns DiagnosticResult carrying per-sub-test evidence, missing-attribute report, layer indices satisfied, WRB literature reference.
code: ferralic(ferralsol) #>  #> ── DiagnosticResult: ferralic #> Status: PASSED #> Layers satisfying: 3, 4, 5 #> Sub-tests: #> [PASS] texture #> [PASS] cec_per_clay #> [PASS] thickness #> Reference: IUSS Working Group WRB (2022), Chapter 3.1.10, Ferralic horizon (p. #> 44) #> Notes: v0.3.1: ECEC/clay <= 12 test removed; not part of WRB 2022 ferralic. #> v0.9.67 engine=soilkey threshold = 16 cmol_c/kg clay.

what: 4. Diagnostic matrix across the canonical fixtures
title: Getting started with soilKey
text: Every fixture activates exactly one diagnostic (, argic-derived RSGs Acrisol / Lixisol / Alisol / Luvisol, just shared argic).
code: diagnostics <- c("argic", "ferralic", "mollic", "calcic", "gypsic", "salic",                   "cambic", "plinthic", "spodic",                   "gleyic_properties", "vertic_properties")  mat <- vapply(fixtures, function(p) {   vapply(diagnostics, function(d) {     fn <- get(d, envir = asNamespace("soilKey"))     isTRUE(fn(p)$passed)   }, logical(1)) }, logical(length(diagnostics)))  knitr::kable(t(mat))

what: 5. RSG-derived diagnostics: argic and mollic families
title: Getting started with soilKey
text: argic horizon shared four RSGs – Acrisols, Lixisols, Alisols, Luvisols – differ clay activity (CEC per kg clay) chemistry (BS Al saturation). soilKey provides one diagnostic per RSG runs argic() internally, applies activity chemistry tests argic layer: pattern mollic-derived family (Chernozems / Kastanozems / Phaeozems):
code: acrisol(make_acrisol_canonical())$passed #> [1] TRUE lixisol(make_lixisol_canonical())$passed #> [1] TRUE alisol (make_alisol_canonical())$passed #> [1] TRUE luvisol(make_luvisol_canonical())$passed #> [1] TRUE chernozem (make_chernozem_canonical())$passed #> [1] TRUE kastanozem(make_kastanozem_canonical())$passed #> [1] TRUE phaeozem  (make_phaeozem_canonical())$passed #> [1] TRUE

title: Getting started with soilKey
text: classify_wrb2022() consumes PedonRecord runs YAML key (inst/rules/wrb2022/key.yaml). v0.2 wires 16 32 RSGs end--end; 16 stubbed not_implemented_v01: markers return NA trace. canonical fixture maps intended RSG. trace shows RSGs tested, canonical key order, assigned one.
code: classify_wrb2022(ferralsol) #>  #> ── ClassificationResult (WRB 2022) ── #>  #> Name: Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, #> Rubic) #> RSG/Order: Ferralsols #> Qualifiers: Geric, Ferric, Rhodic, Chromic, Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, #> Rubic, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, #> al_ox_pct, fe_ox_pct, phosphate_retention_pct, volcanic_glass_pct, FALSE, #> volcanic_glass_pct, FALSE, FALSE, plinthite_pct, FALSE, plinthite_pct, FALSE, #> plinthite_pct, FALSE, top_cm, bottom_cm, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, #> redoximorphic_features_pct, FALSE, redoximorphic_features_pct, FALSE, FALSE, #> p_mehlich3_mg_kg, FALSE, p_mehlich3_mg_kg, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, #> FALSE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, TRUE, FALSE, #> TRUE, FALSE #> Evidence grade: A #>  #> ── Ambiguities #> - TC: Indeterminate -- missing 3 attribute(s): artefacts_pct, #> geomembrane_present, technic_hardmaterial_pct #> - CR: Indeterminate -- missing 1 attribute(s): permafrost_temp_C #> - VR: Indeterminate -- missing 1 attribute(s): slickensides #> - SC: Indeterminate -- missing 1 attribute(s): ec_dS_m #> - PZ: Indeterminate -- missing 2 attribute(s): al_ox_pct, fe_ox_pct #> - PT: Indeterminate -- missing 1 attribute(s): plinthite_pct #> - ST: Indeterminate -- missing 1 attribute(s): redoximorphic_features_pct #>  #> ── Missing data that would refine result #> artefacts_pct, geomembrane_present, technic_hardmaterial_pct, #> permafrost_temp_C, slickensides, ec_dS_m, redoximorphic_features_pct, #> al_ox_pct, fe_ox_pct, phosphate_retention_pct, volcanic_glass_pct, #> plinthite_pct #>  #> ── Warnings #> ! 12 distinct attribute(s) missing across the key trace -- see $missing_data #>  #> ── Key trace #> (16 RSGs tested before assignment) #> 1. HS Histosols -- failed #> 2. AT Anthrosols -- failed #> 3. TC Technosols -- NA (3 attrs missing) #> 4. CR Cryosols -- NA (1 attrs missing) #> 5. LP Leptosols -- failed #> 6. SN Solonetz -- failed #> 7. VR Vertisols -- NA (1 attrs missing) #> 8. SC Solonchaks -- NA (1 attrs missing) #> 9. GL Gleysols -- failed (1 attrs missing) #> 10. AN Andosols -- failed (4 attrs missing) #> 11. PZ Podzols -- NA (2 attrs missing) #> 12. PT Plinthosols -- NA (1 attrs missing) #> 13. PL Planosols -- failed #> 14. ST Stagnosols -- NA (1 attrs missing) #> 15. NT Nitisols -- failed #> 16. FR Ferralsols -- PASSED classifications <- vapply(fixtures, function(p) {   classify_wrb2022(p, on_missing = "silent")$rsg_or_order }, character(1)) data.frame(fixture = names(classifications), assigned_rsg = classifications) #>               fixture assigned_rsg #> Ferralsol   Ferralsol   Ferralsols #> Luvisol       Luvisol     Luvisols #> Acrisol       Acrisol     Acrisols #> Lixisol       Lixisol     Lixisols #> Alisol         Alisol      Alisols #> Chernozem   Chernozem   Chernozems #> Kastanozem Kastanozem  Kastanozems #> Phaeozem     Phaeozem    Phaeozems #> Calcisol     Calcisol    Calcisols #> Gypsisol     Gypsisol    Gypsisols #> Solonchak   Solonchak   Solonchaks #> Cambisol     Cambisol    Cambisols #> Plinthosol Plinthosol  Plinthosols #> Podzol         Podzol      Podzols #> Gleysol       Gleysol     Gleysols #> Vertisol     Vertisol    Vertisols

title: Getting started with soilKey
text: PedonRecord$add_measurement() records value’s provenance structured log. final ClassificationResult$evidence_grade summarises log –D scale: means every recorded value laboratory-measured, D means result rests attributes extracted VLM assumed user.
code: ferralsol_v <- make_ferralsol_canonical()  # Mark the Bw1 clay value as predicted from spectroscopy ferralsol_v$add_measurement(   horizon_idx = 4,   attribute   = "clay_pct",   value       = 60,   source      = "predicted_spectra",   confidence  = 0.85,   overwrite   = TRUE )  classify_wrb2022(ferralsol_v)$evidence_grade #> [1] "B" ferralsol_w <- make_ferralsol_canonical() ferralsol_w$add_measurement(1, "clay_pct", 50, "extracted_vlm",                               confidence = 0.7, overwrite = TRUE) classify_wrb2022(ferralsol_w)$evidence_grade #> [1] "D"

title: Getting started with soilKey
text: PedonRecord$to_aqp() returns aqp::SoilProfileCollection, allowing soilKey plug aqp’s plotting aggregation tooling without owning infrastructure:

title: Getting started with soilKey
text: horizon attributes missing, profile carries Vis-NIR MIR spectra, soilKey can fill gaps via Open Soil Spectral Library. pipeline preprocesses spectra (SNV / SG1 / trim), dispatches memory-based PLSR backend, writes prediction PedonRecord provenance predicted_spectra – authority hierarchy treats laboratory-measured VLM-extracted values. PI95 prediction interval mapped [0, 1] confidence score via pi_to_confidence().
code: # Synthetic example -- a profile with measured spectra but missing CEC. pr_spec <- make_synthetic_pedon_with_spectra(n_horizons = 4) pr_spec$horizons$cec_cmol <- NA_real_   # erase CEC  # Predict via memory-based learning against the OSSL global library. pr_filled <- fill_from_spectra(   pedon   = pr_spec,   backend = "mbl",         # or "plsr_local" / "pretrained"   attrs   = c("cec_cmol")  # which attributes to gap-fill )  # Each predicted cell is logged with provenance source = "predicted_spectra". pr_filled$provenance classify_wrb2022(pr_filled)$evidence_grade   # B (predicted_spectra present)

what: 10. Module 3 – SoilGrids / Embrapa spatial prior (sanity check)
title: Getting started with soilKey
text: deterministic key reached verdict, soilKey can cross-check verdict spatial prior derived ISRIC SoilGrids (global) Embrapa raster (Brazil). prior never overrides key – attaches prior_check entry result emits warning deterministic outcome lies low-probability region prior.
code: prior <- spatial_prior(lon = -43.7, lat = -22.5, source = "auto") prior   # data.table of (rsg_code, probability)  res <- classify_wrb2022(   pedon           = ferralsol,   prior           = prior,   prior_threshold = 0.01   # warn if assigned RSG has prior < 1% ) res$prior_check

title: Getting started with soilKey
text: field PDF photo can turned PedonRecord via extract_* functions, driven ellmer chat object (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Ollama). output schema-validated JSON (draft-07, inst/schemas/) {value, confidence, source_quote} per attribute, merged PedonRecord provenance extracted_vlm. package ships MockVLMProvider (R6) validation + retry loop can exercised tests without API key: real use:
code: mock <- MockVLMProvider$new(   responses = list(     list(horizons = list(       list(top_cm = 0,  bottom_cm = 15, designation = "A",             clay_pct = list(value = 30, confidence = 0.9,                             source_quote = "30% clay (table 1)")),       list(top_cm = 15, bottom_cm = 65, designation = "Bw",             clay_pct = list(value = 55, confidence = 0.85,                             source_quote = "Bw horizon, 55% clay"))     ))   ) ) pr_extracted <- extract_horizons_from_pdf(   pdf_path = "fieldsheet.pdf",   provider = mock          # in production: vlm_provider("anthropic") ) classify_wrb2022(pr_extracted)$evidence_grade   # C or D depending on cell coverage chat <- vlm_provider("anthropic", model = "claude-sonnet-4-5") pr   <- extract_horizons_from_pdf("RADAMBRASIL_perfil_007.pdf",                                     provider = chat) res  <- classify_wrb2022(pr) res

title: Getting started with soilKey
text: soilKey ships parallel SiBCS key alongside WRB 2022. 13 ordens wired canonical Cap 4 order; calling classify_sibcs() PedonRecord runs engine backs classify_wrb2022(). diagnostic helpers also Portuguese names match SiBCS literature. example:
code: # A canonical Latossolo (Brazilian Ferralsol equivalent) pr_lat <- make_latossolo_canonical() classify_sibcs(pr_lat, on_missing = "silent")$rsg_or_order #> [1] "Latossolos"  # A canonical Argissolo (B textural, low BS) pr_arg <- make_argissolo_canonical() classify_sibcs(pr_arg, on_missing = "silent")$rsg_or_order #> [1] "Argissolos"  # A canonical Nitossolo (clay >=35% throughout, B/A <=1.5, cerosidade) pr_nit <- make_nitossolo_canonical() classify_sibcs(pr_nit, on_missing = "silent")$rsg_or_order #> [1] "Nitossolos"  # Cross-system: the SAME profile classified by both keys classify_wrb2022(pr_lat, on_missing = "silent")$rsg_or_order #> [1] "Ferralsols" classify_sibcs(pr_lat, on_missing = "silent")$rsg_or_order #> [1] "Latossolos" # Atividade da fração argila (Ta vs Tb) per Cap 1, p 30 atividade_argila_alta(make_luvissolo_canonical())$passed   # TRUE  -> Ta #> [1] TRUE atividade_argila_alta(make_nitossolo_canonical())$passed   # FALSE -> Tb #> [1] FALSE  # Caráter alítico (Cap 1, p 32): Al >= 4 cmol_c/kg + sat Al >= 50% + V < 50% carater_alitico(make_argissolo_canonical())$passed #> [1] NA

what: 13. v0.7 scope and the v0.3.3+ roadmap
title: Getting started with soilKey
text: See ARCHITECTURE.md (package root) full design rationale.

title: Começando com soilKey (PT-BR)
text: Se você só quer ver soilKey funcionando ponta--ponta em um perfil real, sem escrever código R, há dois caminhos.

title: Começando com soilKey (PT-BR)
text: Faça upload de um CSV (uma linha por horizonte) ou edite manualmente, clique em Classify, e leia os nomes WRB / SiBCS / USDA junto com o key trace determinístico e evidence grade.
code: library(soilKey) run_classify_app()   # abre um app Shiny em uma única tela no navegador

what: B. Uma chamada R, uma fixture
title: Começando com soilKey (PT-BR)
text: É o pacote inteiro: PedonRecord entra, classificação sai. seções seguintes mostram como construir o seu próprio pedon e como os módulos laterais (VLM, espacial, espectral) se conectam.
code: library(soilKey) #>  #> Attaching package: 'soilKey' #> The following object is masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     %||%  pedon <- make_ferralsol_canonical()   # Latossolo Vermelho canônico classify_wrb2022(pedon, on_missing = "silent")$name #> [1] "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)" classify_sibcs(pedon)$name #> [1] "Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos" classify_usda(pedon, on_missing = "silent")$name #> [1] "Rhodic Hapludox"

title: Começando com soilKey (PT-BR)
text: PedonRecord é estrutura central de dados. Ele agrupa: Metadados de sítio (id, lat/lon, país, material de origem, …) Tabela de horizontes com o esquema canônico fixo (veja horizon_column_spec() para lista completa de colunas) Espectros opcionais (Vis-NIR / SWIR / MIR) Imagens opcionais (foto de perfil) Documentos opcionais (PDF / field report) Log de proveniência por atributo: cada valor numérico ou categórico carrega uma tag source ∈ {measured, predicted_spectra, extracted_vlm, inferred_prior, user_assumed} Ao chamar qualquer classify_*() em cima desse pedon, soilKey lê os dados, anda chave taxonômica, e retorna um objeto ClassificationResult com: O nome atribuído (Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos na SiBCS, por exemplo) O trace da chave (quais RSGs / Ordens foram testadas, quais diagnósticos passaram) O evidence grade -D resumindo qualidade dos dados que produziram decisão lista de atributos faltantes que, se medidos, mudariam ou refinariam classificação
code: meu_pedon <- PedonRecord$new(   site = list(     id              = "exemplo-001",     lat             = -22.5,     lon             = -43.7,     country         = "BR",     parent_material = "gnaisse"   ),   horizons = data.frame(     top_cm      = c(0,  15, 65, 130),     bottom_cm   = c(15, 65, 130, 200),     designation = c("A", "AB", "Bw1", "Bw2"),     munsell_hue_moist    = rep("2.5YR", 4),     munsell_value_moist  = c(3, 3, 4, 4),     munsell_chroma_moist = c(4, 6, 6, 6),     clay_pct = c(50, 55, 60, 60),     silt_pct = c(15, 10, 8,  8),     sand_pct = c(35, 35, 32, 32),     cec_cmol = c(8, 5.5, 5.0, 4.8),     bs_pct   = c(24, 14, 13, 13),     ph_h2o   = c(4.8, 4.7, 4.8, 4.9),     oc_pct   = c(2.0, 0.6, 0.3, 0.2)   ) )

title: Começando com soilKey (PT-BR)
text: interface é simétrica: um PedonRecord entra, um ClassificationResult sai. três funções aceitam um argumento on_missing que controla o que fazer quando atributos críticos não estão presentes: "warn" (padrão): avisa, classifica com base que tem "silent": classifica silenciosamente "error": para com erro
code: res_wrb   <- classify_wrb2022(meu_pedon, on_missing = "silent") res_sibcs <- classify_sibcs(meu_pedon, include_familia = TRUE) res_usda  <- classify_usda(meu_pedon,  on_missing = "silent")  res_wrb$name #> [1] "Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)" res_sibcs$name #> [1] "Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos, argilosa, moderado" res_usda$name #> [1] "Typic Hapludox"

what: Atalho: classificar nos três sistemas em uma chamada
title: Começando com soilKey (PT-BR)
text: classify_all() retorna uma lista nomeada com wrb, sibcs, usda (cada uma um ClassificationResult completo) e um resumo summary em data.frame de uma linha — útil para tabular muitos pedons de uma vez só.
code: todos <- classify_all(meu_pedon, on_missing = "silent") todos$summary #>                                                  wrb #> 1 Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic) #>                                                          sibcs           usda #> 1 Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos, argilosa, moderado Typic Hapludox

title: Começando com soilKey (PT-BR)
text: O trace da chave é o histórico determinístico da decisão. Cada RSG / Ordem / Sistema testado aparece com o resultado de cada diagnóstico envolvido: Isso é feature distintiva soilKey vs. um classificador “caixa-preta” baseado em LLM: cada classificação carrega o motivo. Se você discorda resultado, o trace mostra exatamente em qual diagnóstico chave se desviou da sua expectativa.
code: res_wrb$trace #> $HS #> $HS$code #> [1] "HS" #>  #> $HS$name #> [1] "Histosols" #>  #> $HS$passed #> [1] FALSE #>  #> $HS$evidence #> $HS$evidence[[1]] #> $HS$evidence[[1]]$test_name #> [1] title: Começando com soilKey (PT-BR)
text: Cada valor que entra na chave tem uma tag de proveniência: O evidence_grade ClassificationResult é regra “pior provenance entre os atributos que foram decisivos para classificação”: : tudo measured (laboratório) B: ao menos um predicted_spectra decisivo C: ao menos um extracted_vlm decisivo D: ao menos um inferred_prior ou user_assumed decisivo Isso garante que um perfil classificado partir de chemistry de laboratório recebe , mas o mesmo perfil com um valor de argila chave preenchido por VLM ou predição espectral é honestamente B ou C, mesmo que o nome final seja idêntico.
code: meu_pedon$add_measurement(   horizon_idx = 4,   attribute   = "clay_pct",   value       = 60,   source      = "predicted_spectra",   confidence  = 0.85,   notes       = "Vis-NIR PLSR-local, OSSL South-America library",   overwrite   = TRUE )

what: 5. Cross-system: o mesmo perfil em três taxonomias
title: Começando com soilKey (PT-BR)
text: Para um Latossolo Vermelho típico Cerrado / Mata Atlântica brasileira, esperamos: WRB: Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (...) SiBCS: Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos USDA: Rhodic Hapludox correspondência entre os três sistemas (Schad 2023, Annex Table 1) está documentada pacote — veja vignette("v03_cross_system_correlation", package = "soilKey").
code: todos <- classify_all(meu_pedon, on_missing = "silent") data.frame(   Sistema = c("WRB 2022", "SiBCS 5ª ed.", "USDA ST 13"),   Nome    = c(todos$wrb$name, todos$sibcs$name, todos$usda$name) ) #>        Sistema                                                         Nome #> 1     WRB 2022           Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic) #> 2 SiBCS 5ª ed. Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos, argilosa, moderado #> 3   USDA ST 13                                               Typic Hapludox

title: Começando com soilKey (PT-BR)
text: Para comunidade brasileira: o caminho mais natural é começar aqui (v01_getting_started_pt), depois ir para v03_cross_system_correlation para entender correspondência SiBCS ↔︎ WRB ↔︎ USDA, e finalmente v06_wosis_benchmark se você quiser validar contra dados reais.

what: 7. Onde reportar bugs / sugerir features / pedir ajuda
title: Começando com soilKey (PT-BR)
text: Bugs reproduzíveis: abra uma issue GitHub usando o template “🐛 Bug report”. Pedido de feature (novo diagnóstico, qualifier, loader): use o template “💡 Feature request”. Discordância sobre classificação de um perfil real: use o template “🌱 Soil profile classification help” — colamos juntos o trace da chave e identificamos exatamente onde soilKey divergiu da sua expectativa. Perguntas gerais: GitHub Discussions.

title: Começando com soilKey (PT-BR)
text: IUSS Working Group WRB (2022). World Reference Base Soil Resources, 4ª ed. International Union Soil Sciences, Vienna. PDF Soil Survey Staff (2022). Keys Soil Taxonomy, 13ª ed. USDA-NRCS, Washington, DC. Santos, H.G. et al. (2018). Sistema Brasileiro de Classificação de Solos, 5ª ed. revista e ampliada. Embrapa, Brasília.

title: End-to-end WRB 2022 classification with Ch 6 names
text: canonical fixture exposes published-quality profile. Use working pedon. glance horizons chemistry: Notable features WRB key: Clay 50-60 % throughout, hue 2.5YR, low chroma -> ferralic-like reddish tint; OC 2.0 % surface, decreasing depth; Low CEC (5-8 cmol+/kg fine earth) low BS (13-24 %); pH H2O 4.7-4.9, pH KCl 4.0-4.2 -> delta pH negative (Posic).
code: pr <- make_ferralsol_canonical() pr #>  #> ── PedonRecord ── #>  #> Site: id=FR-canonical-01 | (-22.5000, -43.7000) | BR | 2024-03-10 | on gneiss #> Horizons (5): #> 1) A 0-15 cm clay=50.0 silt=15.0 sand=35.0 CEC=8.0 pH=4.8 OC=2.0 #> 2) AB 15-35 cm clay=52.0 silt=14.0 sand=34.0 CEC=6.5 pH=4.7 OC=1.2 #> 3) BA 35-65 cm clay=55.0 silt=10.0 sand=35.0 CEC=5.5 pH=4.7 OC=0.6 #> 4) Bw1 65-130 cm clay=60.0 silt=8.0 sand=32.0 CEC=5.0 pH=4.8 OC=0.3 #> 5) Bw2 130-200 cm clay=60.0 silt=8.0 sand=32.0 CEC=4.8 pH=4.9 OC=0.2 knitr::kable(   pr$horizons[, .(top_cm, bottom_cm, designation,                   munsell_hue_moist, munsell_value_moist, munsell_chroma_moist,                   clay_pct, oc_pct, cec_cmol, bs_pct,                   ph_h2o, ph_kcl)] )

title: End-to-end WRB 2022 classification with Ch 6 names
text: classify_wrb2022() walks canonical Ch 4 RSG order (HS -> -> … -> RG) returns first RSG whose tier-2 gate satisfied. returned ClassificationResult carries: $rsg_or_order – assigned Reference Soil Group (, Ferralsols); $name – full Ch 6 name principal supplementary qualifiers; $qualifiers – resolved principal supplementary lists, plus per-qualifier trace; $trace – RSG--RSG key trace, including RSGs failed assignment; $evidence_grade – D, summarising provenance classification.
code: res <- classify_wrb2022(pr) res #>  #> ── ClassificationResult (WRB 2022) ── #>  #> Name: Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, #> Rubic) #> RSG/Order: Ferralsols #> Qualifiers: Geric, Ferric, Rhodic, Chromic, Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, #> Rubic, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, #> al_ox_pct, fe_ox_pct, phosphate_retention_pct, volcanic_glass_pct, FALSE, #> volcanic_glass_pct, FALSE, FALSE, plinthite_pct, FALSE, plinthite_pct, FALSE, #> plinthite_pct, FALSE, top_cm, bottom_cm, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, #> redoximorphic_features_pct, FALSE, redoximorphic_features_pct, FALSE, FALSE, #> p_mehlich3_mg_kg, FALSE, p_mehlich3_mg_kg, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, #> FALSE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, TRUE, FALSE, #> TRUE, FALSE #> Evidence grade: A #>  #> ── Ambiguities #> - TC: Indeterminate -- missing 3 attribute(s): artefacts_pct, #> geomembrane_present, technic_hardmaterial_pct #> - CR: Indeterminate -- missing 1 attribute(s): permafrost_temp_C #> - VR: Indeterminate -- missing 1 attribute(s): slickensides #> - SC: Indeterminate -- missing 1 attribute(s): ec_dS_m #> - PZ: Indeterminate -- missing 2 attribute(s): al_ox_pct, fe_ox_pct #> - PT: Indeterminate -- missing 1 attribute(s): plinthite_pct #> - ST: Indeterminate -- missing 1 attribute(s): redoximorphic_features_pct #>  #> ── Missing data that would refine result #> artefacts_pct, geomembrane_present, technic_hardmaterial_pct, #> permafrost_temp_C, slickensides, ec_dS_m, redoximorphic_features_pct, #> al_ox_pct, fe_ox_pct, phosphate_retention_pct, volcanic_glass_pct, #> plinthite_pct #>  #> ── Warnings #> ! 12 distinct attribute(s) missing across the key trace -- see $missing_data #>  #> ── Key trace #> (16 RSGs tested before assignment) #> 1. HS Histosols -- failed #> 2. AT Anthrosols -- failed #> 3. TC Technosols -- NA (3 attrs missing) #> 4. CR Cryosols -- NA (1 attrs missing) #> 5. LP Leptosols -- failed #> 6. SN Solonetz -- failed #> 7. VR Vertisols -- NA (1 attrs missing) #> 8. SC Solonchaks -- NA (1 attrs missing) #> 9. GL Gleysols -- failed (1 attrs missing) #> 10. AN Andosols -- failed (4 attrs missing) #> 11. PZ Podzols -- NA (2 attrs missing) #> 12. PT Plinthosols -- NA (1 attrs missing) #> 13. PL Planosols -- failed #> 14. ST Stagnosols -- NA (1 attrs missing) #> 15. NT Nitisols -- failed #> 16. FR Ferralsols -- PASSED

title: End-to-end WRB 2022 classification with Ch 6 names
text: RSG assigned, resolver walks canonical Ch 4 principal-qualifier list RSG (e.g. Ferralsols: Vetic, Posic, Acric, Lixic, Geric, Hyperdystric, ...) tests pedon. four principals pass Ferralsol fixture, canonical Ch 4 order: trace slot keeps every Ch 4 principal tested, including failed. Useful diagnostic debugging:
code: qres <- resolve_wrb_qualifiers(pr, "FR") qres$principal #> [1] "Geric"   "Ferric"  "Rhodic"  "Chromic" #>   Qualifier #> 1     Geric #> 2    Ferric #> 3    Rhodic #> 4   Chromic #>                                                                                                                                               Why #> 1 ECEC = sum of bases + Al_KCl <= 1.5 cmol+/kg fine earth in some layer of the upper 100 cm. Layer 4 (Bw1, top = 65 cm) has ECEC = 1.18 cmol+/kg. #> 2                                                                         Iron-rich subsoil (Fe_dcb >= 5%); fe_dcb_pct hits 8-9% in this fixture. #> 3                                   Hue 2.5YR moist, value < 4 in 25-150 cm. Bw1 has value = 4 (failing in some layers but BA satisfies value 3). #> 4                                                              Hue redder than 7.5YR + chroma > 4 in 25-150 cm subsoil. Bw1 chroma = 6 satisfies. trace_df <- do.call(   rbind,   lapply(names(qres$trace), function(q) {     t <- qres$trace[[q]]     data.frame(qualifier = q,                passed    = if (is.null(t$passed)) NA else t$passed,                note      = t$note %||% "")   }) ) head(trace_df, 12) #>       qualifier passed note #> 1         Vetic  FALSE      #> 2         Posic  FALSE      #> 3         Acric  FALSE      #> 4         Lixic  FALSE      #> 5         Geric   TRUE      #> 6  Hyperdystric  FALSE      #> 7   Hypereutric  FALSE      #> 8        Histic  FALSE      #> 9         Folic  FALSE      #> 10        Andic  FALSE      #> 11       Vitric  FALSE      #> 12      Sombric  FALSE

title: End-to-end WRB 2022 classification with Ch 6 names
text: Supplementary qualifiers parenthesised tags WRB Ch 6 name. refine soil description texture / chemistry / colour information strong enough principal still informative. tag captures Ferralsol:
code: qres$supplementary #> [1] "Clayic"  "Humic"   "Dystric" "Ochric"  "Rubic" #>   Qualifier #> 1    Clayic #> 2     Humic #> 3   Dystric #> 4    Ochric #> 5     Rubic #>                                                                                                Why #> 1 Clay >= 60 % over a layer thicker than 30 cm in the upper 100 cm; Bw1 has clay = 60% over 65 cm. #> 2                                 Weighted OC >= 1 % in the upper 50 cm; weighted OC ~ 1.1 % here. #> 3                       BS < 50 % throughout 20-100 cm; BS = 13-24 % across all four upper layers. #> 4                      OC >= 0.2 % in upper 10 cm + no mollic + no umbric; surface has OC = 2.0 %. #> 5         Hue <= 5YR + chroma >= 4 in upper 100 cm (less strict than Rhodic). 2.5YR / 6 satisfies.

title: End-to-end WRB 2022 classification with Ch 6 names
text: format_wrb_name() glues principal supplementary canonical form: exactly string returned classify_wrb2022()$name.
code: format_wrb_name(   rsg_name      = "Ferralsols",   principal     = qres$principal,   supplementary = qres$supplementary ) #> [1] "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)"

title: End-to-end WRB 2022 classification with Ch 6 names
text: several qualifiers WRB family (e.g. Calcic / Hypocalcic / Protocalcic) pass RSG, -specific sibling appears name. suppression applied candidates evaluated works principal supplementary lists. internal table: worked example: synthetic Calcisol satisfies Calcic, Hypocalcic, Protocalcic simultaneously collapse just Calcic.
code: str(soilKey:::.wrb_qualifier_families) #> List of 10 #>  $ salinity: chr [1:3] "Hypersalic" "Salic" "Hyposalic" #>  $ sodicity: chr [1:3] "Hypersodic" "Sodic" "Hyposodic" #>  $ calcic  : chr [1:4] "Hypercalcic" "Calcic" "Hypocalcic" "Protocalcic" #>  $ gypsic  : chr [1:4] "Hypergypsic" "Gypsic" "Hypogypsic" "Protogypsic" #>  $ vertic  : chr [1:2] "Vertic" "Protovertic" #>  $ albic   : chr [1:2] "Hyperalbic" "Albic" #>  $ skeletic: chr [1:2] "Hyperskeletic" "Skeletic" #>  $ eutric  : chr [1:2] "Hypereutric" "Eutric" #>  $ dystric : chr [1:2] "Hyperdystric" "Dystric" #>  $ alic    : chr [1:2] "Hyperalic" "Alic" soilKey:::.suppress_qualifier_siblings(   c("Mollic", "Calcic", "Hypocalcic", "Protocalcic", "Cambic") ) #> [1] "Mollic" "Calcic" "Cambic"

title: End-to-end WRB 2022 classification with Ch 6 names
text: classify_wrb2022() reports evidence_grade summarising provenance every attribute used classification. means every used value lab-measured; D means result rests VLM-extracted user-assumed values. Ferralsol fixture measured values, grade . v01_getting_started vignette shows pedon$add_measurement() source = "extracted_vlm" source = "predicted_spectra" lowers grade – always know robust classification .

title: End-to-end WRB 2022 classification with Ch 6 names
text: report() generic takes ClassificationResult (list , PedonRecord – case three keys run automatically) writes single-file HTML report inline CSS, external network requests, suitable archiving laudo. PDF path goes rmarkdown::render() requires working LaTeX engine. HTML output includes: cross-system summary, full key trace per system, qualifiers (principal + supplementary), evidence grade, ambiguities, missing data, horizons table, per-source provenance summary. ClassificationResult$report(file) R6-method-style equivalent delegates code.
code: # Pass the three classifications as a list: results <- list(   classify_wrb2022(pr),   classify_sibcs(pr, include_familia = TRUE),   classify_usda(pr) ) report(results, file = "perfil_ferralsol.html", pedon = pr)  # Or pass the pedon directly and let report() run the three keys: report(pr, file = "perfil_ferralsol.html")  # Same content as PDF (requires LaTeX): # report(pr, file = "perfil_ferralsol.pdf")

title: End-to-end WRB 2022 classification with Ch 6 names
text: v03_cross_system_correlation vignette runs profile Brazilian SiBCS USDA Soil Taxonomy keys shows alignment three classifications.
code: #> WRB 2022 name : Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic) #> Assigned RSG  : Ferralsols #> Principal     : Geric, Ferric, Rhodic, Chromic #> Supplementary : Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic #> Evidence grade: A

what: 1. The same Ferralsol through three keys
title: Cross-system classification: WRB 2022, SiBCS 5, USDA Soil Taxonomy
text: canonical Ferralsol fixture clay-rich, low-CEC, low-BS Brazilian profile. three systems converge conceptual unit: WRB : Ferralsol canonical Ch 6 qualifiers. SiBCS: Latossolo Vermelho (red Latossolo). USDA : Oxisol. three-way alignment textbook correspondence: WRB Ferralsol ↔︎ SiBCS Latossolo ↔︎ USDA Oxisol.
code: pr <- make_ferralsol_canonical()  w <- classify_wrb2022(pr, on_missing = "silent") s <- classify_sibcs (pr, on_missing = "silent") u <- classify_usda  (pr, on_missing = "silent")  data.frame(   System  = c("WRB 2022", "SiBCS 5", "USDA"),   Class   = c(w$rsg_or_order, s$rsg_or_order, u$rsg_or_order),   Full    = c(w$name, s$name, u$name) ) #>     System      Class #> 1 WRB 2022 Ferralsols #> 2  SiBCS 5 Latossolos #> 3     USDA    Oxisols #>                                                                            Full #> 1 Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic) #> 2                                      Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos #> 3                                                               Rhodic Hapludox

what: 2. Cross-system table on the canonical fixtures
title: Cross-system classification: WRB 2022, SiBCS 5, USDA Soil Taxonomy
text: subset fixtures across three systems: table reproduces canonical correspondences:
code: fxs <- list(   Ferralsol  = make_ferralsol_canonical(),   Acrisol    = make_acrisol_canonical(),   Lixisol    = make_lixisol_canonical(),   Luvisol    = make_luvisol_canonical(),   Nitisol    = make_nitisol_canonical(),   Vertisol   = make_vertisol_canonical(),   Andosol    = make_andosol_canonical(),   Histosol   = make_histosol_canonical(),   Podzol     = make_podzol_canonical(),   Cambisol   = make_cambisol_canonical(),   Gleysol    = make_gleysol_canonical(),   Plinthosol = make_plinthosol_canonical() )  tab <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(fxs), function(nm) {   pr <- fxs[[nm]]   data.frame(     Fixture = nm,     WRB     = classify_wrb2022(pr, on_missing = "silent")$rsg_or_order,     SiBCS   = classify_sibcs (pr, on_missing = "silent")$rsg_or_order,     USDA    = classify_usda  (pr, on_missing = "silent")$rsg_or_order   ) })) #> Warning in max(h$worm_holes_pct[contiguous] %||% 0, na.rm = TRUE): no #> non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf #> Warning in max(h$worm_holes_pct[contiguous] %||% 0, na.rm = TRUE): no #> non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf knitr::kable(tab)

title: Cross-system classification: WRB 2022, SiBCS 5, USDA Soil Taxonomy
text: profile can land different “RSGs” system uses slightly different gating criterion. important divergences: Argic horizon chemistry: SiBCS lumps Acrisol/Lixisol/Alisol/Luvisol Argissolos, WRB splits CEC (Lixisol/Luvisol = high CEC) base saturation (Acrisol/Alisol = low BS, low/high Al). USDA equivalent split Ultisol (low BS) vs Alfisol (high BS). Andic vs cambic priority: volcanic ash soil weak Bw can land WRB Andosol SiBCS Cambissolo andic criteria narrowly fail. USDA Andisol uses andic criterion WRB. Plinthic / petric variants: WRB Plinthosols, USDA Plinthudults / Plinthumults, SiBCS Plintossolos – rely plinthite criterion apply different gating order key.

title: Cross-system classification: WRB 2022, SiBCS 5, USDA Soil Taxonomy
text: profile, system provides additional discriminators: WRB qualifier ladder (Geric, Ferric, Rhodic, Chromic + Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic) expressive: captures CEC, iron, colour, texture, organic carbon, base saturation one parenthesised string. SiBCS achieves 2nd-categorical-level subordem names (e.g. Latossolos Vermelhos, Distroférricos), encoded separately. USDA’s information density concentrated great group / subgroup level (currently scaffolded v1.0).
code:
pr <- make_ferralsol_canonical() w  <- classify_wrb2022(pr, on_missing = "silent") s  <- classify_sibcs (pr, on_missing = "silent") u  <- classify_usda  (pr, on_missing = "silent")  cat("WRB principal qualifiers:    ",     paste(w$qualifiers$principal,     collapse = ", "), "
") #> WRB principal qualifiers:     Geric, Ferric, Rhodic, Chromic cat("WRB supplementary qualifiers:",     paste(w$qualifiers$supplementary, collapse = ", "), "
") #> WRB supplementary qualifiers: Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic cat("SiBCS subordem (2nd level):  ", s$rsg_or_order,    "
") #> SiBCS subordem (2nd level):   Latossolos cat("USDA suborder / great group: ", u$rsg_or_order,    "
") #> USDA suborder / great group:  Oxisols

title: Cross-system classification: WRB 2022, SiBCS 5, USDA Soil Taxonomy
text: soilKey runs SiBCS key canonical fixtures used WRB. fixture-level correspondence asserted test suite:
code: sibcs_expectations <- c(   Ferralsol  = "Latossolos",   Acrisol    = "Argissolos",   Lixisol    = "Argissolos",   Luvisol    = "Argissolos",   Nitisol    = "Nitossolos",   Vertisol   = "Vertissolos",   Andosol    = "Cambissolos",   # Cambissolo Háplico Tb (Andic-leaning)   Histosol   = "Organossolos",   Podzol     = "Espodossolos",   Plinthosol = "Plintossolos" )  actual <- vapply(names(sibcs_expectations), function(nm) {   fx <- get(paste0("make_", tolower(nm), "_canonical"))()   classify_sibcs(fx, on_missing = "silent")$rsg_or_order }, character(1)) #> Warning in max(h$worm_holes_pct[contiguous] %||% 0, na.rm = TRUE): no #> non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf #> Warning in max(h$worm_holes_pct[contiguous] %||% 0, na.rm = TRUE): no #> non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf  data.frame(   fixture       = names(sibcs_expectations),   expected      = unname(sibcs_expectations),   actual        = actual,   match         = actual == sibcs_expectations ) #>               fixture     expected       actual match #> Ferralsol   Ferralsol   Latossolos   Latossolos  TRUE #> Acrisol       Acrisol   Argissolos   Argissolos  TRUE #> Lixisol       Lixisol   Argissolos   Argissolos  TRUE #> Luvisol       Luvisol   Argissolos   Luvissolos FALSE #> Nitisol       Nitisol   Nitossolos   Nitossolos  TRUE #> Vertisol     Vertisol  Vertissolos  Vertissolos  TRUE #> Andosol       Andosol  Cambissolos  Cambissolos  TRUE #> Histosol     Histosol Organossolos Organossolos  TRUE #> Podzol         Podzol Espodossolos Espodossolos  TRUE #> Plinthosol Plinthosol Plintossolos Plintossolos  TRUE

title: Cross-system classification: WRB 2022, SiBCS 5, USDA Soil Taxonomy
text: Brazilian field surveys – producers extension services use SiBCS, international literature uses WRB. PedonRecord resolved keys gives bilingual name without re-entering data. Global benchmarks – WoSIS profiles carry WRB names; legacy datasets use Soil Taxonomy. cross-system table makes corpora analysable side side. Concept stress-testing – WRB SiBCS disagree profile, cause almost always single threshold (CEC/clay, BS, andic). Inspecting disagreement fast way find data-entry errors identify ambiguous profiles deserve closer look. next vignette (v04_vlm_extraction) shows PedonRecord can assembled PDFs field photos via vision-language extraction, cross-system pass can run freshly-described profiles without manual data entry.

title: Vision-language extraction of pedon data (Module 2)
text: MockVLMProvider exposes $chat() method ellmer chat object, pops responses pre-loaded queue. soilKey’s VLM logic talks provider $chat(), swapping real chat (custom backend) transparent.

title: Vision-language extraction of pedon data (Module 2)
text: VLM responses constrained JSON Schema (Draft-07). schema horizon extraction shipped inst/schemas/horizon.json; rendered prompt template lives inst/prompts/extract_horizons.md. schema enforces strict types – top_cm must numeric, designation must string, Munsell colours come {hue, value, chroma, confidence, source_quote} triples, etc. response fails validation triggers retry loop validation error included next prompt.

what: 3. Walking the extraction loop with the mock
title: Vision-language extraction of pedon data (Module 2)
text: Suppose model returns following horizon-level JSON synthetic Latossolo description: Wire mock provider return JSON, send prompt validate_or_retry, watch loop: returned res$data parsed (validated) R object. res$attempts == 1 canned response passed validation first try. validation_error_at argument MockVLMProvider$new() forces attempt return malformed JSON, exercising retry path – see tests/testthat/test-vlm-extract.R worked retry-loop test.
code:
horizon_json <- '{   "horizons": [     {       "top_cm": 0,       "bottom_cm": 15,       "designation": "A",       "munsell_moist": {"hue": "2.5YR", "value": 3, "chroma": 4,                           "confidence": 0.85, "source_quote": "vermelho-escuro"},       "clay_pct": {"value": 50, "confidence": 0.9, "source_quote": "muito argilosa (50%)"},       "oc_pct"  : {"value": 2.0, "confidence": 0.85, "source_quote": "C org. 2.0%"}     },     {       "top_cm": 15,       "bottom_cm": 65,       "designation": "Bw1",       "munsell_moist": {"hue": "2.5YR", "value": 3, "chroma": 6,                           "confidence": 0.85, "source_quote": "vermelho"},       "clay_pct": {"value": 60, "confidence": 0.9, "source_quote": "muito argilosa"},       "oc_pct"  : {"value": 1.2, "confidence": 0.85, "source_quote": "C org. 1.2%"}     }   ] }' mock <- MockVLMProvider$new(responses = list(horizon_json))  res <- soilKey:::validate_or_retry(   provider    = mock,   prompt      = "extract horizons from <fake document>",   schema      = "horizon",   max_retries = 0L )  str(res, max.level = 2) #> List of 3 #>  $ data    :List of 1 #>   ..$ horizons:List of 2 #>  $ raw     : chr "{
  "horizons": [
    {
      "top_cm": 0,
      "bottom_cm": 15,
      "designation": "A",
   "| __truncated__ #>  $ attempts: int 1

title: Vision-language extraction of pedon data (Module 2)
text: apply_horizons_extraction() consumes parsed JSON merges PedonRecord, recording value provenance log source = "extracted_vlm".
code: pr <- PedonRecord$new(   site = list(id = "VLM-demo", lat = -22.5, lon = -43.7,                 country = "BR", parent_material = "gneiss"),   horizons = data.table::data.table(top_cm = numeric(0), bottom_cm = numeric(0)) )  added <- soilKey:::apply_horizons_extraction(pr, res$data, overwrite = TRUE) cat("Provenance entries added:", added, "\n") #> Provenance entries added: 12  # Inspect what landed in the horizons table. pr$horizons[, .(top_cm, bottom_cm, designation,                 munsell_hue_moist, munsell_value_moist, munsell_chroma_moist,                 clay_pct, oc_pct)] #>    top_cm bottom_cm designation munsell_hue_moist munsell_value_moist #>     <num>     <num>      <char>            <char>               <num> #> 1:      0        15           A             2.5YR                   3 #> 2:     15        65         Bw1             2.5YR                   3 #>    munsell_chroma_moist clay_pct oc_pct #>                   <num>    <num>  <num> #> 1:                    4       50    2.0 #> 2:                    6       60    1.2

title: Vision-language extraction of pedon data (Module 2)
text: Every value came VLM carries source = "extracted_vlm" provenance log. classification’s evidence grade reacts: Even simplest call classify_wrb2022() reflect lower-confidence VLM source via lower evidence grade profile assembled lab data. (See vignette 02 grade ladder.)
code: prov <- pr$provenance head(prov[, .(horizon_idx, attribute, source, confidence)]) #>    horizon_idx            attribute        source confidence #>          <int>               <char>        <char>      <num> #> 1:           1          designation extracted_vlm         NA #> 2:           1    munsell_hue_moist extracted_vlm       0.85 #> 3:           1  munsell_value_moist extracted_vlm       0.85 #> 4:           1 munsell_chroma_moist extracted_vlm       0.85 #> 5:           1             clay_pct extracted_vlm       0.90 #> 6:           1               oc_pct extracted_vlm       0.85
