title: Vision-language extraction of pedon data (Module 2)
text: sensitive field descriptions, governmental surveys just reproducibility without API key, recommended path local Gemma 4 model via Ollama. data never leaves machine.
code: # Install once (any platform) ollama pull gemma4:e4b           # ~3 GB, multimodal, fits a laptop # ollama pull gemma4:31b         # frontier dense, best quality # ollama serve                   # background server # Local Gemma 4 edge -- multimodal text + image (and audio). provider <- vlm_provider("ollama")             # default: gemma4:e4b # provider <- vlm_provider("ollama", model = "gemma4:31b")  # frontier  extract_horizons_from_pdf(   pedon       = pr,   pdf_path    = "field-reports/perfil-LV-001.pdf",   provider    = provider,   max_retries = 3L )

text: canonical case – “PDF (optionally) profile photo, give three classifications one-pager report” – chain everything single call. default provider local Gemma 4 edge: Photos go extract_munsell_from_photo(); site metadata extract_site_from_fieldsheet(). three follow validate--retry contract accept provider exposes $chat(prompt, ...). next vignette (v05_spatial_spectra_pipeline) shows SoilGrids spatial prior OSSL Vis-NIR predictions can fill remaining gaps pedon classification.

title: Spatial prior + OSSL spectra pipeline (Modules 3 & 4)
text: Take canonical Latossolo intentionally erase CEC base-saturation values lower horizons. fill back simulated OSSL predictions §3. classification incomplete pedon already differs: evidence grade reflects missing data – per-RSG trace records RSGs returned NA attributes erased.
code: pr_full   <- make_ferralsol_canonical() pr_partial <- pr_full$clone(deep = TRUE) pr_partial$horizons[3:5, c("cec_cmol", "bs_pct") := NA]  pr_partial$horizons[, .(top_cm, bottom_cm, designation,                         clay_pct, cec_cmol, bs_pct, oc_pct)] #>    top_cm bottom_cm designation clay_pct cec_cmol bs_pct oc_pct #>     <num>     <num>      <char>    <num>    <num>  <num>  <num> #> 1:      0        15           A       50      8.0     24    2.0 #> 2:     15        35          AB       52      6.5     17    1.2 #> 3:     35        65          BA       55       NA     NA    0.6 #> 4:     65       130         Bw1       60       NA     NA    0.3 #> 5:    130       200         Bw2       60       NA     NA    0.2 res_partial <- classify_wrb2022(pr_partial, on_missing = "silent") res_partial$rsg_or_order #> [1] "Nitisols" res_partial$evidence_grade #> [1] "A"

title: Spatial prior + OSSL spectra pipeline (Modules 3 & 4)
text: prior probability vector RSGs source – regional SoilGrids extract, national Embrapa map, interpolated kriging surface, etc. vignette build inline example runs without network access: prior_consistency_check() confirms deterministic call (FR) supported prior: Now suppose deterministic key instead landed Cambisols. prior flag disagreement (Cambisols probability 0.12 vs dominant Ferralsols 0.62 – inconsistency margin): deterministic key never overwritten prior. check flags cases manual review warranted; user remains charge final assignment. Real production runs source prior spatial_prior_soilgrids() (live SoilGrids-WCS request) spatial_prior_embrapa():
code: # Synthetic prior consistent with the gneiss-Mata-Atlantica context: # Ferralsols dominate, with a tail of Acrisols and Cambisols. prior <- data.table::data.table(   rsg_code    = c("FR", "AC", "CM", "AL"),   probability = c(0.62, 0.20, 0.12, 0.06) ) prior #>    rsg_code probability #>      <char>       <num> #> 1:       FR        0.62 #> 2:       AC        0.20 #> 3:       CM        0.12 #> 4:       AL        0.06 chk <- prior_consistency_check(rsg_code = "FR", prior = prior, threshold = 0.05) chk #> $consistent #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $p #> [1] 0.62 #>  #> $threshold #> [1] 0.05 #>  #> $status #> [1] "consistent" #>  #> $note #> [1] "Assigned RSG 'FR' has prior probability 0.620 at this location (>= threshold 0.050)." #>  #> $top_prior #>    rsg_code probability #>      <char>       <num> #> 1:       FR        0.62 #> 2:       AC        0.20 #> 3:       CM        0.12 prior_consistency_check(rsg_code = "AL", prior = prior, threshold = 0.05) #> $consistent #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $p #> [1] 0.06 #>  #> $threshold #> [1] 0.05 #>  #> $status #> [1] "consistent" #>  #> $note #> [1] "Assigned RSG 'AL' has prior probability 0.060 at this location (>= threshold 0.050)." #>  #> $top_prior #>    rsg_code probability #>      <char>       <num> #> 1:       FR        0.62 #> 2:       AC        0.20 #> 3:       CM        0.12 prior <- spatial_prior_soilgrids(pr_partial, buffer_m = 250)

title: Spatial prior + OSSL spectra pipeline (Modules 3 & 4)
text: OSSL workflow : Pre-process raw spectra (SNV / Savitzky-Golay 1st derivative). predict_ossl_mbl() – memory-based learning (recommended); predict_ossl_plsr_local() – partial-least-squares local subset; predict_ossl_pretrained() – pre-trained Cubist RF model. Convert property’s prediction-interval width –D confidence grade via pi_to_confidence(). fill_from_spectra() writes predicted value horizons table adds provenance entry source = "predicted_spectra". production call look like (skipped vignette OSSL multi-GB dataset downloaded): vignette, simulate predicted values directly pedon$add_measurement(): predicted values now horizons table, provenance log records predicted_spectra:
code: fill_from_spectra(   pr_partial,   library     = "ossl",   region      = "south_america",   properties  = c("clay_pct", "cec_cmol", "bs_pct", "oc_pct"),   method      = "mbl",   preprocess  = "snv+sg1",   k_neighbors = 100L,   ossl_library = "/path/to/ossl-soilsite-vnir.parquet" ) preds <- list(   list(idx = 3, attribute = "cec_cmol", value = 5.5, confidence = 0.78),   list(idx = 3, attribute = "bs_pct",   value = 14,  confidence = 0.72),   list(idx = 4, attribute = "cec_cmol", value = 4.9, confidence = 0.79),   list(idx = 4, attribute = "bs_pct",   value = 13,  confidence = 0.74),   list(idx = 5, attribute = "cec_cmol", value = 4.7, confidence = 0.70),   list(idx = 5, attribute = "bs_pct",   value = 13,  confidence = 0.71) )  pr_filled <- pr_partial$clone(deep = TRUE) for (p in preds) {   pr_filled$add_measurement(     horizon_idx = p$idx,     attribute   = p$attribute,     value       = p$value,     source      = "predicted_spectra",     confidence  = p$confidence,     overwrite   = TRUE   ) }  pr_filled$horizons[, .(top_cm, bottom_cm, cec_cmol, bs_pct)] #>    top_cm bottom_cm cec_cmol bs_pct #>     <num>     <num>    <num>  <num> #> 1:      0        15      8.0     24 #> 2:     15        35      6.5     17 #> 3:     35        65      5.5     14 #> 4:     65       130      4.9     13 #> 5:    130       200      4.7     13 prov <- pr_filled$provenance prov[source == "predicted_spectra", .(horizon_idx, attribute, source, confidence)] #>    horizon_idx attribute            source confidence #>          <int>    <char>            <char>      <num> #> 1:           3  cec_cmol predicted_spectra       0.78 #> 2:           3    bs_pct predicted_spectra       0.72 #> 3:           4  cec_cmol predicted_spectra       0.79 #> 4:           4    bs_pct predicted_spectra       0.74 #> 5:           5  cec_cmol predicted_spectra       0.70 #> 6:           5    bs_pct predicted_spectra       0.71

title: Spatial prior + OSSL spectra pipeline (Modules 3 & 4)
text: combine_priors() merges multiple sources (SoilGrids + Embrapa + custom map) weights, returning single normalised prior can feed prior_consistency_check(): posterior_classify() take ClassificationResult prior, returning posterior probability RSGs (deterministic key contributes sharply peaked likelihood). Used ranking ambiguous fixtures active-learning loops.
code: combined <- combine_priors(   priors = list(     soilgrids = data.table::data.table(rsg_code = c("FR", "AC", "CM"),                                           probability = c(0.62, 0.20, 0.18)),     embrapa   = data.table::data.table(rsg_code = c("FR", "AC", "NT"),                                           probability = c(0.55, 0.30, 0.15))   ),   weights = c(soilgrids = 0.6, embrapa = 0.4) ) combined #>    rsg_code  probability #>      <char>        <num> #> 1:       FR 0.7139748011 #> 2:       AC 0.2841641547 #> 3:       CM 0.0017189621 #> 4:       NT 0.0001420821

title: Spatial prior + OSSL spectra pipeline (Modules 3 & 4)
text: deterministic key always runs first never overwritten. Module 3 gives probabilistic sanity check external maps warns disagreement. Module 4 fills missing horizon attributes spectra, full provenance, evidence grade tracks substitution. Putting together, can take partially-described pedon, fill gaps spectra, classify deterministically, cross-check result global map – one pipeline. next vignette (v06_wosis_benchmark) shows run whole stack scale WoSIS global pedon archive paper-grade validation.

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS
text: benchmark profile triple (P,T,C)(P, T, C): PP – PedonRecord assembled source dataset’s horizons + chemistry; TT – target RSG name source’s classification (e.g. WoSIS’ cwrb_reference_soil_group); CC – candidate classification produced classify_wrb2022(P). benchmark reports four numbers per source: Top-1 agreement – fraction profiles C$rsg=TC\$rsg = T. Top-3 agreement – fraction TT among three highest-likelihood RSGs trace (key trace + spatial prior, available). Coverage – fraction profiles CC definite RSG (“Regosols” catch-). Indeterminate-rate – fraction profiles C$rsgC\$rsg NA owing missing diagnostic attributes. Confusion matrices reported alongside headline numbers: RSGs soilKey confuses (e.g. Acrisol vs Lixisol differ base saturation, disagreement border informative).

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS
text: Beyond RSG, classify_wrb2022() returns principal- supplementary-qualifier lists. benchmark can deepened track agreement qualifier prefix soil name (strongest principal fired). canonical fixtures: -specific principal qualifier per canonical fixture.
code: qualifier_prefix <- vapply(names(expected), function(code) {   fx <- fixfns[[code]]()   res <- classify_wrb2022(fx, on_missing = "silent")   if (length(res$qualifiers$principal) == 0) NA_character_   else res$qualifiers$principal[1] }, character(1))  knitr::kable(   data.frame(fixture          = names(qualifier_prefix),              principal_prefix = qualifier_prefix),   caption = "Most-specific principal qualifier per canonical fixture." )

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS
text: mini-benchmark proves package’s internal consistency. paper-grade benchmark runs flow ISRIC WoSIS, global archive ~118 000 georeferenced pedon descriptions WRB 2014 / 2022 classifications. protocol intentionally separated package (live download vignettes) can re-run controlled date. driver script shipped inst/benchmarks/run_wosis_benchmark.R. Skeleton: flow extends naturally : WoSIS subsets region – e.g. South America , Brazilian profiles (relevant SiBCS cross-system check). WoSIS profiles missing one attributes – exercise gap-filling pipeline (vignette 05) report agreement uplift predicted_spectra. WoSIS profiles labelled v2014 vs v2022 RSGs – benchmark separates soilKey’s v2022-key penalised WoSIS used deprecated RSG name.
code: library(soilKey)  # 1. Pull a snapshot of WoSIS profiles via the WoSIS API. profiles <- read_wosis_profiles(   url     = "https://wosis.isric.org/api/v3/profiles?format=json",   page_size = 500L )  # 2. Build a PedonRecord per profile. pedons <- lapply(profiles, build_pedon_from_wosis)  # 3. Classify each through the v0.9.3 key. classifications <- lapply(pedons, classify_wrb2022, on_missing = "silent")  # 4. Compare against the WoSIS-recorded RSG. bench <- mapply(function(c, p) {   data.frame(     profile_id  = p$site$id,     target_rsg  = p$site$wosis_rsg,     assigned    = c$rsg_or_order,     grade       = c$evidence_grade,     match       = c$rsg_or_order == p$site$wosis_rsg   ) }, classifications, pedons, SIMPLIFY = FALSE) bench <- do.call(rbind, bench)  # 5. Headline numbers. list(   n              = nrow(bench),   top1           = mean(bench$match, na.rm = TRUE),   indeterminate  = mean(is.na(bench$assigned)),   pct_grade_A    = mean(bench$grade == "A"),   by_rsg         = table(bench$target_rsg, bench$assigned) )

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS
text: benchmark run produces versioned report inst/benchmarks/reports/wosis_<DATE>.md containing: Snapshot date WoSIS subset used. Top-1 / Top-3 agreement, 95% CI non-parametric bootstrap. Confusion matrix (RSG-level) qualifier-prefix agreement. Per-RSG agreement common confused pair . Evidence-grade distribution. Notable disagreements diagnostic-level traces, QA. script used periodic regression run ships minor soilKey release.

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS

Sourcing file running package root produces real, reproducible numbers without network call: recent canonical run (committed inst/benchmarks/reports/) achieved: canonical run release-time CI check key + qualifier system + cross-system mapping hold ; WoSIS run paper-grade external validation.

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS
text: soilKey’s benchmarking layer intentionally minimal: mini-benchmark canonical fixtures (vignette) fully reproducible inside rendered HTML acts CI check key + qualifier system holds 100% top-1 agreement profiles constructed match RSG. offline run_canonical_benchmark() driver runs three systems multi-valued cross-system targets writes versioned report — invoked per release, network-free. full WoSIS benchmark run--per-release, -line paper, driver script reports versioned inst/benchmarks/. three layers report metrics comparison across releases direct. closes methodological loop opened architecture document: deterministic key, side modules (VLM, spatial, OSSL), benchmark integrated without able overrule others.

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS
text: ISRIC WoSIS GraphQL endpoint occasionally returns “canceling statement due statement timeout” load — observed consistently ~40 profiles per session May 2026. naïve read_wosis_profiles_graphql() abort whole pull surface hard error; unfriendly 30+ profiles already came back fine. v0.9.27 adds per-page retry exponential backoff (1 s, 2 s, 4 s, 8 s; 4 attempts) plus graceful degradation: least one page succeeded, transient failures later pages return partial pull rather aborting. makes live WoSIS benchmark robust enough run CI casual user sessions without surprise crashes.
code: source(system.file("benchmarks", "run_wosis_benchmark.R",                     package = "soilKey")) profs <- read_wosis_profiles_graphql(   continent = "South America",   n_max     = 100L,   page_size = 10L,   verbose   = TRUE ) length(profs) #> [1] 40   # 100 was requested but server timed out at offset = 40; #>          # the partial pull was returned cleanly.

what: 2. Spatial guide – before any pedon data
title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: soil_classes_at_location() queries SoilGrids 2.0 (WRB-coded raster user provides) returns ranked list likely classes plus canonical attribute thresholds distinguish . function classify – tells pedologist “likely standing Ferralsol; look confirm”.
code: guide <- soil_classes_at_location(   lat        = field_lat,   lon        = field_lon,   system     = "wrb2022",   source_url = "https://files.isric.org/soilgrids/latest/data/wrb/MostProbable.vrt" )  guide$distribution #> # Ranked candidate classes: #> # rsg_code  rsg_name      probability #> # FR        Ferralsols    0.62 #> # AC        Acrisols      0.21 #> # NT        Nitisols      0.12 #> # CM        Cambisols     0.05 guide$typical_attributes #> # Per-class diagnostic thresholds to confirm in the field.

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: pedologist arrives pit, photographs wall Munsell chart, scans field sheet, exports survey report PDF. classify_from_documents() chains entire downstream pipeline – VLM extraction, three classifications, optional report rendering – single call. default provider local Gemma 4 edge (gemma4:e4b, ~3 GB, multimodal text + image + audio) via Ollama – API key, data leaving laptop. Pull model : Every extracted attribute stamped source = "extracted_vlm" PedonRecord’s provenance log; deterministic key consumed PedonRecord unaware value got . architectural invariant – key never delegated model – holds. rest vignette keep working populated pedon res$pedon.
code: ollama pull gemma4:e4b ollama serve res <- classify_from_documents(   pdf      = "perfil_042_descricao.pdf",   image    = "perfil_042_parede.jpg",   report   = "perfil_042.html",   provider = "ollama"  # default; uses gemma4:e4b )  res$classifications$wrb$name #> [1] "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)" res$classifications$sibcs$name #> [1] "Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos, argilosa, moderado" res$classifications$usda$name #> [1] "Rhodic Hapludox" # For a runnable demo without Ollama / a real PDF, reuse the # canonical Ferralsol fixture -- the downstream code is the same. pedon <- make_ferralsol_canonical()

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: Vis-NIR scan available surface horizon, classify_by_spectral_neighbours() adds another evidence layer. finds K spectrally similar OSSL profiles within regional radius returns probabilistic class prediction. biome-aware regional filter prevents analogy drifting non-tropical reference soils.
code: # Hypothetical: a real OSSL South-America library with WRB labels # obtained via `download_ossl_subset_with_labels()`. ossl_lib <- download_ossl_subset_with_labels(   region          = "south_america",   max_distance_km = 10 )  # Pull the surface-horizon Vis-NIR scan from the populated pedon. query_spectrum <- pedon$spectra$vnir[1, ]  spectral <- classify_by_spectral_neighbours(   spectrum     = query_spectrum,   ossl_library = ossl_lib,   k            = 25,   region       = list(lat = field_lat, lon = field_lon,                       radius_km = 500) ) spectral$distribution #> # class    n_neighbours  probability #> # FR              22       0.88 #> # AC               2       0.08 #> # NT               1       0.04 spectral$neighbours #> # The 25 closest OSSL profiles + their distances + labels.

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: canonical step. classify_wrb2022() / classify_sibcs() / classify_usda() walk canonical YAML rules populated PedonRecord.
code: cls_wrb   <- classify_wrb2022(pedon, on_missing = "silent") cls_sibcs <- classify_sibcs(pedon, include_familia = TRUE) cls_usda  <- classify_usda(pedon)  cls_wrb$name #> [1] "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)" cls_sibcs$name #> [1] "Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos, argilosa, moderado" cls_usda$name #> [1] "Rhodic Hapludox"  # Each ClassificationResult carries the full key trace, the per- # attribute provenance, and an evidence grade A/B/C/D. cls_wrb$evidence_grade #> [1] "A" length(cls_wrb$trace)         # number of RSGs tested before assignment #> [1] 16

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: report() writes self-contained HTML one-pager cross-system summary, full key trace, evidence grade, qualifiers, ambiguities, missing-data hints, horizons table, per-source provenance summary. output single HTML file inline CSS – external network requests, suitable emailing colleague attaching laudo.
code: results <- list(wrb = cls_wrb, sibcs = cls_sibcs, usda = cls_usda) report(results, file = "perfil_042.html", pedon = pedon)

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: report_to_qgis() produces multi-layer GeoPackage (.gpkg) QGIS reads natively. GeoPackage carries three layers: pedon_point – POINT geometry profile coordinates classification metadata attributes (WRB / SiBCS / USDA names, RSG / Ordem / Order codes, evidence grades, principal qualifiers, supplementary qualifiers, hyperlink rendered HTML report). horizons_table – one row per horizon, canonical horizon-schema attributes. Joined pedon_point site_id. provenance_log – per-(horizon, attribute, source) provenance rows downstream auditing. QGIS: Layer → Add Layer → Add Vector Layer → perfil_042.gpkg. point appears canvas classification metadata feature pop-; styling rules can map symbol colour evidence grade assigned RSG.
code: results <- list(wrb = cls_wrb, sibcs = cls_sibcs, usda = cls_usda) report_to_qgis(   pedon           = pedon,   classifications = results,   file            = "perfil_042.gpkg",   report_html     = "perfil_042.html" )

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: step’s output carries explicit provenance next; final evidence_grade reflects worst-source rule applied attributes decisive assigned name. Two pedologists running pipeline documents get output bit--bit.
code: # Pipeline summary: # #   field GPS      ->  soil_classes_at_location()         "what to expect" #                                  | #                                  v #   PDF + photo    ->  classify_from_documents() (Gemma 4)  populates PedonRecord #                                  | #                                  v #   Vis-NIR scan   ->  classify_by_spectral_neighbours()    spectral prior #                                  | #                                  v #                  ->  classify_wrb2022()  + classify_sibcs() + classify_usda() #                                  |       (the deterministic step -- canonical) #                                  v #                  ->  report() / report_to_qgis()         deliverables

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: soilKey separates four distinct stages: Spatial guides (soil_classes_at_location) – expectations soil-class raster. Extraction (classify_from_documents, extract_*) – VLM populates PedonRecord, never classifies. Spectral analogy (classify_by_spectral_neighbours) – OSSL nearest-neighbour analogy prior. Deterministic classification (classify_wrb2022 / classify_sibcs / classify_usda) – canonical step. Plus two delivery formats: HTML reports (report) GeoPackage exports (report_to_qgis). four stages preserve provenance evidence grading; deterministic key remains thing assigns class.

title: KSSL + NASIS: multi-level USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark
text: Kellogg Soil Survey Laboratory (NCSS lab data, USDA-NRCS) ships ~36 000 pedons full analytical chemistry (clay, sand, silt, OC, pH, CEC, BS, …) minimal field morphology. NASIS Morphological database (separate sqlite distribution) carries matching field-survey morphology: Munsell colors, structure grade / size / type, clay films (phpvsf), slickensides, pediagfeatures diagnostic-features table (~13 500 entries identifying argillic, mollic, cambic, spodic, fragipan, etc.). load_kssl_pedons_with_nasis() joins two peiid depth-overlap matching, returning one PedonRecord per profile lab morphology populated: NASIS-derived attributes coverage 2021 snapshot (n=865 quality-filtered): high Munsell + structure coverage powers pediagfeatures tie-breaker (v0.9.21) – canonical lab + morphology gate returns passed = NA, surveyor’s diagnostic flag flips TRUE. argillic / fragipan / clay-films paths (v0.9.27, v0.9.28, v0.9.31) leverage NASIS slot direct positive evidence.
code: peds <- load_kssl_pedons_with_nasis(   gpkg   = "<path>/ncss_labdata.gpkg",   sqlite = "<path>/NASIS_Morphological_09142021.sqlite",   head   = 3000)  length(peds) #> [1] 2964   # after the 3000-row gpkg slice and the join  peds[[1]]$horizons[, c("designation", "clay_pct", "munsell_chroma_moist",                           "clay_films_amount")] #>    designation clay_pct munsell_chroma_moist clay_films_amount #> 1: Ap          22.5     3                    NA #> 2: Bt1         34.7     4                    common #> 3: Bt2         38.3     4                    many

title: KSSL + NASIS: multi-level USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark
text: list PedonRecords reference_usda, reference_usda_subgroup, reference_usda_grtgroup, reference_usda_suborder populated (gpkg loader handles four), benchmark_run_classification() measures top-1 accuracy four hierarchy levels:
code: # Quality filter: profiles with usable clay data and a non-empty # subgroup reference label. keep <- vapply(peds, function(p) {   hz <- p$horizons   if (is.null(hz) || nrow(hz) == 0) return(FALSE)   if (!any(!is.na(hz$clay_pct))) return(FALSE)   !is.null(p$site$reference_usda_subgroup) &&     !is.na(p$site$reference_usda_subgroup) &&     nzchar(p$site$reference_usda_subgroup) }, logical(1)) peds <- peds[keep]  for (lvl in c("order", "suborder", "great_group", "subgroup")) {   res <- benchmark_run_classification(peds, system = "usda",                                          level = lvl, boot_n = 500L)   cat(sprintf("%-12s n=%d  top1=%.4f  CI=[%.3f, %.3f]\n",                 lvl, res$n_evaluated, res$accuracy_top1,                 res$accuracy_ci[1], res$accuracy_ci[2])) }

title: KSSL + NASIS: multi-level USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark
text: values reported methodology paper. measured n=2638 large-scale validation sample (3x n=865 development sample) 500 bootstrap reps ±1.7 pp confidence intervals.

title: KSSL + NASIS: multi-level USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark
text: v0.9.24 → v0.9.31 release series progressively closed key reasoning gaps. Apples--apples n=865 development sample: v0.9.25 KST 13ed Great Group canonicaliser remains largest single-version GG lift (+3.84 pp, +59 % relative). required classifier changes — predictor already correct KST 13ed; comparison unfair legacy KSSL labels (span Soil Taxonomy editions 8 13).

what: 5. The four levels are independently measurable
title: KSSL + NASIS: multi-level USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark
text: level = "great_group" reads pedon$site$reference_usda_grtgroup compares LAST token classify_usda(pedon)$name v0.9.25 KST canonicaliser collapses edition-driven renames. level = "suborder" reads pedon$site$reference_usda_suborder applies .gg_to_suborder() (~70-Suborder canonical-suffix mapping derived KST 13ed Ch 4) predicted Great Group name. level = "subgroup" reads pedon$site$reference_usda_subgroup compares full subgroup name token--token (Subgroup modifier kept intact; Great Group canonicalised). level = "order" reads pedon$site$reference_usda compares top-level Order name directly.

title: KSSL + NASIS: multi-level USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark
text: KSSL samp_taxgrtgroup populated historical pedon descriptions spanning Soil Taxonomy editions 8 13. Several Great Group names changed editions, KSSL retroactively update . soilKey’s classifier follows KST 13ed (current edition), direct string equality predicted (13ed) reference (mixed editions) Great Group names produces false-negative misses every profile whose KSSL label pre-13ed name. 16 common edition-driven renames KSSL: canonicaliser canonicalise_kst13ed_gg() collapses pair (triple, Hapl-/Endo-/Epi- splits) shared canonical key. Apply ref pred comparing – Subgroup modifier (Typic / Aquic / …) left intact canonicalisation affects Great Group token.

title: KSSL + NASIS: multi-level USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark
text: 4.2 % Subgroup top-1 leaves substantial headroom. v0.9.27 confusion analysis identified dominant remaining gaps: Pale- / Glossic Alfisol prefixes (~11 misses, deferred v0.9.32+): current pale_qualifying_usda() proxy strict (clay >= 35 %); KST 13ed actually defines Pale- clay-stability within 150 cm. Tightening without regressing Hapludalfs delicate work. NASIS data sparsity (~47 % profiles lack pediagfeatures / phpvsf record): roadmap candidate field-survey morphology + soilKey’s pedometric proxies third evidence path. Endo/Epi-aquic precise distinction: currently v0.9.25 canonicaliser collapses fair comparison; future release restore distinction inferring saturation depth redoximorphic_features_pct distribution.

title: KSSL + NASIS: multi-level USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark
text: KSSL + NASIS join soilKey’s headline real-data benchmark. exercises every level USDA Soil Taxonomy 13ed hierarchy n=2638 profiles full lab + morphology data, confidence intervals tight enough (±1.7 pp) paper-grade claims. v0.9.24 → v0.9.31 release series moved Great Group accuracy 6.5 % baseline 10.92 %, primarily KST 13ed name canonicaliser (v0.9.25) collapsed edition-driven renames historical KSSL labels. WRB SiBCS benchmark axes, see vignettes v06_wosis_benchmark FEBR / Embrapa report inst/benchmarks/reports/embrapa_v0929_2026-05-03.md.

title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: Esta vinheta acompanha um unico perfil de solo Brasil tropical desde os dados brutos de campo + laboratorio ate tres classificacoes paralelas (SiBCS 5a edicao, WRB 2022, USDA Soil Taxonomy 13a edicao), passando por: construcao PedonRecord formato canonico soilKey; inspecao dos diagnosticos disparados (B textural, atividade da argila, V%); classificacao tripla com explicacao trace; relatorio HTML pronto para entregar um colega ou aluno; (opcional) cruzamento com o priore espacial MapBiomas Solos v0.9.48. O objetivo e dar ao agronomo / pedologo brasileiro um caminho reproduzivel e em portugues, estilo dos boletins da Embrapa Solos. Todo o codigo abaixo roda sem dependencia externa pesada (nem rede, nem ESDB, nem OSSL); etapa final MapBiomas e opcional e exige um raster baixado.

what: O perfil: Argissolo Vermelho-Amarelo distrofico tipico
title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: Adaptado Levantamento Reconhecimento dos Solos Estado Rio de Janeiro (Embrapa, 2003), perfil RJ-1 da regiao de Itaguai. Coordenadas aproximadas: 22.86 S, 43.78 W. Material de origem: sedimentos argilosos Terciario. Construindo o PedonRecord (R6 com site + horizons):
code: horizontes <- data.table::data.table(   top_cm                = c(0,    20,   55,   115,  170),   bottom_cm             = c(20,   55,   115,  170,  220),   designation           = c("A",  "AB", "Bt1","Bt2","BC"),   munsell_hue_moist     = c("10YR","7.5YR","5YR","2.5YR","2.5YR"),   munsell_value_moist   = c(4,     4,     4,     3,     3),   munsell_chroma_moist  = c(3,     5,     6,     6,     6),   structure_grade       = c("moderate", "moderate",                               "strong", "strong", "moderate"),   structure_type        = c("granular", "subangular blocky",                               "subangular blocky",                               "subangular blocky",                               "subangular blocky"),   clay_films_amount     = c(NA, "few", "common", "common", "few"),   clay_pct              = c(18,   28,   45,   42,   38),   silt_pct              = c(30,   25,   20,   22,   24),   sand_pct              = c(52,   47,   35,   36,   38),   ph_h2o                = c(5.5,  5.3,  5.0,  5.0,  5.1),   ph_kcl                = c(4.4,  4.3,  4.1,  4.1,  4.2),   oc_pct                = c(1.5,  0.6,  0.3,  0.2,  0.2),   cec_cmol              = c(8.0,  6.0,  5.5,  4.5,  4.0),   bs_pct                = c(35,   25,   20,   18,   20),   # V baixa -> distrofico   al_cmol               = c(0.5,  0.8,  1.2,  1.5,  1.4),   ca_cmol               = c(2.0,  1.4,  1.0,  0.7,  0.7),   mg_cmol               = c(0.6,  0.4,  0.3,  0.2,  0.2),   k_cmol                = c(0.10, 0.06, 0.04, 0.03, 0.03),   na_cmol               = c(0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02),   bulk_density_g_cm3    = c(1.30, 1.40, 1.45, 1.45, 1.42) ) horizontes <- soilKey:::ensure_horizon_schema(horizontes) str(horizontes[, .(designation, top_cm, bottom_cm, ph_h2o, clay_pct, bs_pct)]) #> Classes 'data.table' and 'data.frame':   5 obs. of  6 variables: #>  $ designation: chr  "A" "AB" "Bt1" "Bt2" ... #>  $ top_cm     : num  0 20 55 115 170 #>  $ bottom_cm  : num  20 55 115 170 220 #>  $ ph_h2o     : num  5.5 5.3 5 5 5.1 #>  $ clay_pct   : num  18 28 45 42 38 #>  $ bs_pct     : num  35 25 20 18 20 #>  - attr(*, ".internal.selfref")=<pointer: 0x55cbe5409f20> perfil <- PedonRecord$new(   site = list(     id        = "RJ-1-Itaguai",     lat       = -22.86,     lon       = -43.78,     country   = "BR",     state     = "RJ",     municipality = "Itaguai",     parent_material = "sedimentos argilosos do Terciario",     survey_year = 2003,     reference_source = "Embrapa Solos (2003) - Levantamento RJ"   ),   horizons = horizontes ) perfil #>  #> ── PedonRecord ── #>  #> Site: id=RJ-1-Itaguai | (-22.8600, -43.7800) | BR | on sedimentos argilosos do #> Terciario #> Horizons (5): #> 1) A 0-20 cm clay=18.0 silt=30.0 sand=52.0 CEC=8.0 pH=5.5 OC=1.5 #> 2) AB 20-55 cm clay=28.0 silt=25.0 sand=47.0 CEC=6.0 pH=5.3 OC=0.6 #> 3) Bt1 55-115 cm clay=45.0 silt=20.0 sand=35.0 CEC=5.5 pH=5.0 OC=0.3 #> 4) Bt2 115-170 cm clay=42.0 silt=22.0 sand=36.0 CEC=4.5 pH=5.0 OC=0.2 #> 5) BC 170-220 cm clay=38.0 silt=24.0 sand=38.0 CEC=4.0 pH=5.1 OC=0.2

title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: Antes de chamar chave completa, vale rodar os predicados-chave para sentir o perfil. Isso e equivalente ao trabalho classificador humano antes de aplicar chave: Os tres devem disparar TRUE para um Argissolo distrofico tipico.
code:
# B textural (SiBCS Cap 5 / WRB argic): gradiente de argila bt   <- soilKey::B_textural(perfil) arg  <- soilKey::argic(perfil) cat("B_textural (SiBCS):", bt$passed,     "  argic (WRB):",     arg$passed, "
") #> B_textural (SiBCS): TRUE   argic (WRB): TRUE  # Atividade da argila (SiBCS Cap 5) ta <- soilKey::atividade_argila_alta(perfil) cat("atividade_argila_alta:", ta$passed, "
") #> atividade_argila_alta: FALSE  # Saturacao por bases (V%) -- distrofico se V < 50 distr <- soilKey::distrofico(perfil) cat("distrofico:", distr$passed, "
") #> distrofico: TRUE

title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: descida vai nivel da Ordem (Argissolos) ate o Subgrupo, passando pelos discriminantes: Matiz Munsell em B (5YR / 2.5YR) -> Subordem Vermelho-Amarelo V% baixa em B -> Grande Grupo Distrofico Atividade da argila baixa, sem cerosidade forte -> Subgrupo tipico

PV Argissolos Vermelhos -- NA #> 4. ??  -- NA #> 5. PVd Argissolos Vermelhos Distroficos -- NA #> 6. ??  -- NA #> 7. PVdTp Argissolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos -- NA #> 8. ??  -- NA

text: WRB devolve o RSG mais qualificadores (em ordem canonica): Acrisol Dystric Cutanic (caracter argico + V baixa + presenca de revestimentos argilosos).

title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: Esses tres rotulos sao complementares, nao competidores. O SiBCS captura nuance brasileira (Distrofico, Tb, presenca/ausencia de cerosidade); o WRB captura o consenso internacional (Acrisol); o USDA-ST encaixa esquema norte-americano (Ultisol). Todos os tres apontam para mesma realidade: um solo argico, acido, baixa em bases, vermelho-amarelo.
code: data.frame(   Sistema  = c("SiBCS 5a", "WRB 2022", "USDA-ST 13a"),   Classe   = c(res$sibcs$name, res$wrb$name, res$usda$name),   EvidGrade = c(res$sibcs$evidence_grade %||% NA,                   res$wrb$evidence_grade   %||% NA,                   res$usda$evidence_grade  %||% NA) ) #>       Sistema #> 1    SiBCS 5a #> 2    WRB 2022 #> 3 USDA-ST 13a #>                                                                                  Classe #> 1                    Argissolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos, argilosa, moderado, Tmob #> 2 Ferralic Cutanic Acrisol (Loamic, Dystric, Ochric, Profondic, Rhodic, Rubic, Chromic) #> 3                                                                     Typic Kandiudults #>   EvidGrade #> 1         A #> 2         A #> 3         A

title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: O HTML carrega: nome canonico em cada sistema, qualificadores, evidencia grade, trace dos predicados, ambiguidades, atributos faltantes, e os horizontes formatados estilo planilha de boletim.
code: res$sibcs$report(   file  = "perfil_RJ1.html",   format = "html",   pedon = perfil ) # Ou, para os tres sistemas em um arquivo: soilKey::report(   list(res$sibcs, res$wrb, res$usda),   file  = "perfil_RJ1_triplo.html",   format = "html",   pedon = perfil )

title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: Se voce baixou o raster nacional MapBiomas Solos Colecao 2 (30 m, classes SiBCS, 2023+), pode cruzar coordenada perfil com o mapa para verificar consistencia: Concordancia entre o classificador e o mapa nacional aumenta confianca. Discordancia (ex.: classificador diz Argissolo, mapa diz Latossolo) e um sinal de revisao – o pedologo decide qual fonte e mais confiavel para o caso.
code: sibcs_no_mapa <- soilKey::lookup_mapbiomas_solos(   coords      = c(perfil$site$lon, perfil$site$lat),   raster_path = "soil_data/mapbiomas/mapbiomas_solos_30m_2023.tif",   legend      = data.frame(     value      = c(3),     class_name = c("Argissolo Vermelho-Amarelo")   ) ) sibcs_no_mapa #> [1] "Argissolo Vermelho-Amarelo"

title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: Para qualquer coordenada mundo, lookup_soilgrids() (v0.9.48) le direto endpoint COG da ISRIC sem download:
code: ph_topsoil <- soilKey::lookup_soilgrids(   coords   = c(perfil$site$lon, perfil$site$lat),   property = "phh2o",  depth = "0-5cm", quantile = "mean" ) clay_subsoil <- soilKey::lookup_soilgrids(   coords   = c(perfil$site$lon, perfil$site$lat),   property = "clay",   depth = "30-60cm", quantile = "mean" ) cat("SoilGrids pH (0-5cm):", ph_topsoil,     " | clay subsoil (30-60cm):", clay_subsoil, "%\n")

title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: Esse e o caminho reproduzivel que o pacote oferece: o pedologo chega com horizontes + cores Munsell + analises de laboratorio, roda um comando (classify_all()), e recebe os tres rotulos junto com o trace explicando exatamente quais diagnosticos dispararam em cada sistema. Em ~10 linhas de R.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Automated soil profile classification WRB 2022 (4th ed.), USDA Soil Taxonomy (13th ed.), Brazilian SiBCS (5ª edição). three systems wired end--end deepest categorical level. Multimodal extraction, spatial priors, OSSL spectroscopy explicit per-attribute provenance — without ever delegating taxonomic key language model.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: canonical Brazilian Latossolo Vermelho Distrocoeso Mata Atlântica gneiss, classified end--end across three canonical systems deepest level: WRB delivers complete Chapter 6 name — four principal qualifiers + five supplementary qualifiers canonical order. SiBCS descends four hierarchical levels (Ordem → Subordem → Grande Grupo → Subgrupo) plus 5th-level Família 15 orthogonal adjectival dimensions (Família label includes adjectives sufficient evidence; richer profiles produce longer labels). USDA Soil Taxonomy walks complete Path C (Order → Suborder → Great Group → Subgroup) per Keys Soil Taxonomy 13th ed. three keys deterministic R code driven versioned YAML rules.
code: library(soilKey)  pedon <- make_ferralsol_canonical()  # WRB 2022: full Chapter 6 name (RSG + qualifiers + specifiers) classify_wrb2022(pedon)$name #> [1] "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)"  # SiBCS 5a ed.: 4o nivel (Subgrupo) + Familia (5o nivel) classify_sibcs(pedon, include_familia = TRUE)$name #> [1] "Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos, argilosa, moderado"  # USDA Soil Taxonomy 13ed: Order -> Suborder -> Great Group -> Subgroup classify_usda(pedon)$name #> [1] "Rhodic Hapludox"

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: v0.9.24 → v0.9.27 release series progressively closed key reasoning gaps USDA Soil Taxonomy 13ed validated gains three real-data benchmarks (KSSL+NASIS, FEBR/Embrapa, WoSIS): v0.9.24 — Path C subgroup tightening (aquic_conditions_usda + oxyaquic_subgroup_usda): predicates now require dual evidence (reduction redoximorphic indicator) instead single-trigger disjunctive , producing false-positive Aquic / Oxyaquic subgroup predictions Typic-reference profiles. New benchmark levels added: level = "great_group" level = "suborder" benchmark_run_classification(), completing four-level USDA hierarchy. v0.9.25 — KST 13ed Great Group canonicaliser (canonicalise_kst13ed_gg()): many--one map collapsing pre-KST 13ed obsolete Great Group names modern split children shared canonical key. Pellusterts ↔︎ Hapluderts; Haplaquolls ↔︎ Endo/Epi-Aquolls; Camborthids ↔︎ Haplocambids; Vitrandepts ↔︎ Vitrudands; Dystrochrepts ↔︎ Dystrudepts; Medisaprists ↔︎ Haplosaprists. KSSL’s samp_taxgrtgroup mixes labels Soil Taxonomy editions 8 13; fix delivered +3.84 pp Great Group KSSL+NASIS without classifier change. v0.9.26 — Per-system argic clay-increase threshold API: test_clay_increase_argic(h, system = c("wrb2022", "usda")) argic(pedon, system = ...) now route either stricter WRB 2022 thresholds (6 pp / 1.4× / 20 pp) looser KST 13ed thresholds (3 pp / 1.2× / 8 pp). Default remains WRB back-compat. v0.9.27 — Clay-illuviation evidence test (argillic_clay_films_test()) reads two complementary NASIS-derived slots: (1) pediagfeatures.featkind argillic-horizon entries (~13 500 2021 NASIS snapshot), (2) per-horizon clay_films_amount derived phpvsf. argillic_usda() now uses two-tier strategy: KST 13ed thresholds clay-films evidence present, WRB 2022 thresholds (proxy) . Plus Embrapa FEBR benchmark fix (label normalisation) yielding +16.1 pp Order v0.9.22 → v0.9.27, WoSIS GraphQL retry+fallback ISRIC server intermittency. full /B trajectory across releases inst/benchmarks/reports/. See NEWS.md per-release diff.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: public, mantained, end--end implementation three major soil classification systems. WRB acknowledges (4th-edition preface) internal classification algorithms exist within IUSS Working Group released. U.S. SoilTaxonomy package CRAN provides lookup tables key. zero public software SiBCS. soilKey closes gap three principles: taxonomic key never delegated language model. LLMs restricted schema-validated extraction. Every classification deterministic walk versioned YAML rules full decision trace. Every value carries provenance tag. measured · predicted_spectra · extracted_vlm · inferred_prior · user_assumed. result’s evidence grade (–D) summarises log callers always know robust classification . Side modules never overrule key. Spatial priors flag inconsistencies silently change assigned RSG; spectral predictions fill missing attributes explicit confidence; multimodal extraction pulls structured data without writing class names.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Module 1 (key) Module 4 (spectra) independent. profile spectra still classifies; profile full lab data still benefits spatial-prior consistency check.
code:
flowchart TB subgraph M2["Module 2 — Multimodal extraction"] A[PDF · Field report] --> V(VLM via ellmer) B[Profile photo] --> V C[Field sheet] --> V V --> J["JSON-Schema
validation + retry"] end subgraph M4["Module 4 — Spectra"] K[Vis-NIR / SWIR / MIR] --> O("OSSL prediction
MBL · PLSR-local · pretrained") O --> P["PI95 → confidence"] end subgraph M3["Module 3 — Spatial prior"] S[SoilGrids WCS] --> R(("P(RSG)")) EM[Embrapa map] --> R end J --> PR["PedonRecord
(provenance log)"] P --> PR PR --> M1["Module 1 — Taxonomic keys"] M1 --> W["WRB 2022 key
32 RSGs · Ch 4–6 (qualifiers + specifiers)"] M1 --> SC["SiBCS 5 key
13 ordens · 44 subordens · 192 GG · 938 SG · Família"] M1 --> U["USDA ST 13
12 Orders · 68 Suborders · 339 GG · 1288 SG"] W --> CR["ClassificationResult
name · trace · evidence grade"] SC --> CR U --> CR R -.consistency check.-> CR

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Single-CPU wall-clock timing 44 canonical fixtures, mean 10 iterations: USDA ~10x slower WRB / SiBCS Path C (Order → Suborder → Great Group → Subgroup) walks full subgroup tier (~85% runtime). 4-level multi-tier benchmark KSSL+NASIS n=2 638 takes ~14 min wall-clock; 1 000-pedon classify-runs ~5 minutes. See inst/benchmarks/reports/perf_v0935_2026-05-03.md full timing.

what: 1. Build a PedonRecord from horizon data
title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
code: library(soilKey)  pr <- PedonRecord$new(   site = list(     id              = "BR-LV-001",     lat             = -22.5, lon = -43.7,     country         = "BR",     parent_material = "gneiss"   ),   horizons = data.frame(     top_cm    = c(0,    15,   35,   65,   130),     bottom_cm = c(15,   35,   65,   130,  200),     designation        = c("A",  "AB", "BA", "Bw1","Bw2"),     munsell_hue_moist  = rep("2.5YR", 5),     munsell_value_moist  = c(3, 3, 3, 4, 4),     munsell_chroma_moist = c(4, 4, 6, 6, 6),     clay_pct = c(50, 52, 55, 60, 60),     silt_pct = c(15, 14, 10,  8,  8),     sand_pct = c(35, 34, 35, 32, 32),     cec_cmol = c(8, 6.5, 5.5, 5.0, 4.8),     bs_pct   = c(24, 17, 14, 13, 13),     al_cmol  = c(0.7, 0.8, 0.6, 0.5, 0.5),     ph_h2o   = c(4.8, 4.7, 4.7, 4.8, 4.9),     ph_kcl   = c(4.0, 4.0, 4.0, 4.1, 4.2),     oc_pct   = c(2.0, 1.2, 0.6, 0.3, 0.2)   ) )

what: 2. Classify across three systems in one pass
title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
code: # WRB 2022 -- full Chapter 6 name classify_wrb2022(pr)$name #> [1] "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)"  # SiBCS 5a ed. -- 4o nivel categorico (Subgrupo) + Familia (5o nivel) classify_sibcs(pr, include_familia = TRUE)$name #> [1] "Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos, argilosa, moderado"  # USDA Soil Taxonomy 13ed -- Subgroup classify_usda(pr)$name #> [1] "Rhodic Hapludox"

what: 3. Inspect the trace and evidence grade
title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
code: result <- classify_wrb2022(pr) result$evidence_grade #> [1] "A"  result$qualifiers$principal #> [1] "Geric"   "Ferric"  "Rhodic"  "Chromic"  result$qualifiers$supplementary #> [1] "Clayic"  "Humic"   "Dystric" "Ochric"  "Rubic"  # The key tested 15 RSGs before assigning Ferralsols. result$trace

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
code: # Vis-NIR spectrum per horizon, OSSL backbone: pr$spectra$vnir <- my_spectra_matrix  fill_from_spectra(   pr,   library    = "ossl",   region     = "south_america",   properties = c("clay_pct", "cec_cmol", "bs_pct", "oc_pct"),   method     = "mbl" ) # Now classify_wrb2022(pr)$evidence_grade may be "B" (predicted_spectra) # instead of "A" (measured) — provenance survives.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
code: prior <- spatial_prior_soilgrids(pr, buffer_m = 250) prior_consistency_check(rsg_code = result$rsg_or_order, prior = prior) #> $consistent : TRUE #> $note       : "Ferralsols at probability 0.62 in the SoilGrids buffer"

what: 6. Render a self-contained report (HTML or PDF)
title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: HTML output single self-contained file (inline CSS, external network requests) suitable emailing attaching laudo. system gets card full Ch 6 / Família / Subgroup name, evidence grade, key trace, ambiguities, missing-data hints.
code: # All three results in a single one-pager (HTML, no external deps): report(pr, file = "perfil_042_report.html")  # Or pass an explicit list of results: results <- list(   classify_wrb2022(pr),   classify_sibcs(pr, include_familia = TRUE),   classify_usda(pr) ) report(results, file = "perfil_042_report.html", pedon = pr)  # PDF (requires rmarkdown + LaTeX): report(results, file = "perfil_042_report.pdf", pedon = pr)

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: soilKey ships five benchmark drivers inst/benchmarks/ plus per-loader benchmark functions KSSL/NASIS, FEBR/Embrapa EU-LUCAS:

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: headline real-data benchmark: USDA Soil Taxonomy 13ed every level keyed hierarchy NCSS / Kellogg Soil Survey Laboratory database joined NASIS Morphological sqlite (n=2638 quality filter, 500 bootstrap reps, ±1.7 pp CI). first public USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark resolving every level hierarchy real, published lab data. v0.9.24-v0.9.27 release series progressively closed key reasoning gaps. Apples--apples n=865 development sample (CI ±3 pp): v0.9.25 KST canonicaliser second-biggest single-version Great Group lift project’s history (v0.9.23 argic clay-increase fix bigger). required classifier changes — predictor already correct KST 13ed; comparison just needed handle legacy KSSL labels (span Soil Taxonomy editions 8 13) fairly. See inst/benchmarks/reports/kssl_nasis_v0927_n3000_validation_2026-05-03.md full /B trajectory per-Order breakdown.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Brazilian SiBCS validation FEBR (febr-superconjunto.txt) snapshot, n=554 quality filter: +16.1 pp Order Embrapa across four releases single biggest cumulative real-data lift project. See inst/benchmarks/reports/embrapa_v0927_2026-05-03.md per-Ordem confusion analysis.

what: 4. WoSIS GraphQL (paper-grade WRB baseline, real external data)
title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: 100 South-America profiles pulled WoSIS GraphQL maximal attribute query (24 *Values fields per layer); classified classify_wrb2022(). Top-1 agreement stratified data-coverage tier: 0 / 5 Tier-1 classifier failure. forensic walkthrough every miss (inst/benchmarks/reports/wosis_forensic_2026-04-30.md) shows: 1 / 5: defensible disagreement different WRB edition (WoSIS labelled “Acrisol” using pre-2022 source; soilKey WRB 2022 says Ferralsol data CEC ≪ argic threshold). 1 / 5: indeterminate due missing exchangeable cations WoSIS — trace says missing: bs_pct. Package correctly returns indeterminate rather guessing. 3 / 5: indeterminate due systematic WoSIS schema gap — WoSIS slickensides field . soilKey assigns next--defensible RSG WRB Ch 4 chave order. WoSIS target informed field morphology database archive. Genuine classifier failures: 0 / 5. apparent 0 % top-1 data-availability statement WoSIS, correctness statement soilKey. v0.9.27 added per-page retry exponential backoff (1 s, 2 s, 4 s, 8 s) plus graceful degradation read_wosis_profiles_graphql() — first page succeeds, transient page failures return partial pull rather aborting. addresses ISRIC GraphQL endpoint’s intermittent “canceling statement due statement timeout” responses observed v0.9.24 refresh.

what: ✦ Two user-facing helpers that guide classification
title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: soilKey ships two ways user get prior expectation soil class running deterministic key — canonical “’m field, expect?” use case.

previous_headings: ✦ Two user-facing helpers that guide classification
title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Given coordinates, returns ranked list likely RSGs / SiBCS ordens / USDA orders location plus canonical attribute thresholds distinguish . Backed SoilGrids 2.0 (WRB-coded raster user provides) WRB ↔︎ SiBCS Schad (2023) Annex Table 1 correspondence. classify profile. tells pedologist arriving field expect data prioritise.
code: library(soilKey)  # Mata Atlântica (Seropédica RJ). res <- soil_classes_at_location(   lat        = -22.7,   lon        = -43.7,   system     = "wrb2022",   source_url = "https://files.isric.org/soilgrids/latest/data/wrb/MostProbable.vrt" ) res$distribution        # ranked list of likely RSGs with P(RSG | location) res$typical_attributes  # canonical thresholds per RSG -- "what to confirm"

previous_headings: ✦ Two user-facing helpers that guide classification
title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Given Vis-NIR (MIR) spectrum OSSL library enriched WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels, returns K spectrally similar profiles plus probabilistic class prediction. Optional region filter (lat / lon / radius_km) keeps analogy biome-aware: Cerrado profile shouldn’t analogised Boreal taiga. Distance computed PLS-score space resemble installed (matching OSSL reference workflow, Ramirez-Lopez et al. 2013), PCA fallback otherwise. guides, classifiers. populate priors deterministic key runs. architectural invariant — “key never delegated model” — still holds: canonical assignment still comes classify_wrb2022() / classify_sibcs() / classify_usda() consuming fully populated PedonRecord.
code: data(ossl_demo_sa) ossl_demo_sa$Yr$wrb_rsg <- c(...)  # real OSSL ships labels; demo lacks them  res <- classify_by_spectral_neighbours(   spectrum     = my_vnir_spectrum,   ossl_library = ossl_demo_sa,   k            = 25,   region       = list(lat = -22.7, lon = -43.7, radius_km = 500) ) res$distribution    # ranked classes with P(class | spectral neighbours) res$neighbours      # the 25 closest profiles + their distances + labels

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: soilKey separates extraction (multimodal LLM) classification (deterministic R code driven versioned YAML rules). VLM never classifies; every value extracts carries source = "extracted_vlm" deterministic key consumes PedonRecord unaware value got . default local stack uses Gemma 4 via Ollama (gemma4:e4b, ~3 GB, multimodal text+image+audio). Cloud providers (anthropic / openai / google) remain one argument away. full canonical pipeline – extract PDF + extract Munsell photo + classify three systems + render report – one function call: Switch model / provider one argument: MockVLMProvider (offline, schema-validated) documented v04_vlm_extraction.Rmd tests CI runs.
code: library(soilKey)  # One-liner. Local-first; no API key needed; data never leaves your machine. res <- classify_from_documents(   pdf      = "perfil_042_descricao.pdf",   image    = "perfil_042_parede.jpg",   report   = "perfil_042.html"          # optional self-contained HTML output )  res$classifications$wrb$name #> "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)"  res$classifications$sibcs$name #> "Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos, argilosa, moderado"  res$classifications$usda$name #> "Rhodic Hapludox" classify_from_documents(pdf = "...", provider = "ollama",   model = "gemma4:31b") classify_from_documents(pdf = "...", provider = "anthropic")  # claude-sonnet-4-7 classify_from_documents(pdf = "...", provider = "openai")     # gpt-4o classify_from_documents(pdf = "...", provider = "google")     # gemini-2.0-pro

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Every value used key recorded pedon$provenance : attribute : column name source : measured / predicted_spectra / extracted_vlm / inferred_prior / user_assumed confidence : [0, 1] (NA measured) notes : free-form (often source quote) ClassificationResult$evidence_grade worst-source rule applied attributes actually decisive classification — Ferralsol classified entirely lab data yields ; profile one critical clay value predicted spectra yields B. distinguishing feature soilKey versus hypothetical LLM-driven classifier. Provenance survives entire pipeline; output never produced every input lab-measured.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Education without ethics rhetoric. Power without reflection violence. Made ❤️ Hugo Rodrigues Soil Science      Status: CRAN-ready, v0.9.27 (2026-05-03). R CMD check returns Status: OK — 0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes. Test suite 2 908 passing / 0 failing / 10 expected skips. GitHub Actions green across 5 OS×R matrix. three classification systems wired end--end deepest categorical level — WRB 2022 (32 RSGs + qualifiers + supplementary + specifiers), SiBCS 5ª ed. (Ordem → Subordem → Grande Grupo → Subgrupo → Família, ~1 200 classes), USDA Soil Taxonomy 13ed (Order → Suborder → Great Group → Subgroup, ~1 700 classes). v0.9.27 highlights: clay-illuviation evidence test (NASIS pediagfeatures argillic flag + per-horizon clay_films_amount phpvsf); per-system argic clay-increase threshold API (WRB 6/1.4/20 vs KST 13ed 3/1.2/8); FEBR / Embrapa benchmark normalisation fix (Order +16.1 pp v0.9.22 → v0.9.27 = 56.7 %); WoSIS GraphQL retry+fallback ISRIC server intermittency. v0.9.25 shipped KST 13ed Great Group canonicaliser collapsing pre-13ed legacy KSSL labels (Pellusterts → Hapluderts; Haplaquolls → Endo/Epi-Aquolls; Camborthids → Haplocambids; etc.) — KSSL+NASIS Great Group +3.84 pp single release. Headline real-data benchmark large scale (n=2 638, ±1.7 pp CI): Order 34.2 %, Suborder 13.9 %, Great Group 7.9 %, Subgroup 4.2 % (first public USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark resolving every level hierarchy real lab data). DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.19930112 (resolves latest version Zenodo). Per-release changes NEWS.md; roadmap ARCHITECTURE.md §12; CRAN submission instructions inst/cran-submission/HOW_TO_SUBMIT.md.

what: Horizonte B latossolico (SiBCS Cap 2, p 57-59; v0.7 strict) — B_latossolico
title: Horizonte B latossolico (SiBCS Cap 2, p 57-59; v0.7 strict) — B_latossolico
text: Adicionalmente ferralic (WRB), o B latossolico SiBCS exige: Espessura minima de 50 cm; Textura francoarenosa ou mais fina; Estrutura granular muito pequena/pequena ou em blocos         subangulares fraco/moderado; < 5% volume mostrando estrutura da rocha original; Ki \<= 2.2 (geralmente \<= 2.0); Cerosidade maximo pouca e fraca. v0.7 enforce thickness, texture, e ausencia de estrutura primaria herdada via designation e clay; Ki/Kr quantitativos sao v0.8 (precisa de SiO2/Al2O3 lab-data nao schema).

title: ClassificationResult: structured outcome of running a key — ClassificationResult
text: Returned classify_wrb2022 (future classify_sibcs). Carries full decision trace — RSGs tested, passed, failed, indeterminate missing data — plus assigned class, qualifiers, ambiguities (RSGs nearly satisfied), missing data refine result, provenance-aware evidence grade, biogeographical prior-based warnings.

title: ClassificationResult: structured outcome of running a key — ClassificationResult
text: system Character. "WRB 2022" "SiBCS 5". name Character. Full classification name qualifiers (e.g. "Rhodic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric)"). rsg_or_order Character. Bare RSG (WRB) order (SiBCS), e.g. "Ferralsols". qualifiers List. Principal supplementary qualifiers canonical order. trace List. One element per RSG tested (key order), code, name, passed, evidence, missing. ambiguities List. RSGs came close passing — useful hints follow-measurements. missing_data Character vector. Attributes whose measurement refine resolve result. evidence_grade Character. "", "B", "C", "D", NA_character_. prior_check List NULL. Result spatial-prior sanity check (consistent / inconsistent / run). warnings Character vector. Free-form warnings.

title: DiagnosticResult: structured outcome of a diagnostic test — DiagnosticResult
text: Returned every WRB SiBCS diagnostic function (e.g. argic, ferralic, mollic). DiagnosticResult never reduces bare TRUE/FALSE — always carries () layers satisfied criteria, (b) per-sub-test evidence, (c) attributes required missing, (d) literature reference diagnostic definition. passed TRUE/FALSE/NA; NA means test evaluated critical attributes missing. three-valued semantics propagates rule engine — indeterminate test silently fail.

title: DiagnosticResult: structured outcome of a diagnostic test — DiagnosticResult
text: name Character. Name diagnostic (e.g. "argic"). passed Logical. TRUE, FALSE, NA. layers Integer vector. Indices horizons satisfy diagnostic. evidence Named list. Sub-test results, list least passed, layers, missing. missing Character vector. Attribute names needed NA. reference Character. Literature citation diagnostic. notes Character. Free-form notes (interpretation choices, edge cases hit).

what: Mock VLM provider for testing — MockVLMProvider
title: Mock VLM provider for testing — MockVLMProvider
text: Mock VLM provider testing Mock VLM provider testing

title: Mock VLM provider for testing — MockVLMProvider
text: stand-ellmer chat object. Exposes $chat(prompt, ...) method, instead calling model pops next response pre-loaded queue. Designed testthat unit tests exercise extraction logic without API keys network access. call $chat() returns next element responses list. call number matches validation_error_at, response replaced deliberately malformed JSON string, allowing tests exercise retry--validation-failure path implemented validate_or_retry.

title: Mock VLM provider for testing — MockVLMProvider
code: good_json <- '{"horizons": [...]}' mock <- MockVLMProvider$new(responses = list(good_json)) result <- mock$chat("any prompt")  # returns good_json  # Simulate one validation error before success. mock <- MockVLMProvider$new(   responses = list("not really json", good_json),   validation_error_at = NULL  # already invalid as-is )  # Or force an attempt to be invalid via the helper. mock <- MockVLMProvider$new(   responses = list(good_json, good_json),   validation_error_at = 1L )

title: Mock VLM provider for testing — MockVLMProvider
text: use, mock exposes $call_count (integer) $prompts_received (list every prompt passed $chat()), lets tests assert retry prompts include previous validation error.

title: PedonRecord: structured representation of a single pedon — PedonRecord
text: PedonRecord: structured representation single pedon PedonRecord: structured representation single pedon

title: PedonRecord: structured representation of a single pedon — PedonRecord
text: central data carrier soilKey. PedonRecord bundles everything know one soil profile: site metadata, horizons table (fixed canonical schema — see horizon_column_spec), optional Vis-NIR/MIR spectra, profile photographs, source documents, provenance log records, per (horizon, attribute) pair, value came (measured, extracted_vlm, predicted_spectra, inferred_prior, user_assumed). diagnostic functions (argic, ferralic, mollic, ...) consume PedonRecord directly. provenance log allows final ClassificationResult assign meaningful evidence grade.

title: PedonRecord: structured representation of a single pedon — PedonRecord
text: site List. Site-level metadata: lat, lon, crs (default 4326), date, country, elevation_m, slope_pct, aspect_deg, landform, parent_material, land_use, vegetation, drainage_class, plus arbitrary id. horizons data.table canonical horizon schema. spectra List optional vnir matrix (rows = horizons, cols = wavelengths nm), mir matrix, metadata list. images List named lists describing profile photographs. documents List named lists describing source documents. provenance data.table columns horizon_idx, attribute, source, confidence, notes.

title: PedonRecord: structured representation of a single pedon — PedonRecord
text: Validate record soil-physical sanity rules. Checks: top < bottom every horizon; overlapping depths; clay+silt+sand sum 100 ± 2 three reported; pH values plausible (1..12); CEC >= sum exchangeable bases (Ca, Mg, K, Na); Munsell value/chroma plausible ranges; coarse fragments percent [0, 100]; OC geographic ranges. Returns list valid, errors, warnings, n_horizons.

text: Tests andic property complex – volcanic-ash-derived allophanic / imogolitic / Al-humus material. Diagnostic Andosols. Two alternative qualifying paths per WRB 2022 Ch 3.2: Al-Fe oxalate + low BD:         (Al_ox + 0.5*Fe_ox) >= min_alfe (default 2.0%)         bulk_density <= max_bd (default 0.9 g/cm^3)         layer. Phosphate retention: phosphate_retention_pct         >= min_p_retention (default 70%). Either path qualifies. volcanic-glass criterion separate vitric_properties diagnostic; Andosols key (andic vitric) RSG-gate level (andosol).

title: Andic properties (WRB 2022) — andic_properties
code: andic_properties(   pedon,   min_alfe = 2,   max_bd = 0.9,   min_p_retention = 70,   min_oc_proxy = 4,   max_bd_proxy = 0.9 )

title: Andic properties (WRB 2022) — andic_properties
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_alfe Minimum (Al_ox + 0.5*Fe_ox) percent Al-Fe path (default 2.0). max_bd Maximum bulk density g/cm^3 Al-Fe path (default 0.9). min_p_retention Minimum phosphate retention % P path (default 70). min_oc_proxy Minimum SOC % v0.9.80 OC+BD proxy path (default 4.0). consulted proxy enabled via options(soilKey.andic_oc_bd_proxy = TRUE). max_bd_proxy Maximum bulk density g/cm^3 v0.9.80 OC+BD proxy path (default 0.9). consulted proxy enabled.

title: Andic properties (WRB 2022) — andic_properties
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Andic properties (WRB 2022) — andic_properties
text: Field-described volcanic-ash soils (e.g.\ AfSP, KSSL/NASIS, SOTER) routinely lack oxalate Al/Fe phosphate retention measurements, canonical paths return NA Andosols cascade RSGs. genetic signature still detectable coarser data: high SOC (>= 4-5%) plus low bulk density (<= 0.9 g/cm^3) typical allophanic / Al-humus complexation. options(soilKey.andic_oc_bd_proxy = TRUE) function adds third path fires canonical paths fail surface horizon shows oc_pct >= min_oc_proxy bulk_density_g_cm3 <= max_bd_proxy (OC alone >= 5% BD missing). Default FALSE (canonical behaviour preserved).

title: Andic properties (WRB 2022) — andic_properties
text: options(soilKey.andic_oc_bd_proxy_extend = TRUE) (meaningful soilKey.andic_oc_bd_proxy = TRUE), iteratively extend proxy layers include contiguous deeper layers whose oc_pct >= min_oc_proxy / 2 whose bulk_density_g_cm3 missing <= max_bd_proxy + 0.15. extension stops first horizon failing either constraint, ferralic / argic subsoil accidentally inflate andic thickness. Default FALSE – canonical proxy behaviour preserved.

title: Andosol RSG gate (WRB 2022 Ch 4, p 104) — andosol
text: WRB 2022 Ch 4 p 104 specifies Andosol exclusion list (argic / ferralic / petroplinthic / pisoplinthic / plinthic / spodic) "<= 100 cm unless buried 50 cm". earlier implementation excluded Andosol whenever diagnostics passed anywhere profile, including layers starting deeper 50 cm – mis-fires AfSP Andosol references like CM W3_0047, argic layer 56-72 cm wrongly excluded andic surface stack. v0.9.85 restricts exclusion check layers starting <= 50 cm: buried argic / ferralic / plinthic / spodic deeper levels longer disqualifies surface andic stack Andosol.

title: Argic horizon (WRB 2022) — argic
text: Tests whether horizon meets argic horizon criteria per Chapter 3 WRB 2022 (4th edition). Argic subsurface horizon distinctly higher clay content overlying horizon, qualified three depth-conditional clay-increase rules; must also texture sandy loam finer, satisfy minimum thickness, exhibit albeluvic glossic features (direct profile Retisol path).

title: Argic horizon (WRB 2022) — argic
code: argic(   pedon,   min_thickness = 7.5,   system = c("wrb2022", "usda"),   engine = NULL,   require_t = NULL )

title: Argic horizon (WRB 2022) — argic
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_thickness Minimum thickness cm (default 7.5). system One "wrb2022" (default) "usda". Selects clay-increase threshold set: WRB uses 6/1.4/20 pp/ratio/pp; KST 13ed uses 3/1.2/8 (looser). See test_clay_increase_argic table. engine v0.9.63+. One "soilkey" (hand-coded path, default back-compat) "aqp" (canonical NRCS dispatch via aqp::getArgillicBounds). NULL (new default) function reads getOption("soilKey.diagnostic_engine", "soilkey") global options(soilKey.diagnostic_engine = "aqp") flips every argic() call without modifying call sites. See argic_aqp. require_t v0.9.63+. Forwarded aqp::getArgillicBounds engine = "aqp": TRUE requires "t" suffix horizon designation (strict KST 13ed text); FALSE accepts argic clay-increase alone (permissive data-sparse profiles). NULL (default) auto-picks: TRUE system = "usda", FALSE system = "wrb2022". Ignored engine = "soilkey".

title: Argic horizon (WRB 2022) — argic
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Argic horizon (WRB 2022) — argic
text: Sub-tests called (list passed, layers, missing, details, notes): test_clay_increase_argic – three-pronged         WRB 2022 clay-increase rule. test_minimum_thickness – thickness >= 7.5 cm         (configurable via min_thickness). test_texture_argic – texture sandy loam         finer (silt + 2 * clay >= 30). test_not_albeluvic – excludes profiles glossic         tongues (Retisol path). v0.1 limitations: clay-increase distance (<= 30 cm vertical, <= 15 cm abrupt textural change) yet enforced; scheduled v0.2 depends horizon boundary descriptions.

title: Argic horizon (WRB 2022) — argic
text: IUSS Working Group WRB (2022). World Reference Base Soil Resources, 4th edition. International Union Soil Sciences, Vienna. Chapter 3 – Argic horizon.

what: Test for clay-illuviation evidence (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 4) — argillic_clay_films_test
title: Test for clay-illuviation evidence (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 4) — argillic_clay_films_test
text: KST 13ed argillic horizon requires "evidence illuvial accumulation clay" alongside clay-increase rule. Acceptable evidence: oriented clays bridging sand grains >= 1% horizon; clay films lining pores coating ped faces; lamellae 5 mm thick.

title: Test for clay-illuviation evidence (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 4) — argillic_clay_films_test
Test for clay-illuviation evidence (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 4) — argillic_clay_films_test
text: pedon PedonRecord.

title: Test for clay-illuviation evidence (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 4) — argillic_clay_films_test
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Test for clay-illuviation evidence (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 4) — argillic_clay_films_test
text: test reads three complementary slots, order evidence strength: pedon$site$nasis_diagnostic_features – NASIS         pediagfeatures.featkind vector. surveyor's         explicit "Argillic horizon" entry directly confirms         clay-illuviation evidence (~13 500 entries 2021         NASIS snapshot). Strongest evidence. pedon$horizons$clay_films_amount – per-horizon         clay-film abundance derived NASIS phpvsf.         Values: "", "common", "many",         "continuous". Direct measurement. pedon$horizons$designation containing 't'         master suffix (e.g. Bt, Btk, Btx,         Bt1, 2Bt). v0.9.28: pedologist         wrote designation explicitly identified         horizon clay-illuvial – per KST 13ed Ch 18, 't'         suffix means "accumulation silicate clay" –         counts positive evidence even NASIS records         absent. unlocks KST 13ed argillic thresholds         ~47          pediagfeatures phpvsf records. three sources counts positive evidence (logical ). passed = NA none populated horizon designation field present (lab-loaders without horizon descriptions). passed = FALSE designations exist none 't' suffix NASIS slots empty.

title: Test for clay-illuviation evidence (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 4) — argillic_clay_films_test
text: Soil Survey Staff (2022), Keys Soil Taxonomy 13th   ed., Ch. 3, argillic horizon (clay-illuviation criteria, p. 4);   Ch. 18, master horizon symbols (t: silicate-clay   accumulation, p. 332).

what: Atividade da fracao argila (SiBCS Cap 1, p 30) — atividade_argila_alta
title: Atividade da fracao argila (SiBCS Cap 1, p 30) — atividade_argila_alta
text: Calcula atividade da fracao argila Ta = CEC * 1000 / argila (em cmolc/kg de argila, sem correcao para carbono) por horizonte e classifica como **alta (Ta)** se >= 27 cmolc/kg argila ou **baixa (Tb)** se < 27. Nao se aplica texturas areia / areia franca.

text: pedons list PedonRecord objects, typically produced load_bdsolos_csv. systems Character vector. subset c("wrb2022", "sibcs", "usda"). Default runs three. sibcs_level One "order" (default) "subordem". Forwarded normalise_febr_sibcs. max_n Optional integer; cap classification first max_n pedons. NULL (default) classifies every pedon. verbose TRUE (default), emits cli progress messages.

title: Benchmark soilKey classifiers against BDsolos national reference labels — benchmark_bdsolos
text: list elements: per_system – named list (one entry per requested           system) list(accuracy, n_compared, n_correct,           n_errors, confusion, per_class) (          list(accuracy = NA_real_, message)           reference labels present). coverage – named list           list(n_with_ref, n_total, pct) per system. config – named list capturing           n_pedons, systems, sibcs_level, soilKey_version,           timestamp.

title: Benchmark soilKey classifiers against BDsolos national reference labels — benchmark_bdsolos
text: BDsolos densely populates reference_sibcs (~82 v0.9.59 audit) sparsely populates reference_wrb reference_st (UF-dependent; ~5 states). function always reports per-system label coverage ($coverage) caller can judge representative accuracy figure .

title: Benchmark soilKey classifiers against BDsolos national reference labels — benchmark_bdsolos
text: SiBCS comparison level = "order" default, converts BDsolos -caps Portuguese label (e.g. "ARGISSOLO VERMELHO Tb EUTROFICO ...") soilKey plural Title Case form ("Argissolos") via normalise_febr_sibcs. Set sibcs_level = "subordem" compare first two SiBCS tokens (Ordem + Subordem). WRB USDA comparisons Reference Soil Group / Order level: normalise_febr_wrb() strips qualifier parens pluralises bare RSG ("Xanthic Ferralsol" -> "Ferralsols"); normalise_febr_usda() maps suffix last subgroup token USDA Order ("Typic Haplorthox" -> "Oxisols").

title: Benchmark soilKey classifiers against BDsolos national reference labels — benchmark_bdsolos
text: Pedons without reference label given system silently excluded system's comparison (still classified two systems). system zero pedons reference label, corresponding $per_system entry accuracy = NA_real_ message = "no_reference_labels". Classifier errors caught per-pedon recorded n_errors; abort run.

title: Run the LUCAS Soil 2018 / ESDB WRB benchmark — benchmark_lucas_2018
code: benchmark_lucas_2018(   pedons,   esdb_root,   attribute = "WRBLV1",   fill_texture_from = NULL,   fill_topsoil_from = c("none", "soilgrids", "spectra"),   fill_subsoil_from = c("none", "soilgrids"),   fill_properties = c("clay", "sand", "silt", "phh2o", "soc", "cec", "bdod", "nitrogen",     "cfvo"),   ossl_models = NULL,   classify_with = c("wrb2022", "sibcs"),   max_n = NULL,   soilgrids_lookup_fn = lookup_soilgrids,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Run the LUCAS Soil 2018 / ESDB WRB benchmark — benchmark_lucas_2018
text: pedons List PedonRecord objects, typically load_lucas_soil_2018. esdb_root Path unpacked ESDB raster directory (containing WRBLV1/ sub-folder). attribute ESDB attribute use reference. Default "WRBLV1" (Reference Soil Group, 31 codes). sensible choices: "FAO90LV1" (legacy FAO 1990). fill_texture_from Deprecated alias fill_topsoil_from (v0.9.49 signature). "soilgrids", treated fill_topsoil_from = "soilgrids" fill_properties = c("clay", "sand", "silt") fill_subsoil_from = "none". fill_topsoil_from One "none" (default), "soilgrids" (fills topsoil 0-20 cm SoilGrids 250m 0-5 cm), "spectra" (runs predict_from_spectra supplied ossl_models; pedons must $spectra$vnir attached, e.g. via attach_lucas_spectra). fill_subsoil_from One "none" (default) "soilgrids" (synthesises 30-60 cm B horizon SoilGrids 250m). Unlocks WRB diagnostic horizons depend subsoil features (cambic, argic, mollic). fill_properties Character vector SoilGrids properties fill fill_topsoil_from = "soilgrids" fill_subsoil_from = "soilgrids". Default uses 9 properties: clay, sand, silt, phh2o, soc, cec, bdod, nitrogen, cfvo. Set c("clay", "sand", "silt") recover v0.9.49 behaviour. cfvo mapped coarse_fragments_pct, drives Leptosols diagnostic (>= 90 within 25 cm). ossl_models Required fill_topsoil_from = "spectra". list soilKey_pls_model objects train_pls_from_ossl (v0.9.46). classify_with One "wrb2022" (default) "sibcs". max_n Optional integer cap number pedons benchmarked. Useful quick development runs. soilgrids_lookup_fn Internal: SoilGrids lookup function (defaults lookup_soilgrids). Override unit tests inject deterministic stub. verbose TRUE (default), prints progress.

title: Run a benchmark across one of the loaded pedon lists — benchmark_run_classification
text: pedons List PedonRecord objects (output one load_* functions). system One "wrb2022", "sibcs", "usda". level Granularity comparison: "order" (default) – top-level RSG / Ordem /         Order, compared cls$rsg_or_order; "subgroup" – full classified name (Subgroup         USDA, Subgrupo SiBCS, RSG + qualifiers WRB),         compared cls$name case-insensitive         token normalisation; "subordem" – SiBCS-, 2nd-level         "Ordem + Subordem" (e.g. "Latossolos Vermelhos").         Comparison via first two normalised tokens         predicted name vs reference; "great_group" (USDA, v0.9.24) – LAST token         subgroup name (e.g. "typic hapludalfs" ->         "hapludalfs"). Isolates whether Great Group         machinery correct independent subgroup modifiers         (Typic / Aquic / Vertic / Cumulic / Pachic / etc.).         Reads site$reference_usda_grtgroup; "suborder" (USDA, v0.9.24) – maps Great         Group prediction canonical Suborder suffix         ("hapludalfs" -> "udalfs") using         KST 13ed Ch 4 ~70-Suborder list. Reads         site$reference_usda_suborder. boot_n Bootstrap replicates CI (default 1000).

title: Run a benchmark across one of the loaded pedon lists — benchmark_run_classification
text: list elements accuracy_top1,         accuracy_ci, confusion,         per_pedon (one row per pedon predicted vs         reference).
