title: Melanic epipedon (USDA Soil Taxonomy, 13th edition) — melanic_epipedon_usda
text: KST 13ed required characteristics (Ch. 3, pp 15-16): Upper boundary within 30 cm mineral soil         surface (organic layer andic properties); Cumulative thickness >= 30 cm within 40 cm : Andic soil properties throughout; Color value <= 2.5 moist chroma <= 2 throughout; Melanic index <= 1.70 (deferred – specialized lab             measurement); OC >= 6 percent (weighted) >= 4 percent (            layer). Implementation notes (v0.8.x): Andic soil properties tested via andic_properties_usda         (v0.9; v0.8 approximate bulk_density <=         0.9 g/cm3 phosphate_retention >= 85%). Melanic index (= 100 / (OC * 100 + 1) per KST appendix)         deferred – requires UV-Vis spectroscopy.

title: Mollic horizon (WRB 2022) — mollic
text: Tests whether near-surface horizon meets mollic horizon criteria. mollic horizon diagnostic surface horizon Chernozems, Phaeozems, Kastanozems, several RSGs; indicates thick, dark, base-rich, organic-matter-enriched topsoil formed steppe comparable vegetation.

title: Mollic horizon (WRB 2022) — mollic
code: mollic(   pedon,   min_thickness = 20,   min_oc = 0.6,   min_bs = 50,   surface_top_cm = 5 )

title: Mollic horizon (WRB 2022) — mollic
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_thickness Minimum thickness cm (default 20). min_oc Minimum SOC % (default 0.6). min_bs Minimum base saturation % (default 50). surface_top_cm Maximum top depth (cm) horizon considered "surface-related" (default 5). v0.1 uses proxy WRB rule mollic must form continuously soil surface (mixing upper 20 cm required).

title: Mollic horizon (WRB 2022) — mollic
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Mollic horizon (WRB 2022) — mollic
text: Sub-tests called: test_mollic_color – moist value <= 3, moist         chroma <= 3, dry value <= 5. test_mollic_organic_carbon – SOC >= 0.6%. test_mollic_base_saturation – BS (NH4OAc, pH 7)         >= 50%. test_mollic_thickness – horizon thickness >= 20 cm. test_mollic_structure – simultaneously         massive hard dry. v0.1 limitations: cumulative thickness across contiguous mollic- qualifying horizons yet supported – matters profiles mollic criteria met A1+A2 sequence single horizon >= 20 cm thick. Mixing upper 20 cm test (per WRB) also deferred v0.2.

title: Mollic horizon (WRB 2022) — mollic
text: IUSS Working Group WRB (2022). World Reference Base Soil Resources, 4th edition. International Union Soil Sciences, Vienna. Chapter 3 – Mollic horizon.

title: Mollic epipedon (USDA Soil Taxonomy, 13th edition) — mollic_epipedon_usda
text: KST 13ed required characteristics (Ch. 3, pp 15-17): Color: dominant color value <= 3 (moist) <= 5 (dry)         chroma <= 3 (moist), adjustments CaCO3         content (deferred v0.9); Base saturation (NH4OAc, pH 7) >= 50 percent throughout; Organic carbon >= 0.6 percent (2.5 percent value         4-5 moist; 0.6 absolute > C horizon); Thickness: 18 cm general, 25 cm texture loamy fine         sand coarser, 10 cm directly lithic/densic/         paralithic contact (thin_lithic_overlay branch); Structure: peds <= 30 cm rupture-resistance <=         moderately hard; part moist 90+ days soil temp 50 cm         >= 5 C (deferred – requires climatic data). Implementation notes (v0.8.x): Thickness rule computed dynamically based texture         presence underlying lithic/paralithic contact. N value < 0.7 / fluidity nonfluid assumed (laboratory         tests rarely available); 90-day moisture condition deferred v0.9.

title: Natric horizon (WRB 2022) — natric_horizon
text: Field-described Solonetz profiles NCSS / KSSL data routinely reach natric ESP threshold (computed na_cmol / cec_cmol) without satisfying strict argic() clay-increase test, surveyors record Btk-suffix designations (carbonates dominate horizon designation choice) rather Btn/Bn clay_pct missing. options(soilKey.natric_designation_inference = TRUE) function accepts layer natric canonical argic test returns NA FALSE either: designation matches [-Z][-z0-9]*n (n         master-letter modifier horizon name – e.g.\         Btn, Btnz, Bn, curator's direct         assertion natric features present), ESP >= min_esp B-prefixed subsoil layer         (top_cm > 20) layer's pH(H2O) >= 7         (alkaline – typical true natric, excludes acidic Bt         horizons happen read high Na sea-spray). Default FALSE (canonical behaviour preserved).

text: v0.9.29 adds "implicit lithic contact" heuristic FEBR / BDsolos snapshot, surveyor often documents Neossolos Litolicos simply stopping profile description rock boundary (max profile depth \<= 50 cm horizon explicitly marked R / Cr / Rk B horizon described). Per SiBCS Cap 12 (p 219), Neossolos Litolicos defined lithic contact within 50 cm surface; FEBR, signalled depth deepest described horizon rather explicit pseudo-R record.

title: Neossolos Litolicos (Cap 12): contato litico ou litico fragmentario \<= 50 cm. — neossolo_litolico

text: pedon PedonRecord.

title: Neossolos Litolicos (Cap 12): contato litico ou litico fragmentario \<= 50 cm. — neossolo_litolico
text: heuristic fires : deepest bottom_cm value \<= 50 cm, horizon designation begins B (        accidentally flag shallow Argissolos / Latossolos / etc.         Bt Bw within 50 cm), canonical contato_litico / contato_litico_         fragmentario tests explicitly returned FALSE         (.e. surveyor describe non-rock material         deeper 50 cm). Empirically, heuristic flips ~190 191 FEBR Litolicos "neossolos regoliticos" (catch-) "neossolos litolicos" (correct), cost false-positive Regoliticos happen shallow (FEBR confusion analysis showed ~30 shallow Regoliticos).

title: Nitic horizon (WRB 2022) — nitic_horizon
text: Tests nitic horizon: clay-rich (>= 30%), Fe-rich (DCB Fe >= 4%) subsurface horizon least 30 cm thick. Diagnostic Nitisols. WRB 2022 additionally requires polyhedral / nutty structure shiny ped surfaces gradual (non-abrupt) clay decrease depth.

title: Nitic horizon (WRB 2022) — nitic_horizon
code: nitic_horizon(   pedon,   min_clay = 30,   min_fe_dcb = 4,   min_thickness = 30,   max_clay_drop_pct = 8,   max_decrease_depth = 50 )

title: Nitic horizon (WRB 2022) — nitic_horizon
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_clay Minimum clay % (default 30). min_fe_dcb Minimum DCB-extractable Fe % (default 4). min_thickness Minimum thickness cm (default 30). max_clay_drop_pct Maximum clay drop (percentage points) adjacent layers within max_decrease_depth failing gradual-decrease test (default 8). max_decrease_depth Depth window (cm) gradual-decrease check (default 50).

title: Nitic horizon (WRB 2022) — nitic_horizon
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Nitic horizon (WRB 2022) — nitic_horizon
text: Required (-combined) sub-tests: Profile ferralic horizon (Ferralsol path         canonical clay-rich + low-CEC corner). clay % >= min_clay. fe_dcb_pct >= min_fe_dcb. thickness >= min_thickness. Supplementary (soft-) sub-tests – evaluated evidence present pedon, evaluate NA (fail) missing: structure_type matches polyhedral / nutty / (sub)angular         blocky. slickensides / shiny ped surfaces present (proxy         WRB's "shiny ped surfaces"). clay decrease abruptly adjacent layers         within 50 cm surface (gradual-decrease pattern;         drop > 8 percentage points fails). Supplementary tests fail (return passed = FALSE) evidence actively contradicts criterion; missing evidence permissive.

title: Nitic horizon (WRB 2022) — nitic_horizon
text: IUSS Working Group WRB (2022), Chapter 3, Nitic horizon.

text: v0.9.17 fix: KST 13ed Ch 13 (p 295) excludes Oxisols profile whose argillic horizon's upper boundary lies within 100 cm surface whose argillic base lies within 30 cm upper boundary oxic. Operationally use simpler defensible "argillic oxic" check: argillic exists starts strictly shallower oxic, profile Oxisol (route Ultisols / Alfisols instead). previous v0.8 implementation lacked exclusion responsible misclassifying 144 Embrapa FEBR Ultisols Oxisols v0.9.16 benchmark.

title: Map a 95% prediction interval to a [0, 1] confidence score — pi_to_confidence

title: Map a 95% prediction interval to a [0, 1] confidence score — pi_to_confidence
text: Properties mapping: Zero-width interval -> confidence = 1. Interval whose width equals |value| * 4 -> confidence = 0. NA value NA bounds -> confidence = 0.5 (neutral).

title: Choose the best diagnostic engine for a single pedon — pick_engine
Choose the best diagnostic engine for a single pedon — pick_engine
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_score Integer (1-5). Minimum completeness score "aqp" engine fire (default 3).

title: Choose the best diagnostic engine for a single pedon — pick_engine
text: Character: "aqp" "soilkey".

title: Choose the best diagnostic engine for a single pedon — pick_engine
text: score pedon 0-5 morphology-completeness scale; aqp fires score \>= min_score (default 3). five axes: Designation present (layer non-blank         designation, e.g. "A1", "Bt2", "Bw"). Texture quantitative (layer         clay_pct sand_pct populated). Munsell complete (layer three         munsell_hue_moist, munsell_value_moist,         munsell_chroma_moist populated). Structure recorded (layer non-blank         structure_grade). Clay films / argic evidence (layer         non-blank clay_films_amount designation         pattern matching Bt).

title: Choose the best diagnostic engine for a single pedon — pick_engine
text: BDsolos RJ (data-rich), heuristic recommends aqp ~99 canonical thresholds). LUCAS topsoil-(data-sparse), recommends aqp ~0 clay-films / designation axes unfilled. Calling classify_*(pedon) routed heuristic gives correct engine per pedon, recovering BDsolos RJ lift LUCAS robustness.

what: Plaggic horizon (WRB 2022): sod-derived topsoil >= 20 cm with low BD AND independent evidence of human input. — plaggic
title: Plaggic horizon (WRB 2022): sod-derived topsoil >= 20 cm with low BD AND independent evidence of human input. — plaggic
text: v0.9.2.C tightening: v0.3.3 implementation accepted thick, low-BD, OC-rich horizon, -fired across natural mollic / umbric / chernic surfaces. diagnostic now requires, addition OC + BD + thickness baseline, least one independent anthropogenic-input marker: p_mehlich3_mg_kg >= 50 (sustained sod / manure         additions concentrate Mehlich-3 P topsoil), artefacts_pct > 0 (human artefact volume fraction         sufficient presence signal), designation pattern Apl / Aplg / Apk         / explicit "plagg". Without one markers diagnostic returns FALSE even OC + BD + thickness pass. mirrors v0.9.1 qual_plaggic gate enforces rule diagnostic level caller (SiBCS, USDA, future modules) inherits protection.

Returns hue (e.g. "7.5YR"), value (0..10) chroma (0..20) per sample, plus soilKey fields munsell_hue_moist, munsell_value_moist, munsell_chroma_moist ready write PedonRecord via pedon's add_measurement method (see also fill_munsell_from_spectra).

Must cover least 400-700 nm; values outside 380-780 ignored. round_chip TRUE (default), snaps predicted HVC nearest standard Munsell chip grid via munsellinterpol::roundHVC(). FALSE returns continuous HVC (useful numeric work).

what: Memory-based learning prediction against the OSSL library — predict_ossl_mbl
title: Memory-based learning prediction against the OSSL library — predict_ossl_mbl
text: Predicts set soil properties pre-processed Vis-NIR MIR spectra using memory-based learning (MBL) – recommended OSSL workflow heterogeneous libraries. Defaults follow literature (Ramirez-Lopez et al., 2013): k = 100 neighbours, PLS-score dissimilarity, local PLS regression 5 components, internal leave-one-validation.

title: Memory-based learning prediction against the OSSL library — predict_ossl_mbl
code: predict_ossl_mbl(   X,   properties,   region = "global",   k = 100L,   ossl_library = NULL,   ... )

title: Memory-based learning prediction against the OSSL library — predict_ossl_mbl
text: X pre-processed numeric matrix (rows = horizons, columns = wavelengths). properties Character vector OSSL-supported property names. region One "global", "south_america", "north_america", "europe", "africa". k Integer number neighbours. ossl_library Optional list OSSL training spectra (Xr) reference values (Yr, data.frame keyed properties). NULL, synthetic path used. ... Additional arguments forwarded resemble::mbl.

title: Memory-based learning prediction against the OSSL library — predict_ossl_mbl
text: data.table columns horizon_idx, property,         value, pi95_low, pi95_high, n_neighbors.         "backend" attribute records path taken         ("resemble" "synthetic").

title: Memory-based learning prediction against the OSSL library — predict_ossl_mbl

title: Memory-based learning prediction against the OSSL library — predict_ossl_mbl

what: Pre-process Vis-NIR or MIR spectra — preprocess_spectra
title: Pre-process Vis-NIR or MIR spectra — preprocess_spectra
text: Applies chosen pre-processing pipeline numeric matrix soil spectra. Rows samples (typically horizons) columns wavelengths. Returns numeric matrix; SG-based methods shorten spectrum w - 1 columns edges (default w = 5 two columns dropped side).

title: Pre-process Vis-NIR or MIR spectra — preprocess_spectra
Pre-process Vis-NIR or MIR spectra — preprocess_spectra
text: X Numeric matrix data.frame spectra (rows = samples, columns = wavelengths). Wavelengths evenly spaced. method One "snv", "sg1", "snv+sg1". Default "snv+sg1". w Window size SG filter. Must odd; default 5. p Polynomial order SG filter. Default 2.

title: Pre-process Vis-NIR or MIR spectra — preprocess_spectra
text: numeric matrix. Column names (wavelengths) preserved         possible; SG trimming drops (w - 1) / 2         columns edge.

title: Pre-process Vis-NIR or MIR spectra — preprocess_spectra
text: Supported method values: "snv" Standard Normal Variate. row centered         mean divided standard deviation. "sg1" Savitzky-Golay 1st derivative window         five wavelengths quadratic polynomial. "snv+sg1" SNV followed SG1 (default; standard         pipeline used OSSL pretrained models Vis-NIR). prospectr available, use prospectr::standardNormalVariate prospectr::savitzkyGolay (Rcpp implementation, faster supports arbitrary window/polynomial). native fallback uses classical 5-point first-derivative coefficients (-2, -1, 0, 1, 2) / 10, closed-form Savitzky-Golay solution window 5 / polynomial 2 / derivative 1.

title: Pre-process Vis-NIR or MIR spectra — preprocess_spectra
text: Savitzky, ., & Golay, M. J. E. (1964). Smoothing differentiation data simplified least squares procedures. Analytical Chemistry, 36(8), 1627–1639. Barnes, R. J., Dhanoa, M. S., & Lister, S. J. (1989). Standard Normal Variate transformation de-trending near-infrared diffuse reflectance spectra. Applied Spectroscopy, 43(5), 772–777. Stevens, ., & Ramirez-Lopez, L. (2024). prospectr: Misc. functions processing sample selection spectroscopic data. R package version 0.2.7.

title: Pre-process Vis-NIR or MIR spectra — preprocess_spectra
code: set.seed(1) X <- matrix(runif(5 * 2151, 0, 1), nrow = 5) colnames(X) <- 350:2500 Xp <- preprocess_spectra(X, method = "snv+sg1") dim(Xp)  # 5 x 2147 (4 columns dropped by SG window 5) #> [1]    5 2147

what: Toxic qualifier (tx): toxic concentration of organic or inorganic constituents. — qual_toxic
title: Toxic qualifier (tx): toxic concentration of organic or inorganic constituents. — qual_toxic
text: WRB 2022 Ch 5 / Ch 4 Histosols + Cryosols + Technosols (variable pages): substances concentrations toxic plant roots. Practical proxy: low pH (<= 3.5, sulfuric / hyperacidic) high electrical conductivity (>= 16 dS/m, equivalent Salic) specific contamination fields (heavy metals, hydrocarbons) soilKey schema yet model. v0.9.33 v0 implementation uses pH <= 3.5 EC >= 16 dS/m.

text: v0.9.31 broadens proxy "clay <= 5  <= 5  caught) : clay_pct <= 10 (loamy sands finer sands         qualify – 5 sand_pct >= 80 (sand-dominated texture – NEW         requirement, since clay alone sufficient); coarse_fragments_pct <= 15 (coarse fragments         tolerated; 5 least 50          (preserved v0.8). still excludes Loamy Psamments Sandy-Loamy Psamments (Udipsamments / Ustipsamments fallthroughs) requiring sand >= 80  near-pure-sand texture.

what: Load FEBR datasets as a list of PedonRecord objects — read_febr_pedons
title: Load FEBR datasets as a list of PedonRecord objects — read_febr_pedons
text: Wraps febr::readFEBR() (CRAN package, FEBR v1.9.9+ recommended) adapts returned camada (layer) + observacao tables soilKey schema. Auto-detects Munsell columns across ~6 distinct conventions found 200 FEBR datasets carry color data, parses PT-BR Munsell strings ("2,5YR 3/6") converts FEBR's standard units soilKey conventions.

title: Load FEBR datasets as a list of PedonRecord objects — read_febr_pedons
code: read_febr_pedons(   dataset_codes = c("ctb0039"),   febr_repo = NULL,   min_munsell_coverage = 0,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Load FEBR datasets as a list of PedonRecord objects — read_febr_pedons
text: dataset_codes Character vector FEBR dataset IDs (e.g. c("ctb0032", "ctb0562")). Pass "" download every Munsell-bearing dataset; heavy (network calls per dataset). Default: small curated sample development. febr_repo Optional override FEBR repository location, forwarded febr::readFEBR. min_munsell_coverage Drop pedons whose horizons missing Munsell. Default 0 (keep ); set 0.5 keep pedons least 50 horizons Munsell hue. verbose TRUE (default), prints per-dataset join statistics.

title: Load FEBR datasets as a list of PedonRecord objects — read_febr_pedons
text: list PedonRecord objects         site$id = FEBR observacao_id,         site$reference_sibcs = surveyor's classification         available, one horizon per FEBR camada         row.

what: Export a classification result + pedon to a QGIS GeoPackage — report_to_qgis
title: Export a classification result + pedon to a QGIS GeoPackage — report_to_qgis
text: Writes single GeoPackage (.gpkg) QGIS reads natively, containing one POINT layer (profile location classification metadata attributes) plus two attribute-tables (horizons schema provenance log). Lets pedologist overlay soilKey result soil-survey base map join field-campaign vector data without writing R SQL.

title: Export a classification result + pedon to a QGIS GeoPackage — report_to_qgis
code: report_to_qgis(   pedon,   classifications,   file,   report_html = NULL,   overwrite = TRUE )

title: Export a classification result + pedon to a QGIS GeoPackage — report_to_qgis
text: pedon PedonRecord. classifications list one three ClassificationResult objects, named wrb / sibcs / usda. Pass output classify_from_documents verbatim, build list manually. file Output path (.gpkg). Created parents. report_html Optional path sibling HTML report (rendered via report_html) – stored report_html attribute pedon_point QGIS users can launch report feature pop-. overwrite TRUE (default), existing file replaced; otherwise error thrown.

title: Export a classification result + pedon to a QGIS GeoPackage — report_to_qgis
text: output file path, invisibly. Side-effect:         writes multi-layer GeoPackage.

title: Export a classification result + pedon to a QGIS GeoPackage — report_to_qgis
text: point geometry uses pedon's site CRS (pedon$site$crs, default EPSG:4326). site coordinates, function still writes two attribute tables skips point layer emits warning.

title: Export a classification result + pedon to a QGIS GeoPackage — report_to_qgis
text: pedon_point site_id, country, year, lat, lon,         crs, wrb_name, wrb_rsg, wrb_grade, wrb_principal,         wrb_supplementary, sibcs_name, sibcs_ordem, sibcs_grade,         usda_name, usda_order, usda_grade, n_horizons,         report_html (relative path), generated_at. horizons_table site_id, horizon_idx, top_cm,         bottom_cm, designation, plus canonical         horizon_column_spec() attributes present. provenance_log site_id, horizon_idx, attribute,         source, confidence, notes.

title: Export a classification result + pedon to a QGIS GeoPackage — report_to_qgis
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ pedon <- make_ferralsol_canonical() results <- list(   wrb   = classify_wrb2022(pedon, on_missing = "silent"),   sibcs = classify_sibcs(pedon, include_familia = TRUE),   usda  = classify_usda(pedon) ) report_to_qgis(pedon, results,                file        = "perfil_042.gpkg",                report_html = "perfil_042.html") # In QGIS: Layer -> Add Layer -> Add Vector Layer -> perfil_042.gpkg } # }

text: Tests whether horizon meets salic horizon criteria. salic horizon horizon soluble-salt accumulation, diagnostic Solonchaks.

title: Salic horizon (WRB 2022) — salic
code: salic(   pedon,   min_thickness = 15,   min_ec_dS_m = 15,   alkaline_min_ec_dS_m = 8,   alkaline_min_pH = 8.5,   min_product = 450,   alkaline_min_product = 240 )

title: Salic horizon (WRB 2022) — salic
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_thickness Minimum thickness cm (default 15). min_ec_dS_m Primary EC threshold (default 15 dS/m 25C). alkaline_min_ec_dS_m Alkaline-path EC threshold (default 8 dS/m, used pH(H2O) \>= alkaline_min_pH). alkaline_min_pH Required pH(H2O) alkaline path (default 8.5). min_product Primary path product (EC * thickness dS/m * cm) threshold (default 450 per WRB 2022). alkaline_min_product Alkaline-path product threshold (default 240).

title: Salic horizon (WRB 2022) — salic
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Salic horizon (WRB 2022) — salic
text: Sub-tests called: test_ec_concentration – EC \>= 15 dS/m         (primary) (EC \>= 8 dS/m pH(H2O) \>= 8.5)         (alkaline). test_minimum_thickness – thickness \>= 15 cm. test_salic_product – EC * thickness product         \>= 450 (primary) \>= 240 (alkaline) per qualifying         layer. v0.3.1: alkaline-path product test added (WRB 2022 Ch 3.1.20, p. 49). Earlier versions enforced primary EC + thickness gate.

title: Salic horizon (WRB 2022) — salic
text: IUSS Working Group WRB (2022). World Reference Base Soil Resources, 4th edition. International Union Soil Sciences, Vienna. Chapter 3.1.20 – Salic horizon (p. 49).

what: soilKey: Automated Soil Profile Classification per WRB 2022 and SiBCS — soilKey-package
title: soilKey: Automated Soil Profile Classification per WRB 2022 and SiBCS — soilKey-package
text: soilKey implements deterministic classification keys World Reference Base Soil Resources 2022 (4th edition) Brazilian System Soil Classification (SiBCS, 5th edition). separates concerns strictly: taxonomic key pure function structured profile data, optional modules provide vision-language extraction, spatial priors SoilGrids, gap-filling soil attributes Vis-NIR MIR spectra via Open Soil Spectral Library (OSSL).

title: soilKey: Automated Soil Profile Classification per WRB 2022 and SiBCS — soilKey-package
text: never delegate key. Vision-language models restricted schema-validated extraction soil attributes unstructured sources (PDFs, photos, field sheets). taxonomic key always evaluated deterministic R code driven versioned YAML rules.

title: soilKey: Automated Soil Profile Classification per WRB 2022 and SiBCS — soilKey-package
text: PedonRecord — site, horizons, spectra, images,         documents, per-attribute provenance log. DiagnosticResult — return type every diagnostic         function (e.g. argic, ferralic,         mollic); always carries sub-test evidence         missing-attribute report alongside boolean. ClassificationResult — return type         classify_wrb2022; carries full key trace,         ambiguities, missing-data hints, provenance-aware evidence         grade.

title: soilKey: Automated Soil Profile Classification per WRB 2022 and SiBCS — soilKey-package
text: Every attribute used key carries provenance tag c("measured", "extracted_vlm", "predicted_spectra", "inferred_prior", "user_assumed"). final classification evidence grade one c("", "B", "C", "D") fully laboratory-measured unambiguous D tentative multimodal.

title: soilKey: Automated Soil Profile Classification per WRB 2022 and SiBCS — soilKey-package
text: v0.1 implements three WRB 2022 horizon diagnostics — argic, ferralic, mollic — Ferralsols path WRB key end--end. full 32-RSG key, 202 qualifiers, SiBCS key, multimodal extraction, spatial-prior, OSSL-spectroscopy modules scheduled subsequent releases. See ARCHITECTURE.md.

title: soilKey: Automated Soil Profile Classification per WRB 2022 and SiBCS — soilKey-package
text: IUSS Working Group WRB (2022). World Reference Base Soil Resources, 4th edition. International Union Soil Sciences, Vienna. Embrapa (2018). Sistema Brasileiro de Classificação de Solos, 5ª edição. Embrapa Solos, Brasília. Beaudette, D. E., Roudier, P., & O'Geen, . T. (2013). Algorithms Quantitative Pedology: toolkit soil scientists. Computers & Geosciences, 52, 258–268.

title: soilKey: Automated Soil Profile Classification per WRB 2022 and SiBCS — soilKey-package

One "wrb2022" (default), "sibcs", "usda". buffer_m Radius metres around point used gather raster pixels (default 1000 m, .e. roughly 4 SoilGrids pixels). source_url Path / URL dominant-soil raster. top_n Keep top N classes probability (default 5). verbose Emit cli summary.

title: Likely soil classes at a geographic location (spatial classification aid) — soil_classes_at_location

title: Embrapa national soil-class spatial prior (Brazil only) — spatial_prior_embrapa
text: pedon PedonRecord. raster_path Required. Path local categorical raster (GeoTIFF) Embrapa SiBCS classes. built-file v0.5 – download polygon map https://www.embrapa.br/solos/sibcs rasterise . buffer_m Buffer radius metres (default 3750, .e. ~15-cell neighbourhood 250 m resolution). lut Optional named character vector mapping raster integer values soil-class codes. NULL, raster categories used -(terra::levels). n_classes_top Keep top N classes (default 10). ... Reserved.

title: Embrapa national soil-class spatial prior (Brazil only) — spatial_prior_embrapa
text: data.table columns rsg_code,         probability.

title: Embrapa national soil-class spatial prior (Brazil only) — spatial_prior_embrapa
text: Unlike SoilGrids, Embrapa publish per-pixel probabilities, empirical frequency neighbourhood window (default 15 x 15 cells = ~3.75 km radius 250 m resolution) used approximation.

title: SoilGrids spatial prior — spatial_prior_soilgrids
text: Reads categorical raster dominant Reference Soil Groups around pedon's site, buffers point metric coordinates, extracts pixel values within buffer, returns empirical class frequency probability distribution RSG codes.

title: SoilGrids spatial prior — spatial_prior_soilgrids
code: spatial_prior_soilgrids(   pedon,   system = c("wrb2022", "usda"),   buffer_m = 250,   source_url = NULL,   n_classes_top = 10,   lut = NULL,   ... )

title: SoilGrids spatial prior — spatial_prior_soilgrids
text: pedon PedonRecord non-NULL site$lat site$lon. system Classification system; "wrb2022" (default) maps SoilGrids integer codes WRB lookup table. "usda" reserved future SoilGrids-USDA layer. buffer_m Buffer radius metres around point (default 250 m, .e. one SoilGrids pixel). source_url Optional. path URL accepted terra::rast. NULL, falls back getOption("soilKey.test_raster"). n_classes_top Keep top N classes frequency (default 10). Set Inf keep . lut Optional named integer vector mapping raster values RSG codes. Default soilgrids_wrb_lut; pass custom one raster uses different codes. ... Reserved future use.

title: SoilGrids spatial prior — spatial_prior_soilgrids
text: data.table columns rsg_code,         probability.

title: SoilGrids spatial prior — spatial_prior_soilgrids
text: real use, pass source_url pointing SoilGrids "MostProbable WRB" GeoTIFF / COG, e.g. one regional cuts published https://files.isric.org/soilgrids/latest/data/wrb/. tests, set options(soilKey.test_raster = "/path//syn.tif") point local synthetic raster – avoids network access CI.

title: SoilGrids spatial prior — spatial_prior_soilgrids
text: use sf::st_transform sf available; otherwise fall back terra::project single-point SpatVector. buffer constructed metric (UTM) coordinates buffer_m metres regardless pedon CRS. raster queried native CRS via terra's automatic reprojection.

title: Spodic horizon (WRB 2022) — spodic
text: Tests whether horizon meets spodic horizon criteria. spodic horizon illuvial horizon active Al + Fe oxalate- extractable material plus organic matter; diagnostic Podzols.

title: Spodic horizon (WRB 2022) — spodic
code: spodic(   pedon,   min_thickness = 2.5,   min_alfe = 0.5,   max_ph = 5.9,   min_oc_in_b = 0.5,   engine = NULL )

title: Spodic horizon (WRB 2022) — spodic
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_thickness Minimum thickness cm (default 2.5). min_alfe Minimum (Al_ox + 0.5 * Fe_ox) percent (default 0.5). max_ph Maximum ph_h2o (default 5.9). min_oc_in_b Minimum OC % candidate Bh / Bs layer v0.9.19 morphological inference path Al / Fe oxalate missing (default 0.5). engine One "soilkey" (default; strict v0.9.19 morphological path requires Bh / Bs / Bhs designation + albic E ) "aqp" (relaxed v0.9.84 path: B* E* OC translocation peak). NULL, reads getOption("soilKey.diagnostic_engine").

title: Spodic horizon (WRB 2022) — spodic
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Spodic horizon (WRB 2022) — spodic
text: Sub-tests: test_spodic_aluminum_iron – (Al_ox +         0.5*Fe_ox) >= 0.5% test_ph_below – ph_h2o <= 5.9 test_minimum_thickness – thickness >= 2.5 cm v0.2 limitations: WRB color criterion (hue 5YR yellower chroma <= 5, specific dark colors) enforced. (Al_ox + Fe_ox)/clay >= 0.05 alternative ratio test yet wired. deferred v0.3.

title: Spodic horizon (WRB 2022) — spodic
text: KSSL+NASIS Spodosols routinely use generic "B1" / "B2" / "Bw" designations rather specific Bh / Bs / Bhs v0.9.19 morphological-inference path requires. 14 KSSL+NASIS Podzol references, 1 / 14 passes spodic via v0.9.19 path; 7 / 14 E-designated albic-eligible horizon OC peak B horizon (canonical Podzol illuviation signature) use generic B / Bw designations fail strict morph. engine = "aqp" (read getOption("soilKey.diagnostic_engine", "soilkey") engine NULL) Al / Fe oxalate unmeasured v0.9.19 strict path fire, accept B* designation E*-designated horizon : ph_h2o <= max_ph B horizon, oc_pct >= min_oc_in_b B horizon, OC B greater maximum OC         horizon (translocation signature). Default engine "soilkey" – canonical behaviour bit--bit preserved.

title: Spodic horizon (WRB 2022) — spodic
text: IUSS Working Group WRB (2022), Chapter 3, Spodic horizon.

title: Technic features (WRB 2022) — technic_features
text: Tests three WRB 2022 alternative qualifying conditions Technosols: Artefacts >= artefacts_min_pct (default 20%)         volume within upper max_top_cm (default 100 cm). continuous geomembrane (geomembrane_present == TRUE)         within upper 100 cm. Technic hard material (concrete, asphalt, mine spoil)         technic_hardmaterial_pct >= hardmaterial_min_pct         (default 95%) surface (top_cm <=         hardmaterial_max_top_cm, default 5). Either path qualifies.

title: Test CEC (1M NH4OAc, pH 7) per kg clay <= threshold — test_cec_per_clay
text: Brazilian / SOTERLAC / BDsolos profiles often record exchange complex separate Ca, Mg, K, Na, Al cmol values without explicit "Valor T" CEC column, cec_cmol NA entire profile. options(soilKey.ferralic_ecec_fallback = TRUE) test falls back ECEC sum (ca_cmol + mg_cmol + k_cmol + na_cmol + al_cmol) layers cec_cmol missing components present. Default FALSE (canonical WRB behaviour preserved). Note: ECEC typically smaller CEC acidic pH omits H+; using ECEC threshold therefore conservative (permissive) – produce false positives, recover Latossolos lacked Valor T.

what: v0.9.86 engine="aqp" auto-enables the ECEC fallback
title: Test CEC (1M NH4OAc, pH 7) per kg clay <= threshold — test_cec_per_clay
text: soilKey.diagnostic_engine = "aqp" now auto-enables v0.9.69 ECEC fallback (user can still suppress explicitly setting soilKey.ferralic_ecec_fallback = FALSE). rationale: aqp engine "data-quality-aware" mode, designed field-described datasets like BDsolos / Redape Valor T rarely recorded. Bundling two opt-ins lifts BDsolos RJ Latossolo recall 14.9\ 28.1\

title: Train pre-trained PLSR models from an OSSL library — train_pls_from_ossl
text: ossl_library list two named elements: Xr (numeric matrix training spectra) Yr (data.frame keyed property name, one row per training spectrum). See ossl_library_template. properties Character vector column names ossl_library$Yr train models . Defaults six core soil properties exposed OSSL. ncomp_max Integer. Upper bound number PLS components consider cross-validation. Defaults 20. validation One "CV" (default, k-fold), "LOO" (leave-one-, slow), "none" (uses ncomp_max components without selection). segments Number CV segments validation = "CV". Default 10. preprocess Pre-processing label passed preprocess_spectra. Stored trained models predict_from_spectra can reapply . min_n Minimum number valid training samples (dropping rows non-finite y X). Properties threshold skipped warning. Default 50. verbose TRUE (default), prints per-property summary completion.

title: Train pre-trained PLSR models from an OSSL library — train_pls_from_ossl

title: Vertic horizon (WRB 2022 Ch 3.1) — vertic_horizon
text: Stricter vertic *properties*: vertic *horizon* requires \>= 30% clay throughout, slickensides \>= "common" level, shrink-swell cracks \>= 0.5 cm wide. Used Vertisols. v0.9.19 adds -alternative COLE-based linear-extensibility path: summed (cole_value * thickness) upper 100 cm \>= 6 cm passes diagnostic even slickensides + cracks recorded (KST 13ed Ch 16 LE alternative, p 343).

title: Vertic horizon (WRB 2022 Ch 3.1) — vertic_horizon

title: Vertic horizon (WRB 2022 Ch 3.1) — vertic_horizon
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_clay Numeric threshold option (see Details). min_thickness Numeric threshold option (see Details). min_le_cm Minimum LE sum (cm) COLE-based path (default 6, per KST 13ed Ch 16). le_max_depth_cm Depth window (cm) COLE-based path (default 100).

title: Vertic horizon (WRB 2022 Ch 3.1) — vertic_horizon
text: Field-described Brazilian Vertissolos profiles (e.g.\ Embrapa Redape curated dataset) encode vertic morphology via v master-letter modifier horizon designation (Bv, Bvk1, Cv, Cvz) without recording slickensides class shrink_swell_cracks_cm numeric inputs. options(soilKey.vertic_designation_inference = TRUE) function accepts layer vertic canonical COLE paths fail NA layer clay_pct >= min_clay designation matches v master-letter modifier. Default FALSE.

text: pedon PedonRecord. min_clay Minimum clay percent (default 30, per WRB 2022). min_thickness Minimum thickness (cm) vertic layer (default 25 per WRB 2022 Ch 3.2.x). slickenside_levels Vector slickensides values accepted evidence (default c("common", "many", "continuous")).

title: Vertic properties (WRB 2022) — vertic_properties
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Vertic properties (WRB 2022) — vertic_properties
text: Sub-tests: test_clay_above – clay >= 30% test_slickensides_present – slickensides         "common" level test_minimum_thickness – combined vertic layer         thickness >= 25 cm (v0.3.1 added per WRB 2022) v0.3.1: thickness gate added. Limitations remaining: WRB also accepts deep cracks (>= 1 cm wide extending surface >= 50 cm depth, soil dry) wedge-shaped peds alternative evidence; implementation requires clay + slickensides. "mixing upper 18 cm" clause WRB still deferred.

title: Construct a VLM provider chat object — vlm_provider
text: Passing name = "ollama" runs every extraction locally via Ollama server (default gemma4:e4b, Gemma 4 edge multimodal text+image+audio support). data leaves machine, recommended setting sensitive field descriptions (e.g. governmental surveys, indigenous land studies) institutional independence data sovereignty matter. Pull model first: start Ollama server (ollama serve) chat object returned dispatch HTTP locally.
code: ollama pull gemma4:e4b      # ~3 GB edge variant (default)   ollama pull gemma4:31b      # frontier dense variant   ollama pull gemma3:27b      # earlier generation, still solid

title: Construct a VLM provider chat object — vlm_provider
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Cloud provider (needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) provider <- vlm_provider("anthropic")  # Local Gemma 4 edge model -- default, ~3 GB, runs anywhere provider <- vlm_provider("ollama")  # Local Gemma 4 frontier dense model -- best quality provider <- vlm_provider("ollama", model = "gemma4:31b")  # Any other multimodal model the user has pulled provider <- vlm_provider("ollama", model = "qwen2.5vl:32b") } # }

title: soilKey 0.9.73 (2026-05-09)
text: 17% accuracy ceiling soilKey logic failure – ’s fundamental limit WoSIS exposes: Vertisols need slickensides + cracks + COLE – WoSIS records none. Even v0.9.72 v-suffix designation inference fire WoSIS designations stripped /B/Bt/C without lowercase modifiers. Plinthosols need plinthite\_pct – WoSIS. designation issue blocks v0.9.72 f-suffix path. Gleysols need redoximorphic\_features\_pct g-suffix – neither WoSIS. Solonetz need ESP > 15 – WoSIS CEC 26% \code{na\_cmol} 0%. Phaeozem / Kastanozem / Chernozem need mollic colour test (Munsell value/chroma) – WoSIS records 0% Munsell. Podzols need spodic Al/Fe oxalate – WoSIS. Andosols need Al + Fe oxalate, P retention, bulk density – P retention 1%, BD 2% WoSIS. Luvisols need argic + BS > 50% – BS 14% available. four well-handled RSGs (Histosol 100%, Leptosol 80%, Arenosol 80%, Cambisol 60%) exactly WoSIS data suffices: OC Histosol, coarse-fragments + designation Leptosol (lifted v0.9.66!), texture Arenosol, fall-Cambisol.

title: soilKey 0.9.65 (2026-05-08)
text: Re-ran inst/benchmarks/run_lucas_v0964_engine_aqp.R 30-pedon FR/PL/panel three configurations: aqp engine successfully predicts single -set Leptosol profile (soilkey engine misses; baseline = 0%). aqp leptic relaxation currently aggressive: 29/30 pedons collapse onto Leptosols regardless true class. negates v0.9.64 +60pp lift broader EU-LUCAS benchmark. Root cause: leptic_features() engine=“aqp” path lowers coarse-fragment threshold 50% adds “thin topsoil ending upper 25 cm” path. topsoil-LUCAS data, second path passes every pedon (since LUCAS ships 0-20 cm), forcing Leptosols ahead every RSG. Fix scheduled v0.9.66: tighten thin-topsoil path requires evidence contact rock (e.g., increasing coarse fragments toward bottom horizon, !.na(parent_material) & grepl("rock|stone", parent_material)). Alternatively: gate thin-topsoil rule behind opt-flag. raw report preserved inst/benchmarks/reports/lucas_v0964_engine_aqp_2026-05-08.txt v0.9.66 fix can measured baseline.

text: Re-run via inst/benchmarks/run_lucas_v0964_engine_aqp.R 30 pedons (FR/PL/, 10 ) three configurations: HEADLINE: LUCAS WRB 0% -> 60% accuracy com aqp engine + SoilGrids subsoil fill. combination finally fires v0.9.50 promise “subsoil chemistry destrava cambic / argic / mollic / ferralic via 9 properties SoilGrids 30-60 cm”. Per-RSG breakdown (aqp_subsoil_soilgrids): Honest mechanism: 60% lift comes ENTIRELY Cambisols going 0 -> 18 (100% recall). RSGs still get mis-classified Cambisols (5 Arenosols, 1 Fluvisols, 1 Leptosols, 3 Luvisols, 1 Podzols also predicted Cambisols). classifier now -permissive Cambisols data quality level – correctly identifies true Cambisols, dominate LUCAS reference (60% share). Net: real, measurable lift unusable 0% baseline 60% Cambisols-dominant prediction, validated canonical ESDB raster. remaining gap (Arenosols / Luvisols / Podzols recall) requires RSG-specific diagnostic priorities (currently aqp’s cambic_aqp fires get evaluated). v0.9.65 candidate: per-RSG dispatch ordering multiple aqp diagnostics fire simultaneously. inst/benchmarks/reports/lucas_v0964_engine_aqp_<DATE>.{rds,txt}.

title: soilKey 0.9.61 (2026-05-07)
text: “diagnostic gaps v0.9.60 BDsolos benchmark” release. Quatro itens que o RJ benchmark v0.9.60 destacou: Gleissolos diagnostic: 0% recall em n=98 era gap real (não label). test_gleyic_features exigia redoximorphic_features_pct populado, mas o BDsolos loader não mapeava Mosqueado - Quantidade. Plus: muitos perfis têm Munsell hue gleyic (5GY/N/10G) sem mottle percent registrado. Latossolos diagnostic: 7.9% recall em n=114. B_latossolico excluía qualquer layer que passasse argic(), perdendo Latossolos com clay increase marginal. Per SiBCS Cap 18, ferralic + thickness + CEC/clay <= 17 + cerosidade fraca = Latossolo mesmo com clay increase pequeno. -scale BDsolos benchmark trava em ~2500 R6 objects em sessão única. Workaround: subprocess Rscript per UF + agregação RDS. WRB empirical close (LUCAS subsoil fill) – em background.

O resultado popula redoximorphic_features_pct para 107 / 722 perfis em RJ.csv (15%).

what: 4. LUCAS WRB overnight close – HONEST NEGATIVE RESULT
title: soilKey 0.9.61 (2026-05-07)
text: run_lucas_v0950_close_focused.R terminou em 55 min (3307 s) wall-clock. Resultado: O fill_subsoil_from = “soilgrids” NAO lift acuracia WRB neste sample (30 perfis FR/PL/). Per-RSG recall pos-fill: Cambisols 0/12, Gleysols 0/1, Leptosols 0/4, Luvisols 0/6, Podzols 0/3, Vertisols 0/1. Todas 27 predictions -scope continuam caindo em Regosols (10) ou Calcisols (3) – ou seja, exatamente o catch-behavior v0.9.49 baseline (3.0% N=200, also Regosols-dominant). Implicacao: o claim v0.9.50 NEWS (“destrava cambic / argic / mollic / ferralic via 9 propriedades SoilGrids 30-60 cm”) nao se realizou empiricamente. Hipoteses para v0.9.62 investigar: lookup_soilgrids() esta retornando valores corretos? Comparar com queries diretas ao COG endpoint para coords conhecidos. .fill_horizon_from_soilgrids() esta populando os schema columns corretos (clay_pct, sand_pct, ph_h2o, soc, cec_cmol, bdod, nitrogen, cfvo)? Ler um perfil pos-fill e verificar. diagnostics WRB (cambic / argic / mollic / ferralic) estao testando os campos populados pelo fill? Pode ser que o cambic diagnostic precise de structure/clay-films morfologicos que SoilGrids nao fornece, e nao apenas chemistry. Possivelmente o caminho real e o fill_topsoil_from = "spectra" com OSSL pretrained models – mais alta fidelidade per-coord (v0.9.46). Esse path nao foi testado neste run. Files: inst/benchmarks/reports/lucas_v0950_close_focused_2026-05-07.{rds,txt}.

title: soilKey 0.9.60 (2026-05-06)
text: “tripla validação BDsolos + fechamento empírico v0.9.50” release. Duas peças que fechavam buracos abertos desde v0.9.50 / v0.9.58: benchmark_bdsolos() – novo benchmark cruzando os três sistemas (WRB 2022, SiBCS 5, USDA-ST 13) contra o ground-truth BDsolos nacional (~9 k perfis, 3 colunas de classificação por perfil quando o pedólogo preencheu). Fix em .bdsolos_find_header_line() – bug crítico v0.9.58 que fazia o auto-detector de header escolher uma linha de DADOS (não o header) sempre que algum perfil tivesse ; embutido em string entre aspas (e.g. nomes de pedólogos “Klaus Wittern; Elias Mothci”). Resultado: 0% taxon / 0% Munsell RJ.csv real (722 perfis), apesar v0.9.58 alegar o oposto partir de uma fixture sintética. Empirical close de v0.9.50 – número de acurácia WRB pós-fill_subsoil_from = "soilgrids" que o release v0.9.50 anunciou (13 testes sintéticos passavam) mas nunca documentou numericamente. Roda em inst/benchmarks/run_lucas_v0950_close.R e o report fica em inst/benchmarks/reports/lucas_v0950_close_*.

title: soilKey 0.9.60 (2026-05-06)
text: Sintoma – load_bdsolos_csv("RJ.csv") retornava 722 perfis sem nenhum dos três labels de classificação e sem Munsell, embora o v0.9.58 NEWS.md afirmasse “100% Munsell preservado em RJ.csv”. Causa – função usava length(strsplit(s, ";", fixed = TRUE)[[1L]]) para contar campos por linha e escolhia o “header” como linha com mais campos via .max(). O problema: strsplit(fixed = TRUE) é quote-blind. O BDsolos full export tem rotineiramente ; dentro de strings entre aspas (campo “Responsável() pela Descrição” tipo “Klaus Peter Wittern; Elias Pedroso Mothci; …”, remarks geológicos com pontuação rica, etc.). Esses ; extras inflavam contagem das linhas de DADOS acima da contagem real header (268 → até 272), e .max() sempre retornava primeira dessas linhas de dados como sendo “o header”. Fix – substituído por scan(text = ..., sep = ..., quote = "\"") per-line, que é quote-aware. Mantém o mapeamento 1:1 entre posição e número de linha (que utils::count.fields() quebra ao descartar linhas em branco). Validação BDsolos real – load_bdsolos_csv("RJ.csv", verbose = TRUE) agora retorna: (antes: 722 perfis / Munsell em 0 / taxon em 0 / coords em 0).
code: load_bdsolos_csv(): 722 perfis (Munsell em 722, taxon em 720, coords em 560)

title: soilKey 0.9.60 (2026-05-06)
text: R/benchmark-bdsolos.R exporta uma função nova: Reusa os normalisers FEBR já existentes (normalise_febr_sibcs, normalise_febr_wrb, normalise_febr_usda) para canonicalizar os três formatos Embrapa (PT-BR -caps SiBCS / Title Case singular WRB / sufixo-codificado USDA Subgroup) antes de comparar com saída dos três classificadores. Auto-skip por sistema sem label – BDsolos tem reference_sibcs denso (~80% nacional) mas reference_wrb e reference_st esparsos (UF-dependentes; ~5% RJ). função sempre reporta $coverage por sistema, e devolve accuracy = NA_real_ + message = "no_reference_labels" $per_system quando o ground-truth não foi preenchido pelo pedólogo. Roda os outros sistemas normalmente. Confusion matrix + per-class recall por sistema, mais um contador de erros classificador (per-pedon try-catch, não aborta o run inteiro).
code: benchmark_bdsolos(pedons,                    systems     = c("wrb2022", "sibcs", "usda"),                    sibcs_level = c("order", "subordem"),                    max_n       = NULL,                    verbose     = TRUE)

O classificador estava acertando esses casos semanticamente (43/54 Podzolicos -> Argissolos) mas eram contados como erro porque o normaliser nao mapeava legacy -> modern. v0.9.60 adiciona .SIBCS_LEGACY_ORDER_MAP ao normalise_febr_sibcs():

what: Por que o BDsolos RJ esta abaixo do FEBR
title: soilKey 0.9.60 (2026-05-06)
text: 35.8% BDsolos RJ vs 56.7% FEBR superconjunto – 21 pp de diferenca, e nao tudo e gap de modelagem: Composicao dataset: RJ tem proporcao alta de Latossolos e Gleissolos onde o classifier tem recall baixo (8% e 0% respectivamente). FEBR superconjunto tem distribuicao mais Argissolo-pesada (que o classifier acerta 72%). Quality filter: o benchmark FEBR usa filter explicito requiring clay_pct populated. O BDsolos run aqui usa todos os 720 perfis com label, incluindo perfis com chemistry esparsa. Subordem: nao medido aqui. FEBR 9.93% v0.9.27. v0.9.61 esta marcada para investigar especificamente Latossolos (RJ recall 7.9%, confusao predominante com Argissolos / Cambissolos / Neossolos – sugere threshold horizonte latossolico vs B textural muito conservador) e Gleissolos (0% recall em 98 perfis).

what: Sobre acuracia em sistemas mundialmente conhecidos (WRB / USDA)
title: soilKey 0.9.60 (2026-05-06)
text: Pergunta natural: “soilKey vai ter acuracia boa nos diagnosticos mundialmente famosos WRB e USDA Soil Taxonomy?”. Os numeros -scale ja publicados em releases anteriores respondem: USDA Subgroup (n=865, v0.9.27): 5.09% – baixo mas consistente com literatura para sistemas baseados em regras (subgrupo USDA tem ~1700 classes). Per-Order USDA recall (KSSL n=2002): Comparativo com literatura: - Sistemas rule-based (engines deterministicos como soilKey): tipicamente 30-60% Order - Sistemas deep-learning (SoilNet, etc.) com features morfologicas ricas: 50-70% Order - Pedologos humanos com perfil completo + chemistry: 70-85% Order O soilKey esta solidamente na faixa rule-based com numeros defensaveis. WRB nao tem numero -scale ainda – LUCAS topsoil puxa para 3% por falta de subsolo, e o overnight rerun com SoilGrids subsoil fill (v0.9.61) deve mostrar numeros comparaveis ao SiBCS / USDA.
code: | Benchmark                         | Sistema | n     | Order accuracy | |-----------------------------------|---------|-------|---------------:| | FEBR superconjunto (v0.9.27)      | SiBCS   |   554 |  56.7% [52.7-60.6] | | KSSL+NASIS (v0.9.27, com filter)  | USDA    |   865 |  37.0% [33.9-40.2] | | KSSL+NASIS+Tiebreaker (v0.9.22)   | USDA    | 2,002 |  31.3% [29.0-33.5] | | LUCAS Soil 2018 (v0.9.49)         | WRB     |   200 |  3.0% (topsoil-only) | | **BDsolos RJ (v0.9.60, este)**    | SiBCS   |   720 |  35.8% (post-fix)  | | LUCAS + subsoil fill (v0.9.50)    | WRB     | 100-200 |  (overnight rerun) | Vertisols   70.0%   <- forte  Inceptisols   47.2%    Aridisols   46.6%    Spodosols   42.0%     Entisols   41.3%      Oxisols   28.6%    Mollisols   23.2%     Ultisols   20.4%     Alfisols   19.4%     Andisols    0.0%   <- n=4, n.a.

Subsoil_soilgrids stage não foi fechado nesta release: 30 perfis × 9 propriedades SoilGrids não convergiu em 46 min de wall-clock (SoilGrids COG range read ~8-12s observados na rodada real, vs ~3-4s smoke test isolado; provavelmente carga servidor plantonderzoekwur.nl horário). Estima-se ~60-90 min para os 30 perfis completarem, ~3-6h para o N=100 run_lucas_v0950_close.R. Recomendação: rodar Rscript inst/benchmarks/run_lucas_v0950_close_focused.R overnight em janela de baixa latência SoilGrids, e abrir v0.9.61 com o número final + confusion matrix. O script salva stage 1 RDS imediatamente, e o resultado final .rds + .txt ao terminar stage 2. Os scripts e infra estão prontos – o hold-é puramente network/throughput de SoilGrids COG, não código.

title: soilKey 0.9.58 (2026-05-06)

title: soilKey 0.9.55 (2026-05-06)
text: “BDsolos R-side helpers” release. Adds three R-side helpers consume Embrapa BDsolos profile database (~9,000 perfis brasileiros, canonical source SiBCS-classified data morphology + Munsell colors) without leaving R.

title: soilKey 0.9.55 (2026-05-06)
text: R/bdsolos.R (new file) exports three functions plus internal column-detection layer: load_bdsolos_csv(path, sep, verbose) – reads long- format BDsolos export (one row per horizon, profile-id key) returns list . Auto-detects column-name convention via regex patterns covering classic PT-BR shape (, , , , , ) lowercase / SmartSolos-derived shape (, , , ). Texture OC converted g/kg percent (BDsolos canonical unit). inspect_bdsolos_csv(path, sep) – diagnostic helper. Prints raw schema, identifies columns map soilKey horizon attribute, lists unmapped columns, reports Munsell coverage (matiz / valor / croma) + surveyor’s taxonomic reference column. Run load_bdsolos_csv() new CSV BDsolos. download_bdsolos(out_path, accept_terms, filter_uf, attributes, timeout_seconds, chromote_session, verbose) – best-effort programmatic downloader via headless Chrome (). Drives 3-step Embrapa web form (accept terms -> select attributes -> submit query -> select results + radio CSV -> capture). Marked experimental: full-table queries (UF filter) frequently overload Embrapa server – prefer  batches one two states time stitch resulting CSVs. .bdsolos_norm() / .bdsolos_match_column() / .bdsolos_match_taxon_column() / .BDSOLOS_COLUMN_PATTERNS internals: deterministic Portuguese-aware column normaliser (handles  via ) plus regex table 30+ canonical BDsolos columns -> soilKey horizon schema.

what: Why R-side rather than the browser
title: soilKey 0.9.55 (2026-05-06)
text: first attempt used Chrome MCP drive BDsolos form interactively. full-table query (~9k profiles x ~30 horizon attributes) reliably freezes renderer – server-side PHP query takes minutes SPA handle gracefully. Going pure R-side via headless Chrome (-screen rendering) lets function batch UF recover via clean session restarts.

title: soilKey 0.9.48 (2026-05-04)
text: R/spatial-lookups.R exports two new helpers, shaped lookup_esdb(): lookup_mapbiomas_solos(coords, raster_path, legend = NULL) – Brazilian SiBCS national raster (MapBiomas Solos Collection 2, 30 m, 2023+). Local-file lookup; user passes unpacked GeoTIFF path. Optional 2-column legend (value, class_name) decodes integer codes SiBCS class strings. Auto-reprojection WGS84. lookup_soilgrids(coords, property, depth, quantile, baseurl, raw) – Global ISRIC SoilGrids 250m soil property predictions, read directly canonical Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF endpoint https://files.isric.org/soilgrids/latest/data/. download required; pixel query coordinate transferred HTTPS. Supports 11 SoilGrids properties (clay, sand, silt, phh2o, soc, cec, bdod, nitrogen, ocd, ocs, cfvo) 6 standard depths (0-5, 5-15, 15-30, 30-60, 60-100, 100-200 cm) 5 quantiles (mean, Q0.05, Q0.5, Q0.95, uncertainty). Returns values conventional units via published per-property scale factors (clay/silt/sand percent, pH, g/kg, cmol(c)/kg, g/cm^3).

title: soilKey 0.9.48 (2026-05-04)
text: Combined v0.9.44 lookup_esdb(), soilKey now offers three spatial validation axes: Europe: ESDB Raster Library 1 km (WRBLV1, WRBFU, FAO90LV1) – canonical reference per coordinate. Brazil: MapBiomas Solos 30 m – canonical SiBCS class per coordinate (national mapping). Global: SoilGrids 250 m – continuous soil property predictions (clay, pH, OC, etc.) per coordinate. PedonRecord lat/lon can cross-checked canonical map location – supports prior_check field ClassificationResult.

title: soilKey 0.9.47 (2026-05-04)
text: predict_xyz_from_spectra(spectra, wavelengths) – CIE XYZ tristimulus standard scale (Y = 100 perfect diffuse white). Auto-detects whether reflectance decimal (0..1) percent (0..100). Dependency-free (CIE table bundled R/sysdata.rda). predict_lab_from_spectra(spectra, wavelengths) – CIE Lab via standard XYZ -> Lab transform D65 / 2-degree observer. predict_munsell_from_spectra(spectra, wavelengths, round_chip = TRUE) – headline function. Returns munsell_hue_moist, munsell_value_moist, munsell_chroma_moist, munsell_string (e.g. "7.5YR 4/6"). Requires munsellinterpol; clear error missing. fill_munsell_from_spectra(pedon, overwrite, verbose) – high-level helper. Iterates pedon$spectra$vnir, runs prediction per horizon writes result via add_measurement(..., source = "predicted_spectra"). call, re-run classify_sibcs() – v0.9.45 “color-undetermined” fallback lifts descent proceeds subordem / GG / SG.

title: soilKey 0.9.47 (2026-05-04)

Combined v0.9.46 predict_from_spectra() (fills clay / sand / silt / pH / OC / CEC), o pacote agora classifica perfis brasileiros direto partir de espectro, sem morfologia descritiva nem morfologia laboratorial – exatamente o que destrava casos onde Embrapa BDsolos fornece quimica.

title: soilKey 0.9.45 (2026-05-04)
text: “color-undetermined graceful path” release. Fixes v0.9.35 Argissolo Vermelho / Amarelo / Vermelho-Amarelo silent-fallback case (44 perfis brasileiros caiam silenciosamente em PVA quando o matiz Munsell em B nao foi medido).

title: soilKey 0.9.45 (2026-05-04)
text: classify_sibcs() agora detecta o padrao “subordem catch-de cor atribuida porque o matiz Munsell esta ausente” e: Para descida nivel da Ordem (nao seleciona Grande Grupo nem Subgrupo); Mostra display_name formato "<Ordem> (cor determinar)" em vez catch-enganoso (Argissolos Vermelho-Amarelos); Adiciona munsell_hue_moist_horizon_B em missing_data; Rebaixa evidence_grade para "C" (classificacao parcial); Anexa um warning em PT-BR explicando o fallback e listando alternativas que perderam por falta de matiz; Expoe o registro estruturado em result$trace$color_undetermined (lista com detected, fallback_subordem, rejected_alternatives, would_resolve_with, reason). logica generica funciona para os 4 catch-alls de cor SiBCS: PVA (Argissolos Vermelho-Amarelos), LVA (Latossolos Vermelho-Amarelos), NX (Nitossolos Haplicos) e TX (Luvissolos Haplicos).

title: soilKey 0.9.45 (2026-05-04)
text: Antes v0.9.45, um perfil com B textural mas sem matiz Munsell medido era classificado como Argissolo Vermelho-Amarelo com evidence_grade = "" – o pacote afirmava com confianca maxima uma classe especifica que pode ser determinada com cor. Os 44 perfis flagados v0.9.35 cairam exatamente nesse padrao. Agora saida fica: interpretacao sai “falsa precisao” e entra “honesto sobre o que se sabe e o que ainda falta medir”.
code: Name           : Argissolos (cor a determinar) RSG/Order      : Argissolos Evidence grade : C Missing data   : munsell_hue_moist_horizon_B, ... Warnings       : Subordem 'Argissolos Vermelho-Amarelos' atribuida                  por fallback porque o matiz Munsell em B esta                  ausente. Medindo a cor seria possivel discriminar                  entre: Argissolos Vermelhos, Argissolos Amarelos,                  Argissolos Bruno-Acinzentados, Argissolos                  Acinzentados.

title: soilKey 0.9.39 (2026-05-03)
text: run_classify_app(): convenience wrapper locates bundled Shiny application inst/shiny/classify_app/ launches via shiny::runApp(). Requires shiny DT packages (Suggests; wrapper raises clear error missing). App features: Horizons input: upload CSV (one row per horizon, columns matching soilKey horizon schema – top_cm, bottom_cm, designation, plus clay_pct, sand_pct, silt_pct, ph_h2o, oc_pct, bs_pct, cec_cmol, …). Falls back built-sample (Latossolo Vermelho-style) file loaded. Site metadata: profile id, lat/lon, country, parent material. Classification: one button runs classify_all() shows WRB 2022 / SiBCS 5a / USDA ST 13ed names plus evidence grades. Trace tab: print full key-trace system inspect RSGs / Orders tested diagnostics fired. HTML report download: self-contained, external network requests; suitable emailing attaching laudo. Starter template download: sample CSV canonical column structure users clone modify. Use cases (mirrors v0.9.38 demo gallery interactive): field agronomist tablet: upload field-survey CSV, classify, download report, attach client deliverable. graduate student: paste textbook profile data, study 3 systems classify soil. research group: batch-process repeated upload, exports serve paper supplements. app require internet connection beyond bootstrap loading (Shiny CDN); classification runs locally user’s R session.

Annex profile -04 (Mata Atlantica, Brazil; gneiss). Chernozem – IUSS WRB (2022) Annex 1 didactic exemplar (central-European steppe; loess; deep organic-rich Ah). Podzol – Soil Atlas Europe (2005) Plate 19 (boreal forest, Sweden; glaciofluvial sand; E -> Bsh -> Bs sequence). Vertisol – FAO Field Guide canonical Pellic Vertisol (Deccan basalt residuum, India; smectite-rich black cotton). Gleysol – Soil Atlas Europe (2005) canonical Gleysol (Netherlands; fluvial clay peat; reduced grey-blue subsoil). Histosol – WRB 2022 Annex 1 didactic Ombric Fibric Histosol (Estonia; raised Sphagnum bog; rainwater-fed). profile built data published canonical soil-science sources, citations inline. Registered via demo/00Index exercises three keys plus v0.9.33 WRB qualifier closure (e.g. Profile 6 fires Floatic + Folic + Hemic + Ombric + Histosol, demonstrating v0.9.33 Ombric / Floatic implementations end--end). Pedagogical use cases: Field practitioners: see 3-system mapping soils know. Students: study one profile + walk key-trace. Researchers: sanity-check fixture set distinct 31 canonical fixtures (synthetic design; demo gallery uses real published profiles).

title: soilKey 0.9.33 (2026-05-03)
text: R/qualifiers-wrb2022-v0933.R ships seven new exported helpers, following existing qual_* calling convention: qual_endocalcic – depth-conditional Calcic (50-100 cm) qual_endogleyic – depth-conditional Gleyic (50-100 cm) qual_endostagnic – depth-conditional Stagnic (50-100 cm) qual_floatic – oc_pct >= 12 bulk_density <= 0.4 g/cm3 qual_toxic – ph_h2o <= 3.5 ec_dS_m >= 16 (proxy) qual_ombric – Histic + acidic (pH <= 4.5) + carbonates qual_rheic – Histic + neutral (pH > 4.5) carbonates present Endo-* helpers share new internal helper .q_endo_presence() checks diagnostic appears within [min_top, max_top] cm band – mirroring .q_presence() upper-50-cm case. Floatic / Toxic / Ombric / Rheic helpers use per-horizon proxies (KSSL-schema-compatible) rather depending fields schema yet model (specific gravity, full heavy-metal panels, hydrology). proxies conservative: function explicitly reports relevant missing attributes underlying signal absent.

what: Critical scientific finding – Embrapa FEBR Subordem ceiling
title: soilKey 0.9.22 (2026-05-01)
text: FEBR (open Brazilian soil-data archive used soilKey’s benchmark source) ships SiBCS labels 2nd-level (Subordem) maximum – 31 unique strings total across 50 485 horizon rows, e.g. “LATOSSOLO VERMELHO”, “ARGISSOLO BRUNO-ACINZENTADO”. 5th-level (Familia, Cap 18) therefore benchmarkable FEBR data alone. release pivots “Familia validation” “Subordem validation” deepest level FEBR actually supports. Future Familia validation requires different reference dataset (IBGE soil-survey volumes, Embrapa BDsolos curated, similar).

title: soilKey 0.9.22 (2026-05-01)
text: Subgroup ceiling reflects even Order gate correct (~ 1/3 profiles), getting full Subgroup modifier right (Typic / Aquic / Vertic / Oxyaquic / Pachic / Cumulic / Inceptic / Ultic / Mollic / etc.) requires full Path C machinery twelve USDA Orders, partial current implementation. Order 30-90 distinct subgroup permutations defined KST 13ed Chs 5-16 – wired. v1.0 / v1.1 work item: complete Path C subgroup trees per Order (currently subgroup machinery handles representative subset within Order, prioritising “Typic” plus -common qualifying subgroups; full combinatorial coverage deferred).

title: soilKey 0.9.22 (2026-05-01)
text: Subordem drop dominated Munsell-colour disagreement (Vermelho / Amarelo / Bruno) profiles FEBR records field-surveyor’s colour judgement lab gpkg lacks Munsell. 26 57 reference Argissolos correctly Order’d Argissolos classified different colour Subordem.

title: soilKey 0.9.22 (2026-05-01)

title: soilKey 0.9.21 (2026-05-01)
text: “surveyor’s diagnostic identification scientific tie-breaker” release. Wires NASIS pediagfeatures.featkind table (64 169 records field-surveyor-identified diagnostic horizons) USDA Order gates TIE-BREAKER : canonical lab + morphology gate returns passed = NA (insufficient data), surveyor’s identification flips TRUE. canonical gate returns TRUE / FALSE, tag recorded evidence override – preserving deterministic-key--data invariant.

title: soilKey 0.9.20 (2026-05-01)
text: “field morphology unlocks lab” release. Integrates NASIS Morphological export (NASIS_Morphological_09142021.sqlite, 562 MB, 431 415 phorizon rows) existing NCSS Lab Data Mart GeoPackage. lab gpkg chemistry + physics; NASIS sqlite Munsell colour, structure grade, clay films, slickensides, cracks, surveyor-identified diagnostic horizons. Joining peiid (Pedon Element ID) unlocks every diagnostic gate needed field morphology fire.

title: soilKey 0.9.20 (2026-05-01)
text: Reads lab gpkg via existing load_kssl_pedons_gpkg(), joins pedon’s lab horizons matching NASIS phorizon (peiid, hzdept, hzdepb), pulls canonical horizon schema: phcolor -> munsell_hue_moist / munsell_value_moist / munsell_chroma_moist / munsell_*_dry (528 421 rows) phstructure -> structure_grade / structure_size / structure_type (lowercase-normalised; 421 881 rows) phpvsf (clay films) -> clay_films_amount (mapped pvsfpct soilKey’s qualitative tiers; 109 793 clay-film rows) phpvsf (slickensides pedogenic / non-intersecting) -> slickensides (4 275 rows) phcracks -> cracks_width_cm / cracks_depth_cm (170 rows) pediagfeatures -> site$nasis_diagnostic_features (64 169 rows – surveyor-identified diagnostic horizons; informational per-site list, currently fed deterministic key) matching depth-overlap-based: lab layer, find NASIS phorizon largest (hzdept, hzdepb) overlap. NASIS also provides richer designations (hzname) – lab gpkg designation NA, NASIS one used.

title: soilKey 0.9.20 (2026-05-01)
text: runs filter quality criteria (clay + lab + B horizon). v0.9.19 lab-run: n=3 213 quality. v0.9.20 lab+NASIS run: n=3 218 quality (essentially identical sample). USDA top-1: 32.2 % (CI [30.7, 33.6], n=3 218).

title: soilKey 0.9.20 (2026-05-01)
text: lab gpkg lacks every field morphology variable KST 13ed Ch 3 lists “diagnostic features disambiguate Order membership chemistry alone ambiguous”: Mollic epipedon (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 15): requires Munsell value moist <= 3 + chroma <= 3. Lab gpkg zero Munsell. Argillic horizon (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 4): requires “evidence clay illuviation” (clay films, lamellae, oriented clay bridges). Lab gpkg clay percentages. Cambic horizon (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 13): requires structure designation evidence weathering. Lab gpkg chemistry. Vertic horizon (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 23): requires slickensides cracks LE >= 6 cm. Lab gpkg COLE. NASIS provides four: 99 % pedons least one Munsell record, 93 % structure data, 36 % clay films, 3 % slickensides directly recorded (another ~5 % via pediagfeatures.featkind = "Slickensides").

title: soilKey 0.9.20 (2026-05-01)
text: Suggests: adds DBI RSQLite (required calling load_kssl_pedons_with_nasis(); existing lab-loader load_kssl_pedons_gpkg() need ).

title: soilKey 0.9.19 (2026-05-01)
text: “lab-data-poor diagnostic recovery” release. Three KSSL Order gates 0 % v0.9.18 (Spodosols 0/276, Vertisols 0/63, Inceptisols 0/463) gained scientifically-grounded morphological inference paths, plus KSSL gpkg loader now extracts oxalate + pyrophosphate + COLE columns diagnostics need.

title: soilKey 0.9.19 (2026-05-01)
text: KSSL apples--apples 5 000-head / n=3 213-quality benchmark (identical sample size + filter v0.9.18 baseline): USDA top-1: 26.0 % (CI [24.6 %, 27.3 %], n=3 213). Mollisol / Alfisol / Entisol per-Order accuracies dropped points profiles previously misrouted larger buckets now correctly route Vertisols / Spodosols / Inceptisols. net +4.6 pp top-1 gain defensible headline number. Embrapa benchmark unchanged SiBCS 40.6 % / WRB 32.7 % / USDA 47.6 % – regression tropical-soil context, 31 canonical fixtures still classify correctly.

title: soilKey 0.9.19 (2026-05-01)
text: KST 13ed Ch 3 (spodic horizon, p 23) defines spodic horizon via several equivalent paths: (Al + 0.5*Fe)_ox >= 0.5 one; spodic morphology characteristic Bh / Bs designation + albic E + low pH + elevated B-horizon OC another (specific “field-described spodic” without lab Al / Fe). al_ox_pct fe_ox_pct missing across candidate layers, v0.9.19 falls back morphological path: designation matches ^Bh|^Bs|^Bhs|^Bsh, albic E horizon lies directly , pH(H2O) <= 5.9 Bh / Bs, OC Bh / Bs >= 0.5 % (illuvial accumulation evidence). fallback fires al_ox / fe_ox entirely absent pedon – lab-grade KSSL pedons still gate canonical chemical criteria.

title: soilKey 0.9.19 (2026-05-01)
text: KST 13ed Ch 16 (Vertisols, p 343) accepts linear extensibility (LE) summed upper 100 cm >= 6 cm alternative slickensides + cracks. v0.9.19 implements LE path: Triggers cole_value measured layer; uses canonical slickensides + cracks path cole_value absent.
code: LE = sum(cole_value[i] * thickness_cm[i])      for layers with top_cm < 100

title: soilKey 0.9.19 (2026-05-01)
text: KST 13ed Ch 3 (cambic horizon, p 13) accepts designation pattern (B[wgkjvzx]) morphological evidence soil formation lieu structure_grade data, since surveyor’s “B*” suffix already records alteration. structure_grade missing across candidate layers, v0.9.19 falls back designation path: designations matching ^B[wgkjvzx] qualify evidence weak horizon development.

what: KSSL gpkg loader – expanded column coverage

text: load_kssl_pedons_gpkg() now extracts oxalate + pyrophosphate + COLE columns diagnostics need: aluminum_ammonium_oxalate -> al_ox_pct (spodic, andic) fe_ammoniumoxalate_extractable -> fe_ox_pct silica_ammonium_oxalate -> si_ox_pct cole_whole_soil -> cole_value (vertic LE-based path) aluminum_saturation -> al_sat_pct (Ultisol BS-low inference)

title: soilKey 0.9.19 (2026-05-01)
text: Inceptisols still 5.4 % (7/129) – cambic-designation fallback unblocks , many Inceptisol references KSSL argillic-like clay increases route Alfisols. Distinguishing field-judged “non-pedogenic clay variation” (Inceptisol) “argillic horizon” (Alfisol) requires clay-film data NASIS sqlite lab-data gpkg. glass mineralogy KSSL Andisols may always report.

title: soilKey 0.9.18 (2026-05-01)
text: “missing-data resilience + KSSL unlocked” release. Three layered improvements v0.9.17: Mollic detection longer brittle missing Munsell. color test now falls back dry Munsell , OC-inferred “dark” Munsell columns absent. CEC/clay 8-36 + albic E ), FEBR loader now maps legacy “NITOSOL” / “GREYZEM” / “AGRISOL” spellings canonical WRB 2022 RSG names. KSSL gpkg loader lands. new load_kssl_pedons_gpkg() reads ncss_labdata.gpkg GeoPackage (joining lab_combine_nasis_ncss / lab_site / lab_layer / lab_chemical_properties / lab_physical_properties) yields list PedonRecords ready benchmarking. First benchmark 666 KSSL pedons reports USDA top-1 = 23.7 % (CI [20.8 %, 26.7 %]) — first US-context external validation number soilKey.

title: soilKey 0.9.18 (2026-05-01)
text: Per-Order changes matter Embrapa FEBR: KSSL per-Order 3 213-pedon production run: Spodosols Inceptisols next-priority KSSL failure modes – 0 % despite n >= 50 . Inceptisol canonical “residual cambic” Order; Spodosol detection requires spodic horizon (Bs / Bh) implemented appears strict missing data. v0.9.19 candidates.

title: soilKey 0.9.18 (2026-05-01)
text: Path 1 (canonical): value_moist <= 3 chroma_moist <= 3 (dry path: value_dry <= 5, value_moist + 1 <= 5 dry missing). Lab-grade profiles use path verbatim. Path 2 (v0.9.18): dry Munsell available. Tests value_dry <= 5 plus chroma_dry (moist) <= 3 chroma evidence present. Path 3 (v0.9.18): Munsell . oc_pct >= 1.5 surface horizon, colour inferred dark (Embrapa Manual de Metodos 2017 + KST 13ed Ch 3 commentary – every Mollic / Phaeozemic / Chernozemic surface horizon reported tropical pedon descriptions OC >> 1.5 A1).

title: soilKey 0.9.18 (2026-05-01)
text: Path 1 (canonical): measured bs_pct >= 50. Path 2: computed sum--cations + CEC available ((Ca + Mg + K + Na) / CEC * 100). Path 3: inferred al_sat_pct < 20 ph_h2o >= 5.8.

title: soilKey 0.9.18 (2026-05-01)
text: Previously returned passed = FALSE structure_type reported match polyhedral / nutty / sub-angular blocky. Now returns passed = NA – supplementary structure test longer hard-vetoes diagnostic. gradual-clay-decrease test still veto power (requires measured clay data showing > 8 percentage-point drop, mineralogically incompatible nitic horizon).

title: soilKey 0.9.18 (2026-05-01)
text: fe_dcb_pct missing across clay-qualifying layers profile Bt designation CEC/clay sits [8, 36] cmol/kg-clay albic E horizon Bt, gate accepts fe_dcb test TRUE inference grounds. -albic-E gate keeps canonical Acrisol / Lixisol / Alisol fixtures (E horizon) proper paths.

text: Maps FEBR / pre-2014 RSG spellings WRB 2022 4th-edition names: NITOSOL -> Nitisols, GREYZEM -> Phaeozems, AGRISOL -> Acrisols, LUVISSOL -> Luvisols, etc. Also handles “VERMELHO- AMARELO” / “NATRAQUOLL” miscellany occasionally appears qualifier-USDA-borrowed value.

Designed scale: head = N parser validation; full run handles 36 090 classified pedons \u2248 5 minutes per N pedon batch.
